packs.name: System Reference Document 5.2.1
packs.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
packs.summary: Open baseline spell and creature content generated through the repo-managed content-pack pipeline.

entries.name: Aid
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A durable support spell that raises both current and maximum hit points for multiple allies.
entries.searchText: aid spell a durable support spell that raises both current and maximum hit points for multiple allies. level 2 abjuration cleric druid paladin ranger support hit points 2 abjuration cleric druid paladin ranger action 30 feet 8 hours choose up to three creatures to increase their current and maximum hit points by 5 choose up to three creatures within range. each target's hit point maximum and current hit points increase by 5 for the duration. using a higher-level spell slot. each target's hit points increase by 5 for each spell slot level above 2.

payload.effect: Choose up to three creatures to increase their current and maximum hit points by 5
payload.description: Choose up to three creatures within range. Each target's Hit Point maximum and current Hit Points increase by 5 for the duration.
payload.higherLevel: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. Each target's Hit Points increase by 5 for each spell slot level above 2.

entries.name: Animal Friendship
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A charm spell that can calm a Beast and make it less likely to turn hostile.
entries.searchText: animal friendship spell a charm spell that can calm a beast and make it less likely to turn hostile. level 1 enchantment bard druid ranger beast wisdom save 1 enchantment bard druid ranger action 30 feet 24 hours save wisdom target a beast that you can see within range. the target must succeed on a wisdom saving throw or have the charmed condition for the duration. if you or one of your allies deals damage to the target, the spell ends. using a higher-level spell slot. you can target one additional beast for each spell slot level above 1.

payload.description: Target a Beast that you can see within range. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the Charmed condition for the duration. If you or one of your allies deals damage to the target, the spell ends.
payload.higherLevel: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional Beast for each spell slot level above 1.

entries.name: Animal Shapes
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Transform willing allies into beasts so a group can adapt to travel, combat, or infiltration on the fly.
entries.searchText:
animal shapes spell transform willing allies into beasts so a group can adapt to travel, combat, or infiltration on the fly. level 8 transmutation druid utility shapeshift beast allies temporary hit points 8 transmutation druid action 30 feet 24 hours transform willing creatures into large or smaller beasts of cr 4 or lower and grant temporary hit points based on the chosen form choose any number of willing creatures that you can see within range. each target shape-shifts into a large or smaller beast of your choice that has a challenge rating of 4 or lower. you can choose a different form for each target. on later turns, you can take a magic action to transform the targets again.

a target’s game statistics are replaced by the chosen beast’s statistics, but the target retains its creature type; hit points; hit point dice; alignment; ability to communicate; and intelligence, wisdom, and charisma scores. the target’s actions are limited by the beast form’s anatomy, and it can’t cast spells. the target’s equipment melds into the new form, and the target can’t use any of that equipment while in that form.

the target gains a number of temporary hit points equal to the beast form’s hit points. the transformation lasts for the duration for each target, until the target has no temporary hit points, or until the target leaves the form as a bonus action.

payload.effect: Transform willing creatures into Large or smaller beasts of CR 4 or lower and grant temporary hit points based on the chosen form
payload.description:
Choose any number of willing creatures that you can see within range. Each target shape-shifts into a Large or smaller Beast of your choice that has a Challenge Rating of 4 or lower. You can choose a different form for each target. On later turns, you can take a Magic action to transform the targets again.

A target’s game statistics are replaced by the chosen Beast’s statistics, but the target retains its creature type; Hit Points; Hit Point Dice; alignment; ability to communicate; and Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores. The target’s actions are limited by the Beast form’s anatomy, and it can’t cast spells. The target’s equipment melds into the new form, and the target can’t use any of that equipment while in that form.

The target gains a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to the Beast form’s Hit Points. The transformation lasts for the duration for each target, until the target has no Temporary Hit Points, or until the target leaves the form as a Bonus Action.

entries.name: Animate Objects
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Animate a cluster of objects into a temporary allied construct swarm under your command.
entries.searchText:
animate objects spell animate a cluster of objects into a temporary allied construct swarm under your command. level 5 transmutation bard sorcerer wizard concentration summon control force 5 transmutation bard sorcerer wizard action 120 feet concentration, up to 1 minute true animate several unattended objects into allied constructs you can command with a bonus action objects animate at your command. choose a number of nonmagical objects within range that aren’t being worn or carried, aren’t fixed to a surface, and aren’t gargantuan. the maximum number of objects is equal to your spellcasting ability modifier; for this number, a medium or smaller target counts as one object, a large target counts as two, and a huge target counts as three.

each target animates, sprouts legs, and becomes a construct that uses the animated object stat block; this creature is under your control until the spell ends or until it is reduced to 0 hit points. each creature you make with this spell is an ally to you and your allies. in combat, it shares your initiative count and takes its turn immediately after yours.

until the spell ends, you can take a bonus action to mentally command any creature you made with this spell if the creature is within 500 feet of you (if you control multiple creatures, you can command any of them at the same time, issuing the same command to each one). if you issue no commands, the creature takes the dodge action and moves only to avoid harm. when the creature drops to 0 hit points, it reverts to its object form, and any remaining damage carries over to that form.

using a higher-level spell slot. the creature’s slam damage increases by 1d4 (medium or smaller), 1d6 (large), or 1d12 (huge) for each spell slot level above 5.

huge or smaller construct, unaligned

ac 15

hp 10 (medium or smaller), 20 (large), 40 (huge)

speed 30 ft.

immunities poison, psychic; charmed, exhaustion, frightened, paralyzed, poisoned

senses blindsight 30 ft., passive perception 6

languages understands the languages you know

cr none (xp 0; pb equals your proficiency bonus)

actions

slam. melee attack roll: bonus equals your spell attack modifier, reach 5 ft. hit: force damage equal to 1d4 + 3 (medium or smaller), 2d6 + 3 + your spellcasting ability modifier (large), or 2d12 + 3 + your spellcasting ability modifier (huge).

payload.effect: Animate several unattended objects into allied Constructs you can command with a Bonus Action
payload.description:
Objects animate at your command. Choose a number of nonmagical objects within range that aren’t being worn or carried, aren’t fixed to a surface, and aren’t Gargantuan. The maximum number of objects is equal to your spellcasting ability modifier; for this number, a Medium or smaller target counts as one object, a Large target counts as two, and a Huge target counts as three.

Each target animates, sprouts legs, and becomes a Construct that uses the Animated Object stat block; this creature is under your control until the spell ends or until it is reduced to 0 Hit Points. Each creature you make with this spell is an ally to you and your allies. In combat, it shares your Initiative count and takes its turn immediately after yours.

Until the spell ends, you can take a Bonus Action to mentally command any creature you made with this spell if the creature is within 500 feet of you (if you control multiple creatures, you can command any of them at the same time, issuing the same command to each one). If you issue no commands, the creature takes the Dodge action and moves only to avoid harm. When the creature drops to 0 Hit Points, it reverts to its object form, and any remaining damage carries over to that form.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The creature’s Slam damage increases by 1d4 (Medium or smaller), 1d6 (Large), or 1d12 (Huge) for each spell slot level above 5.

Huge or Smaller Construct, Unaligned

AC 15

HP 10 (Medium or smaller), 20 (Large), 40 (Huge)

Speed 30 ft.

Immunities Poison, Psychic; Charmed, Exhaustion, Frightened, Paralyzed, Poisoned

Senses Blindsight 30 ft., Passive Perception 6

Languages Understands the languages you know

CR None (XP 0; PB equals your Proficiency Bonus)

Actions

Slam. Melee Attack Roll: Bonus equals your spell attack modifier, reach 5 ft. Hit: Force damage equal to 1d4 + 3 (Medium or smaller), 2d6 + 3 + your spellcasting ability modifier (Large), or 2d12 + 3 + your spellcasting ability modifier (Huge).

entries.name: Antilife Shell
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A living barrier surrounds you and keeps most creatures from crossing into reach.
entries.searchText:
antilife shell spell a living barrier surrounds you and keeps most creatures from crossing into reach. level 5 abjuration druid concentration defense barrier emanation 5 abjuration druid action self concentration, up to 1 hour true a 10-foot emanation prevents most creatures from passing or reaching through it an aura extends from you in a 10-foot emanation for the duration. the aura prevents creatures other than constructs and undead from passing or reaching through it. an affected creature can cast spells or make attacks with ranged or reach weapons through the barrier.

if you move so that an affected creature is forced to pass through the barrier, the spell ends.

payload.effect: A 10-foot Emanation prevents most creatures from passing or reaching through it
payload.description:
An aura extends from you in a 10-foot Emanation for the duration. The aura prevents creatures other than Constructs and Undead from passing or reaching through it. An affected creature can cast spells or make attacks with Ranged or Reach weapons through the barrier.

If you move so that an affected creature is forced to pass through the barrier, the spell ends.

entries.name: Antimagic Field
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Wrap yourself in an antimagic aura that suppresses spells, magic items, teleportation, and planar travel.
entries.searchText:
antimagic field spell wrap yourself in an antimagic aura that suppresses spells, magic items, teleportation, and planar travel. level 8 abjuration cleric wizard concentration anti-magic ward emanation 8 abjuration cleric wizard action self concentration, up to 1 hour true create a 10-foot antimagic aura that suppresses spells, magic items, teleportation, and planar travel an aura of antimagic surrounds you in 10-foot emanation. no one can cast spells, take magic actions, or create other magical effects inside the aura, and those things can’t target or otherwise affect anything inside it. magical properties of magic items don’t work inside the aura or on anything inside it.

areas of effect created by spells or other magic can’t extend into the aura, and no one can teleport into or out of it or use planar travel there. portals close temporarily while in the aura.

ongoing spells, except those cast by an artifact or a deity, are suppressed in the area. while an effect is suppressed, it doesn’t function, but the time it spends suppressed counts against its duration.

payload.effect: Create a 10-foot antimagic aura that suppresses spells, magic items, teleportation, and planar travel
payload.description:
An aura of antimagic surrounds you in 10-foot Emanation. No one can cast spells, take Magic actions, or create other magical effects inside the aura, and those things can’t target or otherwise affect anything inside it. Magical properties of magic items don’t work inside the aura or on anything inside it.

Areas of effect created by spells or other magic can’t extend into the aura, and no one can teleport into or out of it or use planar travel there. Portals close temporarily while in the aura.

Ongoing spells, except those cast by an Artifact or a deity, are suppressed in the area. While an effect is suppressed, it doesn’t function, but the time it spends suppressed counts against its duration.

entries.name: Antipathy/Sympathy
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Bind a creature type to recoil from or gravitate toward a target for days at a time.
entries.searchText:
antipathy/sympathy spell bind a creature type to recoil from or gravitate toward a target for days at a time. level 8 enchantment bard druid wizard wisdom save control charm frighten ward 8 enchantment bard druid wizard 1 hour 60 feet 10 days save wisdom ward a huge or smaller target so a chosen creature type is drawn toward it or repelled from it within 120 feet as you cast the spell, choose whether it creates antipathy or sympathy, and target one creature or object that is huge or smaller. then specify a kind of creature, such as red dragons, goblins, or vampires. a creature of the chosen kind makes a wisdom saving throw when it comes within 120 feet of the target. your choice of antipathy or sympathy determines what happens to a creature when it fails that save:

antipathy. the creature has the frightened condition. the frightened creature must use its movement on its turns to get as far away as possible from the target, moving by the safest route.

sympathy. the creature has the charmed condition. the charmed creature must use its movement on its turns to get as close as possible to the target, moving by the safest route. if the creature is within 5 feet of the target, the creature can’t willingly move away. if the target damages the charmed creature, that creature can make a wisdom saving throw to end the effect, as described below.

ending the effect. if the frightened or charmed creature ends its turn more than 120 feet away from the target, the creature makes a wisdom saving throw. on a successful save, the creature is no longer affected by the target. a creature that successfully saves against this effect is immune to it for 1 minute, after which it can be affected again.

payload.effect: Ward a Huge or smaller target so a chosen creature type is drawn toward it or repelled from it within 120 feet
payload.description:
As you cast the spell, choose whether it creates antipathy or sympathy, and target one creature or object that is Huge or smaller. Then specify a kind of creature, such as red dragons, goblins, or vampires. A creature of the chosen kind makes a Wisdom saving throw when it comes within 120 feet of the target. Your choice of antipathy or sympathy determines what happens to a creature when it fails that save:

Antipathy. The creature has the Frightened condition. The Frightened creature must use its movement on its turns to get as far away as possible from the target, moving by the safest route.

Sympathy. The creature has the Charmed condition. The Charmed creature must use its movement on its turns to get as close as possible to the target, moving by the safest route. If the creature is within 5 feet of the target, the creature can’t willingly move away. If the target damages the Charmed creature, that creature can make a Wisdom saving throw to end the effect, as described below.

Ending the Effect. If the Frightened or Charmed creature ends its turn more than 120 feet away from the target, the creature makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a successful save, the creature is no longer affected by the target. A creature that successfully saves against this effect is immune to it for 1 minute, after which it can be affected again.

entries.name: Arcane Eye
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: An invisible scouting sensor that floats ahead and feeds you remote vision in every direction.
entries.searchText:
arcane eye spell an invisible scouting sensor that floats ahead and feeds you remote vision in every direction. level 4 divination wizard concentration utility scouting vision exploration 4 divination wizard action 30 feet concentration, up to 1 hour true create an invisible invulnerable eye that relays vision and can move 30 feet as a bonus action you create an invisible, invulnerable eye within range that hovers for the duration. you mentally receive visual information from the eye, which can see in every direction. it also has darkvision with a range of 30 feet.

as a bonus action, you can move the eye up to 30 feet in any direction. a solid barrier blocks the eye's movement, but the eye can pass through an opening as small as 1 inch in diameter.

payload.effect: Create an invisible invulnerable eye that relays vision and can move 30 feet as a Bonus Action
payload.description:
You create an Invisible, invulnerable eye within range that hovers for the duration. You mentally receive visual information from the eye, which can see in every direction. It also has Darkvision with a range of 30 feet.

As a Bonus Action, you can move the eye up to 30 feet in any direction. A solid barrier blocks the eye's movement, but the eye can pass through an opening as small as 1 inch in diameter.

entries.name: Astral Projection
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Project yourself and willing allies into the Astral Plane while your bodies remain behind in suspended animation.
entries.searchText:
astral projection spell project yourself and willing allies into the astral plane while your bodies remain behind in suspended animation. level 9 necromancy cleric warlock wizard astral planar travel projection party 9 necromancy cleric warlock wizard 1 hour 10 feet until dispelled project yourself and up to eight willing creatures into the astral plane while your bodies remain in suspended animation until the spell ends you and up to eight willing creatures within range project your astral bodies into the astral plane (the spell ends instantly if you are already on that plane). each target’s body is left behind in a state of suspended animation; it has the unconscious condition, doesn’t need food or air, and doesn’t age.

a target’s astral form resembles its body in almost every way, replicating its game statistics and possessions. the principal difference is the addition of a silvery cord that trails from between the shoulder blades of the astral form. the cord fades from view after 1 foot. if the cord is cut—which happens only when an effect states that it does so—the target’s body and astral form both die.

a target’s astral form can travel through the astral plane. the moment an astral form leaves that plane, the target’s body and possessions travel along the silver cord, causing the target to re-enter its body on the new plane.

any damage or other effects that apply to an astral form have no effect on the target’s body and vice versa. if a target’s body or astral form drops to 0 hit points, the spell ends for that target. the spell ends for all the targets if you take a magic action to dismiss it.

when the spell ends for a target who isn’t dead, the target reappears in its body and exits the state of suspended animation.

payload.effect: Project yourself and up to eight willing creatures into the Astral Plane while your bodies remain in suspended animation until the spell ends
payload.description:
You and up to eight willing creatures within range project your astral bodies into the Astral Plane (the spell ends instantly if you are already on that plane). Each target’s body is left behind in a state of suspended animation; it has the Unconscious condition, doesn’t need food or air, and doesn’t age.

A target’s astral form resembles its body in almost every way, replicating its game statistics and possessions. The principal difference is the addition of a silvery cord that trails from between the shoulder blades of the astral form. The cord fades from view after 1 foot. If the cord is cut—which happens only when an effect states that it does so—the target’s body and astral form both die.

A target’s astral form can travel through the Astral Plane. The moment an astral form leaves that plane, the target’s body and possessions travel along the silver cord, causing the target to re-enter its body on the new plane.

Any damage or other effects that apply to an astral form have no effect on the target’s body and vice versa. If a target’s body or astral form drops to 0 Hit Points, the spell ends for that target. The spell ends for all the targets if you take a Magic action to dismiss it.

When the spell ends for a target who isn’t dead, the target reappears in its body and exits the state of suspended animation.

entries.name: Aura of Life
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A protective aura that resists necrotic harm and helps nearby allies stay standing.
entries.searchText: aura of life spell a protective aura that resists necrotic harm and helps nearby allies stay standing. level 4 abjuration cleric paladin concentration support healing aura necrotic defense 4 abjuration cleric paladin action self concentration, up to 10 minutes true allies in the aura gain resistance to necrotic damage, and a creature at 0 hit points can regain 1 hit point at the start of its turn there an aura radiates from you in a 30-foot emanation for the duration. while in the aura, you and your allies have resistance to necrotic damage, and your hit point maximums can’t be reduced. if an ally with 0 hit points starts its turn in the aura, that ally regains 1 hit point.

payload.effect: Allies in the aura gain resistance to Necrotic damage, and a creature at 0 Hit Points can regain 1 Hit Point at the start of its turn there
payload.description: An aura radiates from you in a 30-foot Emanation for the duration. While in the aura, you and your allies have Resistance to Necrotic damage, and your Hit Point maximums can’t be reduced. If an ally with 0 Hit Points starts its turn in the aura, that ally regains 1 Hit Point.

entries.name: Awaken
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Grant a beast or plant true intellect, speech, and temporary supernatural goodwill.
entries.searchText:
awaken spell grant a beast or plant true intellect, speech, and temporary supernatural goodwill. level 5 transmutation bard druid utility beast plant companion 5 transmutation bard druid 8 hours touch instantaneous raise a beast or plant to intelligence 10, grant speech, and charm it for 30 days unless harmed you spend the casting time tracing magical pathways within a precious gemstone, and then touch the target. the target must be either a beast or plant creature with an intelligence of 3 or less or a natural plant that isn’t a creature. the target gains an intelligence of 10 and the ability to speak one language you know. if the target is a natural plant, it becomes a plant creature and gains the ability to move its limbs, roots, vines, creepers, and so forth, and it gains senses similar to a human’s. the dm chooses statistics appropriate for the awakened plant, such as the statistics for the awakened shrub or awakened tree in “creature stat blocks”.

the awakened target has the charmed condition for 30 days or until you or your allies deal damage to it. when that condition ends, the awakened creature chooses its attitude toward you.

payload.effect: Raise a Beast or Plant to Intelligence 10, grant speech, and Charm it for 30 days unless harmed
payload.description:
You spend the casting time tracing magical pathways within a precious gemstone, and then touch the target. The target must be either a Beast or Plant creature with an Intelligence of 3 or less or a natural plant that isn’t a creature. The target gains an Intelligence of 10 and the ability to speak one language you know. If the target is a natural plant, it becomes a Plant creature and gains the ability to move its limbs, roots, vines, creepers, and so forth, and it gains senses similar to a human’s. The DM chooses statistics appropriate for the awakened Plant, such as the statistics for the Awakened Shrub or Awakened Tree in “Creature Stat Blocks”.

The awakened target has the Charmed condition for 30 days or until you or your allies deal damage to it. When that condition ends, the awakened creature chooses its attitude toward you.

entries.name: Banishment
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A battlefield exile spell that removes a creature and can permanently send extraplanar foes away.
entries.searchText:
banishment spell a battlefield exile spell that removes a creature and can permanently send extraplanar foes away. level 4 abjuration cleric paladin sorcerer warlock wizard control charisma save battlefield removal 4 abjuration cleric paladin sorcerer warlock wizard action 30 feet concentration, up to 1 minute true save charisma on a failed charisma save, the target disappears to a harmless demiplane for the duration and some extraplanar creatures may not return one creature that you can see within range must succeed on a charisma saving throw or be transported to a harmless demiplane for the duration. while there, the target has the incapacitated condition. when the spell ends, the target reappears in the space it left or in the nearest unoccupied space if that space is occupied.

if the target is an aberration, a celestial, an elemental, a fey, or a fiend, the target doesn’t return if the spell lasts for 1 minute. the target is instead transported to a random location on a plane (dm’s choice) associated with its creature type. using a higher-level spell slot. you can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 4.

payload.effect: On a failed Charisma save, the target disappears to a harmless demiplane for the duration and some extraplanar creatures may not return
payload.description:
One creature that you can see within range must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be transported to a harmless demiplane for the duration. While there, the target has the Incapacitated condition. When the spell ends, the target reappears in the space it left or in the nearest unoccupied space if that space is occupied.

If the target is an Aberration, a Celestial, an Elemental, a Fey, or a Fiend, the target doesn’t return if the spell lasts for 1 minute. The target is instead transported to a random location on a plane (DM’s choice) associated with its creature type.
payload.higherLevel: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 4.

entries.name: Befuddlement
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Shatter a creature's mind with psychic force and potentially lock out its spellcasting for weeks.
entries.searchText:
befuddlement spell shatter a creature's mind with psychic force and potentially lock out its spellcasting for weeks. level 8 enchantment bard druid warlock wizard intelligence save psychic control 8 enchantment bard druid warlock wizard action 150 feet instantaneous save intelligence blast a creature for 10d12 psychic damage and potentially block spellcasting and magic actions until it recovers 10d12 psychic you blast the mind of a creature that you can see within range. the target makes an intelligence saving throw.

on a failed save, the target takes 10d12 psychic damage and can’t cast spells or take the magic action. at the end of every 30 days, the target repeats the save, ending the effect on a success. the effect can also be ended by the greater restoration, heal, or wish spell.

on a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.

payload.effect: Blast a creature for 10d12 Psychic damage and potentially block spellcasting and Magic actions until it recovers
payload.description:
You blast the mind of a creature that you can see within range. The target makes an Intelligence saving throw.

On a failed save, the target takes 10d12 Psychic damage and can’t cast spells or take the Magic action. At the end of every 30 days, the target repeats the save, ending the effect on a success. The effect can also be ended by the Greater Restoration, Heal, or Wish spell.

On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.

entries.name: Bigby's Hand
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A giant magical hand batters, shoves, grapples, or shields at your command.
entries.searchText:
bigby's hand spell a giant magical hand batters, shoves, grapples, or shields at your command. level 5 evocation sorcerer warlock wizard control damage force grapple defense 5 evocation sorcerer warlock wizard action 120 feet 1 minute create a large magical hand that can strike, push, grapple, or interpose as a bonus action you create a large hand of shimmering magical energy in an unoccupied space that you can see within range. the hand lasts for the duration, and it moves at your command, mimicking the movements of your own hand.

the hand is an object that has ac 20 and hit points equal to your hit point maximum. if it drops to 0 hit points, the spell ends. the hand doesn't occupy its space.

when you cast the spell and as a bonus action on your later turns, you can move the hand up to 60 feet and then cause one of the following effects:

clenched fist. the hand strikes a target within 5 feet of it. make a melee spell attack. on a hit, the target takes 5d8 force damage.

forceful hand. the hand attempts to push a huge or smaller creature within 5 feet of it. the target must succeed on a strength saving throw, or the hand pushes the target up to 5 feet plus a number of feet equal to five times your spellcasting ability modifier. the hand moves with the target, remaining within 5 feet of it.

grasping hand. the hand attempts to grapple a huge or smaller creature within 5 feet of it. the target must succeed on a dexterity saving throw, or the target has the grappled condition, with an escape dc equal to your spell save dc. while the hand grapples the target, you can take a bonus action to cause the hand to crush it, dealing bludgeoning damage to the target equal to 4d6 plus your spellcasting ability modifier.

interposing hand. the hand grants you half cover against attacks and other effects that originate from its space or that pass through it. in addition, its space counts as difficult terrain for your enemies. using a higher-level spell slot. the damage of the clenched fist increases by 2d8 and the damage of the grasping hand increases by 2d6 for each spell slot level above 5.

payload.effect: Create a Large magical hand that can strike, push, grapple, or interpose as a Bonus Action
payload.description:
You create a Large hand of shimmering magical energy in an unoccupied space that you can see within range. The hand lasts for the duration, and it moves at your command, mimicking the movements of your own hand.

The hand is an object that has AC 20 and Hit Points equal to your Hit Point maximum. If it drops to 0 Hit Points, the spell ends. The hand doesn't occupy its space.

When you cast the spell and as a Bonus Action on your later turns, you can move the hand up to 60 feet and then cause one of the following effects:

Clenched Fist. The hand strikes a target within 5 feet of it. Make a melee spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 5d8 Force damage.

Forceful Hand. The hand attempts to push a Huge or smaller creature within 5 feet of it. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw, or the hand pushes the target up to 5 feet plus a number of feet equal to five times your spellcasting ability modifier. The hand moves with the target, remaining within 5 feet of it.

Grasping Hand. The hand attempts to grapple a Huge or smaller creature within 5 feet of it. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw, or the target has the Grappled condition, with an escape DC equal to your spell save DC. While the hand grapples the target, you can take a Bonus Action to cause the hand to crush it, dealing Bludgeoning damage to the target equal to 4d6 plus your spellcasting ability modifier.

Interposing Hand. The hand grants you Half Cover against attacks and other effects that originate from its space or that pass through it. In addition, its space counts as Difficult Terrain for your enemies.
payload.higherLevel: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage of the Clenched Fist increases by 2d8 and the damage of the Grasping Hand increases by 2d6 for each spell slot level above 5.

entries.name: Black Bear
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A sturdy beast that mauls nearby targets with tooth and claw.
entries.searchText: black bear creature a sturdy beast that mauls nearby targets with tooth and claw. beast cr 1/2 land melee druid form medium beast 1/2 11 19 (3d8 + 6) 40 ft., climb 30 ft. 15 10 14 2 12 7 perception +3 passive perception 13 keen smell multiattack: make one bite attack and one claw attack. bite: a solid melee hit that deals piercing damage. claws: a heavier swipe that deals slashing damage. forest hill true

actions: Multiattack: make one bite attack and one claw attack.
Bite: a solid melee hit that deals piercing damage.
Claws: a heavier swipe that deals slashing damage.

entries.name: Blade Barrier
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Raise a wall of magical blades that shreds and hinders creatures trying to cross it.
entries.searchText:
blade barrier spell raise a wall of magical blades that shreds and hinders creatures trying to cross it. level 6 evocation cleric concentration control barrier wall dexterity save force 6 evocation cleric action 90 feet concentration, up to 10 minutes true save dexterity create a long wall or ring of magical blades that grants cover, becomes difficult terrain, and deals 6d10 force damage to creatures in or moving through it 6d10 force you create a wall of whirling blades made of magical energy. the wall appears within range and lasts for the duration. you make a straight wall up to 100 feet long, 20 feet high, and 5 feet thick, or a ringed wall up to 60 feet in diameter, 20 feet high, and 5 feet thick. the wall provides three-quarters cover, and its space is difficult terrain.

any creature in the wall’s space makes a dexterity saving throw, taking 6d10 force damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. a creature also makes that save if it enters the wall’s space or ends it turn there. a creature makes that save only once per turn.

payload.effect: Create a long wall or ring of magical blades that grants cover, becomes difficult terrain, and deals 6d10 Force damage to creatures in or moving through it
payload.description:
You create a wall of whirling blades made of magical energy. The wall appears within range and lasts for the duration. You make a straight wall up to 100 feet long, 20 feet high, and 5 feet thick, or a ringed wall up to 60 feet in diameter, 20 feet high, and 5 feet thick. The wall provides Three-Quarters Cover, and its space is Difficult Terrain.

Any creature in the wall’s space makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 6d10 Force damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature also makes that save if it enters the wall’s space or ends it turn there. A creature makes that save only once per turn.

entries.name: Bless
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A concentration spell that increases allies' attack rolls and saving throws.
entries.searchText: bless spell a concentration spell that increases allies' attack rolls and saving throws. level 1 buff cleric paladin concentration support 1 enchantment cleric paladin action 30 feet concentration, up to 1 minute true you bless up to three creatures within range. whenever a target makes an attack roll or a saving throw before the spell ends, the target adds 1d4 to the attack roll or save. using a higher-level spell slot. you can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

payload.description: You bless up to three creatures within range. Whenever a target makes an attack roll or a saving throw before the spell ends, the target adds 1d4 to the attack roll or save.
payload.higherLevel: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

entries.name: Blight
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A necrotic withering blast that devastates living targets and instantly kills ordinary plants.
entries.searchText:
blight spell a necrotic withering blast that devastates living targets and instantly kills ordinary plants. level 4 necromancy druid sorcerer warlock wizard damage constitution save necrotic plants 4 necromancy druid sorcerer warlock wizard action 30 feet instantaneous save constitution a creature takes 8d8 necrotic damage on a failed constitution save, and nonmagical plants wither immediately 8d8 necrotic a creature that you can see within range makes a constitution saving throw, taking 8d8 necrotic damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. a plant creature automatically fails the save.

alternatively, target a nonmagical plant that isn’t a creature, such as a tree or shrub. it doesn’t make a save; it simply withers and dies. using a higher-level spell slot. the damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 4.

payload.effect: A creature takes 8d8 Necrotic damage on a failed Constitution save, and nonmagical plants wither immediately
payload.description:
A creature that you can see within range makes a Constitution saving throw, taking 8d8 Necrotic damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. A Plant creature automatically fails the save.

Alternatively, target a nonmagical plant that isn’t a creature, such as a tree or shrub. It doesn’t make a save; it simply withers and dies.
payload.higherLevel: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 4.

entries.name: Burning Hands
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A close-range cone of fire that punishes clustered enemies immediately.
entries.searchText:
burning hands spell a close-range cone of fire that punishes clustered enemies immediately. level 1 evocation sorcerer wizard fire dexterity save cone 1 evocation sorcerer wizard action self (15-foot cone) instantaneous save dexterity 3d6 fire loose flammable objects in the cone start burning a thin sheet of flames shoots forth from you. each creature in a 15-foot cone makes a dexterity saving throw, taking 3d6 fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

flammable objects in the cone that aren't being worn or carried start burning. using a higher-level spell slot. the damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 1.

payload.effect: Loose flammable objects in the cone start burning
payload.description:
A thin sheet of flames shoots forth from you. Each creature in a 15-foot Cone makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 3d6 Fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

Flammable objects in the Cone that aren't being worn or carried start burning.

entries.name: Call Lightning
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A persistent storm spell that repeatedly punishes clustered foes with lightning from above.
entries.searchText:
call lightning spell a persistent storm spell that repeatedly punishes clustered foes with lightning from above. level 3 conjuration druid lightning concentration dexterity save area 3 conjuration druid action 120 feet concentration, up to 10 minutes true save dexterity 3d10 lightning a storm cloud appears at a point within range that you can see above yourself. it takes the shape of a cylinder that is 10 feet tall with a 60-foot radius.

when you cast the spell, choose a point you can see under the cloud. a lightning bolt shoots from the cloud to that point. each creature within 5 feet of that point makes a dexterity saving throw, taking 3d10 lightning damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

until the spell ends, you can take a magic action to call down lightning in that way again, targeting the same point or a different one.

if you're outdoors in a storm when you cast this spell, the spell gives you control over that storm instead of creating a new one. under such conditions, the spell's damage increases by 1d10. using a higher-level spell slot. the damage increases by 1d10 for each spell slot level above 3.

payload.description:
A storm cloud appears at a point within range that you can see above yourself. It takes the shape of a Cylinder that is 10 feet tall with a 60-foot radius.

When you cast the spell, choose a point you can see under the cloud. A lightning bolt shoots from the cloud to that point. Each creature within 5 feet of that point makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 3d10 Lightning damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

Until the spell ends, you can take a Magic action to call down lightning in that way again, targeting the same point or a different one.

If you're outdoors in a storm when you cast this spell, the spell gives you control over that storm instead of creating a new one. Under such conditions, the spell's damage increases by 1d10.
payload.higherLevel: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d10 for each spell slot level above 3.

entries.name: Chain Lightning
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Hurl a lightning bolt that forks into multiple nearby targets for heavy crackling damage.
entries.searchText:
chain lightning spell hurl a lightning bolt that forks into multiple nearby targets for heavy crackling damage. level 6 evocation sorcerer wizard damage lightning dexterity save multi-target 6 evocation sorcerer wizard action 150 feet instantaneous save dexterity strike one target and arc up to three more bolts to nearby creatures or objects for 10d8 lightning damage 10d8 lightning you launch a lightning bolt toward a target you can see within range. three bolts then leap from that target to as many as three other targets of your choice, each of which must be within 30 feet of the first target. a target can be a creature or an object and can be targeted by only one of the bolts.

each target makes a dexterity saving throw, taking 10d8 lightning damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. using a higher-level spell slot. one additional bolt leaps from the first target to another target for each spell slot level above 6.

payload.effect: Strike one target and arc up to three more bolts to nearby creatures or objects for 10d8 Lightning damage
payload.description:
You launch a lightning bolt toward a target you can see within range. Three bolts then leap from that target to as many as three other targets of your choice, each of which must be within 30 feet of the first target. A target can be a creature or an object and can be targeted by only one of the bolts.

Each target makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 10d8 Lightning damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.
payload.higherLevel: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. One additional bolt leaps from the first target to another target for each spell slot level above 6.

entries.name: Charm Monster
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A broad charm spell that can be used to befriend almost any creature on a failed save.
entries.searchText: charm monster spell a broad charm spell that can be used to befriend almost any creature on a failed save. level 4 enchantment bard druid sorcerer warlock wizard control wisdom save social charm 4 enchantment bard druid sorcerer warlock wizard action 30 feet 1 hour save wisdom on a failed wisdom save, the target is charmed until the spell ends or it is harmed by you or your allies one creature you can see within range makes a wisdom saving throw. it does so with advantage if you or your allies are fighting it. on a failed save, the target has the charmed condition until the spell ends or until you or your allies damage it. the charmed creature is friendly to you. when the spell ends, the target knows it was charmed by you. using a higher-level spell slot. you can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 4.

payload.effect: On a failed Wisdom save, the target is Charmed until the spell ends or it is harmed by you or your allies
payload.description: One creature you can see within range makes a Wisdom saving throw. It does so with Advantage if you or your allies are fighting it. On a failed save, the target has the Charmed condition until the spell ends or until you or your allies damage it. The Charmed creature is Friendly to you. When the spell ends, the target knows it was Charmed by you.
payload.higherLevel: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 4.

payload.description: One Humanoid you can see within range makes a Wisdom saving throw. It does so with Advantage if you or your allies are fighting it. payload.higherLevel: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

entries.name: Chill Touch
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A grave-cold melee spell attack that deals necrotic damage and briefly stops healing.
entries.searchText:
chill touch spell a grave-cold melee spell attack that deals necrotic damage and briefly stops healing. cantrip necromancy sorcerer warlock wizard spell attack necrotic healing denial 0 necromancy sorcerer warlock wizard action touch instantaneous spellattack 1d10 necrotic on a hit, the target can't regain hit points until the end of your next turn channeling the chill of the grave, make a melee spell attack against a target within reach. on a hit, the target takes 1d10 necrotic damage, and it can't regain hit points until the end of your next turn.

cantrip upgrade. the damage increases by 1d10 when you reach levels 5 (2d10), 11 (3d10), and 17 (4d10).

payload.effect: On a hit, the target can't regain Hit Points until the end of your next turn
payload.description:
Channeling the chill of the grave, make a melee spell attack against a target within reach. On a hit, the target takes 1d10 Necrotic damage, and it can't regain Hit Points until the end of your next turn.

Cantrip Upgrade. The damage increases by 1d10 when you reach levels 5 (2d10), 11 (3d10), and 17 (4d10).

entries.searchText: circle of death spell unleash a massive wave of negative energy that scours a wide area with necrotic force. level 6 necromancy sorcerer warlock wizard damage necrotic constitution save area 6 necromancy sorcerer warlock wizard action 150 feet instantaneous save constitution burst a 60-foot-radius sphere with negative energy for 8d8 necrotic damage 8d8 necrotic negative energy ripples out in a 60-foot-radius sphere from a point you choose within range. each creature in that area makes a constitution saving throw, taking 8d8 necrotic damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. using a higher-level spell slot. the damage increases by 2d8 for each spell slot level above 6.

payload.effect: Burst a 60-foot-radius Sphere with negative energy for 8d8 Necrotic damage
payload.description: Negative energy ripples out in a 60-foot-radius Sphere from a point you choose within range. Each creature in that area makes a Constitution saving throw, taking 8d8 Necrotic damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.
payload.higherLevel: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 2d8 for each spell slot level above 6.

entries.name: Clone
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Grow a dormant duplicate body that can receive a creature's soul after death.
entries.searchText:
clone spell grow a dormant duplicate body that can receive a creature's soul after death. level 8 necromancy wizard utility duplicate resurrection vessel 8 necromancy wizard 1 hour touch instantaneous create an inert duplicate that matures over 120 days and can house the original creature's returning soul you touch a creature or at least 1 cubic inch of its flesh. an inert duplicate of that creature forms inside the vessel used in the spell’s casting and finishes growing after 120 days; you choose whether the finished clone is the same age as the creature or younger. the clone remains inert and endures indefinitely while its vessel remains undisturbed.

if the original creature dies after the clone finishes forming, the creature’s soul transfers to the clone if the soul is free and willing to return. the clone is physically identical to the original and has the same personality, memories, and abilities, but none of the original’s equipment. the creature’s original remains, if any, become inert and can’t be revived, since the creature’s soul is elsewhere.

payload.effect: Create an inert duplicate that matures over 120 days and can house the original creature's returning soul
payload.description:
You touch a creature or at least 1 cubic inch of its flesh. An inert duplicate of that creature forms inside the vessel used in the spell’s casting and finishes growing after 120 days; you choose whether the finished clone is the same age as the creature or younger. The clone remains inert and endures indefinitely while its vessel remains undisturbed.

If the original creature dies after the clone finishes forming, the creature’s soul transfers to the clone if the soul is free and willing to return. The clone is physically identical to the original and has the same personality, memories, and abilities, but none of the original’s equipment. The creature’s original remains, if any, become inert and can’t be revived, since the creature’s soul is elsewhere.

entries.name: Cloudkill
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A drifting poison fog chokes an area and punishes anything trapped inside it.
entries.searchText:
cloudkill spell a drifting poison fog chokes an area and punishes anything trapped inside it. level 5 conjuration sorcerer wizard concentration damage poison constitution save area 5 conjuration sorcerer wizard action 120 feet concentration, up to 10 minutes true save constitution a drifting 20-foot-radius sphere deals 5d8 poison damage on a constitution save and heavily obscures the area 5d8 poison you create a 20-foot-radius sphere of yellow-green fog centered on a point within range. the fog lasts for the duration or until strong wind (such as the one created by gust of wind) disperses it, ending the spell. its area is heavily obscured.

each creature in the sphere makes a constitution saving throw, taking 5d8 poison damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. a creature must also make this save when the sphere moves into its space and when it enters the sphere or ends its turn there. a creature makes this save only once per turn.

the sphere moves 10 feet away from you at the start of each of your turns. using a higher-level spell slot. the damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 5.

payload.effect: A drifting 20-foot-radius Sphere deals 5d8 Poison damage on a Constitution save and heavily obscures the area
payload.description:
You create a 20-foot-radius Sphere of yellow-green fog centered on a point within range. The fog lasts for the duration or until strong wind (such as the one created by Gust of Wind) disperses it, ending the spell. Its area is Heavily Obscured.

Each creature in the Sphere makes a Constitution saving throw, taking 5d8 Poison damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature must also make this save when the Sphere moves into its space and when it enters the Sphere or ends its turn there. A creature makes this save only once per turn.

The Sphere moves 10 feet away from you at the start of each of your turns.
payload.higherLevel: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 5.

entries.name: Command
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A forceful word of authority that compels a creature to obey a brief single-word order.
entries.searchText:
command spell a forceful word of authority that compels a creature to obey a brief single-word order. level 1 enchantment bard cleric paladin control wisdom save 1 enchantment bard cleric paladin action 60 feet instantaneous save wisdom you speak a one-word command to a creature you can see within range. the target must succeed on a wisdom saving throw or follow the command on its next turn. choose the command from these options:

approach. the target moves toward you by the shortest and most direct route, ending its turn if it moves within 5 feet of you.

drop. the target drops whatever it is holding and then ends its turn.

flee. the target spends its turn moving away from you by the fastest available means.

grovel. the target has the prone condition and then ends its turn.

halt. on its turn, the target doesn't move and takes no action or bonus action. using a higher-level spell slot. you can affect one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

payload.description:
You speak a one-word command to a creature you can see within range. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or follow the command on its next turn. Choose the command from these options:

Approach. The target moves toward you by the shortest and most direct route, ending its turn if it moves within 5 feet of you.

Drop. The target drops whatever it is holding and then ends its turn.

Flee. The target spends its turn moving away from you by the fastest available means.

Grovel. The target has the Prone condition and then ends its turn.

Halt. On its turn, the target doesn't move and takes no action or Bonus Action.
payload.higherLevel: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can affect one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

entries.name: Commune
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Ask a deity or divine proxy a small set of direct questions and receive truthful answers.
entries.searchText:
commune spell ask a deity or divine proxy a small set of direct questions and receive truthful answers. level 5 divination cleric ritual utility knowledge divine 5 divination cleric 1 minute or ritual self 1 minute true ask a deity or divine proxy up to three yes-or-no questions before the spell ends you contact a deity or a divine proxy and ask up to three questions that can be answered with yes or no. you must ask your questions before the spell ends. you receive a correct answer for each question.

divine beings aren’t necessarily omniscient, so you might receive “unclear” as an answer if a question pertains to information that lies beyond the deity’s knowledge. in a case where a one-word answer could be misleading or contrary to the deity’s interests, the dm might offer a short phrase as an answer instead.

if you cast the spell more than once before finishing a long rest, there is a cumulative 25 percent chance for each casting after the first that you get no answer.

payload.effect: Ask a deity or divine proxy up to three yes-or-no questions before the spell ends
payload.description:
You contact a deity or a divine proxy and ask up to three questions that can be answered with yes or no. You must ask your questions before the spell ends. You receive a correct answer for each question.

Divine beings aren’t necessarily omniscient, so you might receive “unclear” as an answer if a question pertains to information that lies beyond the deity’s knowledge. In a case where a one-word answer could be misleading or contrary to the deity’s interests, the DM might offer a short phrase as an answer instead.

If you cast the spell more than once before finishing a Long Rest, there is a cumulative 25 percent chance for each casting after the first that you get no answer.

entries.name: Commune with Nature
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Nature spirits reveal key facts about the surrounding region, from portals to powerful threats.
entries.searchText:
commune with nature spell nature spirits reveal key facts about the surrounding region, from portals to powerful threats. level 5 divination druid ranger ritual utility exploration nature 5 divination druid ranger 1 minute or ritual self instantaneous true learn three facts about the nearby natural area, such as settlements, portals, creatures, terrain resources, or water you commune with nature spirits and gain knowledge of the surrounding area. in the outdoors, the spell gives you knowledge of the area within 3 miles of you. in caves and other natural underground settings, the radius is limited to 300 feet. the spell doesn’t function where nature has been replaced by construction, such as in castles and settlements.

choose three of the following facts; you learn those facts as they pertain to the spell’s area:

locations of settlements

locations of portals to other planes of existence

location of one challenge rating 10+ creature (dm’s choice) that is a celestial, an elemental, a fey, a fiend, or an undead

the most prevalent kind of plant, mineral, or beast (you choose which to learn)

locations of bodies of water

for example, you could determine the location of a powerful monster in the area, the locations of bodies of water, and the locations of any towns.

payload.effect: Learn three facts about the nearby natural area, such as settlements, portals, creatures, terrain resources, or water
payload.description:
You commune with nature spirits and gain knowledge of the surrounding area. In the outdoors, the spell gives you knowledge of the area within 3 miles of you. In caves and other natural underground settings, the radius is limited to 300 feet. The spell doesn’t function where nature has been replaced by construction, such as in castles and settlements.

Choose three of the following facts; you learn those facts as they pertain to the spell’s area:

Locations of settlements

Locations of portals to other planes of existence

Location of one Challenge Rating 10+ creature (DM’s choice) that is a Celestial, an Elemental, a Fey, a Fiend, or an Undead

The most prevalent kind of plant, mineral, or Beast (you choose which to learn)

Locations of bodies of water

For example, you could determine the location of a powerful monster in the area, the locations of bodies of water, and the locations of any towns.

entries.name: Compulsion
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A charm-driven control spell that forces enemies to march in a chosen direction.
entries.searchText:
compulsion spell a charm-driven control spell that forces enemies to march in a chosen direction. level 4 enchantment bard concentration control wisdom save charm movement 4 enchantment bard action 30 feet concentration, up to 1 minute true save wisdom on a failed wisdom save, creatures are charmed and must spend their movement heading in a direction you choose each creature of your choice that you can see within range must succeed on a wisdom saving throw or have the charmed condition until the spell ends.

for the duration, you can take a bonus action to designate a direction that is horizontal to you. each charmed target must use as much of its movement as possible to move in that direction on its next turn, taking the safest route. after moving in this way, a target repeats the save, ending the spell on itself on a success.

payload.effect: On a failed Wisdom save, creatures are Charmed and must spend their movement heading in a direction you choose

Each creature of your choice that you can see within range must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the Charmed condition until the spell ends.

For the duration, you can take a Bonus Action to designate a direction that is horizontal to you. Each Charmed target must use as much of its movement as possible to move in that direction on its next turn, taking the safest route. After moving in this way, a target repeats the save, ending the spell on itself on a success.

entries.name: Conjure Celestial

entries.summary: Call down a moving pillar of celestial light that heals allies or sears foes.
entries.searchText:
conjure celestial spell call down a moving pillar of celestial light that heals allies or sears foes. level 7 conjuration cleric concentration healing radiant dexterity save cylinder 7 conjuration cleric action 90 feet concentration, up to 10 minutes true save dexterity create a moving pillar of upper planes light that heals chosen creatures or deals 6d12 radiant damage 6d12 radiant you conjure a spirit from the upper planes, which manifests as a pillar of light in a 10-foot-radius, 40-foot-high cylinder centered on a point within range. for each creature you can see in the cylinder, choose which of these lights shines on it:

until the spell ends, bright light fills the cylinder, and when you move on your turn, you can also move the cylinder up to 30 feet.

whenever the cylinder moves into the space of a creature you can see and whenever a creature you can see enters the cylinder or ends its turn there, you can bathe it in one of the lights. a creature can be affected by this spell only once per turn. using a higher-level spell slot. the healing and damage increase by 1d12 for each spell slot level above 7.

payload.effect: Create a moving pillar of Upper Planes light that heals chosen creatures or deals 6d12 Radiant damage
payload.description:
You conjure a spirit from the Upper Planes, which manifests as a pillar of light in a 10-foot-radius, 40-foot-high Cylinder centered on a point within range. For each creature you can see in the Cylinder, choose which of these lights shines on it:

Healing Light. The target regains Hit Points equal to 4d12 plus your spellcasting ability modifier.

Searing Light. The target makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 6d12 Radiant damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

Until the spell ends, Bright Light fills the Cylinder, and when you move on your turn, you can also move the Cylinder up to 30 feet.

Whenever the Cylinder moves into the space of a creature you can see and whenever a creature you can see enters the Cylinder or ends its turn there, you can bathe it in one of the lights. A creature can be affected by this spell only once per turn.

entries.name: Conjure Elemental
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Conjure an elemental spirit that restrains nearby foes in punishing elemental force.
entries.searchText:
conjure elemental spell conjure an elemental spirit that restrains nearby foes in punishing elemental force. level 5 conjuration druid wizard concentration summon control dexterity save elemental 5 conjuration druid wizard action 60 feet concentration, up to 10 minutes true save dexterity conjure a large elemental spirit that can restrain nearby creatures and deal 8d8 elemental damage on a dexterity save you conjure a large, intangible spirit from the elemental planes that appears in an unoccupied space within range. choose the spirit’s element, which determines its damage type: air (lightning), earth (thunder), fire (fire), or water (cold). the spirit lasts for the duration.

whenever a creature you can see enters the spirit’s space or starts its turn within 5 feet of the spirit, you can force that creature to make a dexterity saving throw if the spirit has no creature restrained. on failed save, the target takes 8d8 damage of the spirit’s type, and the target has the restrained condition until the spell ends. at the start of each of its turns, the restrained target repeats the save. on a failed save, the target takes 4d8 damage of the spirit’s type. on a successful save, the target isn’t restrained by the spirit. using a higher-level spell slot. the damage increases by 2d8 for each spell slot level above 5.

payload.effect: Conjure a Large elemental spirit that can restrain nearby creatures and deal 8d8 elemental damage on a Dexterity save
payload.description:
You conjure a Large, intangible spirit from the Elemental Planes that appears in an unoccupied space within range. Choose the spirit’s element, which determines its damage type: air (Lightning), earth (Thunder), fire (Fire), or water (Cold). The spirit lasts for the duration.

Whenever a creature you can see enters the spirit’s space or starts its turn within 5 feet of the spirit, you can force that creature to make a Dexterity saving throw if the spirit has no creature Restrained. On failed save, the target takes 8d8 damage of the spirit’s type, and the target has the Restrained condition until the spell ends. At the start of each of its turns, the Restrained target repeats the save. On a failed save, the target takes 4d8 damage of the spirit’s type. On a successful save, the target isn’t Restrained by the spirit.
payload.higherLevel: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 2d8 for each spell slot level above 5.

entries.name: Conjure Fey
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Call a fey spirit that lashes minds, spreads fear, and blinks around the battlefield.
entries.searchText:
conjure fey spell call a fey spirit that lashes minds, spreads fear, and blinks around the battlefield. level 6 conjuration druid concentration summon psychic fear spell attack 6 conjuration druid action 60 feet concentration, up to 10 minutes true spellattack summon a fey spirit that makes a melee spell attack for 3d12 psychic damage and can teleport before attacking again 3d12 psychic you conjure a medium spirit from the feywild in an unoccupied space you can see within range. the spirit lasts for the duration, and it looks like a fey creature of your choice. when the spirit appears, you can make one melee spell attack against a creature within 5 feet of it. on a hit, the target takes psychic damage equal to 3d12 plus your spellcasting ability modifier, and the target has the frightened condition until the start of your next turn, with both you and the spirit as the source of the fear.

as a bonus action on your later turns, you can teleport the spirit to an unoccupied space you can see within 30 feet of the space it left and make the attack against a creature within 5 feet of it. using a higher-level spell slot. the damage increases by 2d12 for each spell slot level above 6.

payload.effect: Summon a fey spirit that makes a melee spell attack for 3d12 Psychic damage and can teleport before attacking again
payload.description:
You conjure a Medium spirit from the Feywild in an unoccupied space you can see within range. The spirit lasts for the duration, and it looks like a Fey creature of your choice. When the spirit appears, you can make one melee spell attack against a creature within 5 feet of it. On a hit, the target takes Psychic damage equal to 3d12 plus your spellcasting ability modifier, and the target has the Frightened condition until the start of your next turn, with both you and the spirit as the source of the fear.

As a Bonus Action on your later turns, you can teleport the spirit to an unoccupied space you can see within 30 feet of the space it left and make the attack against a creature within 5 feet of it.
payload.higherLevel: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 2d12 for each spell slot level above 6.

conjure minor elementals spell elemental spirits whirl around you, empowering your attacks and hindering nearby foes. level 4 conjuration druid wizard concentration damage terrain acid cold fire lightning emanation 4 conjuration druid wizard action self concentration, up to 10 minutes true a 15-foot emanation grants your attacks extra elemental damage and creates difficult terrain for enemies 2d8 elemental you conjure spirits from the elemental planes that flit around you in a 15-foot emanation for the duration. until the spell ends, any attack you make deals an extra 2d8 damage when you hit a creature in the emanation. this damage is acid, cold, fire, or lightning (your choice when you make the attack).

The creature takes 5d8 Force damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature makes this save only once per turn.
