entries.name: Contact Other Plane
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Risk a mind-breaking communion with an extraplanar intelligence to ask difficult questions.
entries.searchText:
contact other plane spell risk a mind-breaking communion with an extraplanar intelligence to ask difficult questions. level 5 divination warlock wizard ritual utility knowledge psychic 5 divination warlock wizard 1 minute or ritual self 1 minute true contact an extraplanar intelligence to ask up to five questions, risking 6d6 psychic damage and incapacitated on a failed intelligence save you mentally contact a demigod, the spirit of a long-dead sage, or some other knowledgeable entity from another plane. contacting this otherworldly intelligence can break your mind. when you cast this spell, make a dc 15 intelligence saving throw. on a successful save, you can ask the entity up to five questions. you must ask your questions before the spell ends. the dm answers each question with one word, such as “yes,” “no,” “maybe,” “never,” “irrelevant,” or “unclear” (if the entity doesn’t know the answer to the question). if a one-word answer would be misleading, the dm might instead offer a short phrase as an answer.

on a failed save, you take 6d6 psychic damage and have the incapacitated condition until you finish a long rest. a greater restoration spell cast on you ends this effect.

payload.effect: Contact an extraplanar intelligence to ask up to five questions, risking 6d6 Psychic damage and Incapacitated on a failed Intelligence save
payload.description:
You mentally contact a demigod, the spirit of a long-dead sage, or some other knowledgeable entity from another plane. Contacting this otherworldly intelligence can break your mind. When you cast this spell, make a DC 15 Intelligence saving throw. On a successful save, you can ask the entity up to five questions. You must ask your questions before the spell ends. The DM answers each question with one word, such as “yes,” “no,” “maybe,” “never,” “irrelevant,” or “unclear” (if the entity doesn’t know the answer to the question). If a one-word answer would be misleading, the DM might instead offer a short phrase as an answer.

On a failed save, you take 6d6 Psychic damage and have the Incapacitated condition until you finish a Long Rest. A Greater Restoration spell cast on you ends this effect.

entries.name: Contagion
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A malignant touch inflicts necrotic sickness, poison, and lingering saving-throw weakness.
entries.searchText:
contagion spell a malignant touch inflicts necrotic sickness, poison, and lingering saving-throw weakness. level 5 necromancy cleric druid damage necrotic constitution save poisoned debuff 5 necromancy cleric druid action touch 7 days save constitution a touched creature takes 11d8 necrotic damage on a failed constitution save and can remain poisoned for 7 days 11d8 necrotic your touch inflicts a magical contagion. the target must succeed on a constitution saving throw or take 11d8 necrotic damage and have the poisoned condition. also, choose one ability when you cast the spell. while poisoned, the target has disadvantage on saving throws made with the chosen ability.

the target must repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns until it gets three successes or failures. if the target succeeds on three of these saves, the spell ends on the target. if the target fails three of the saves, the spell lasts for 7 days on it.

whenever the poisoned target receives an effect that would end the poisoned condition, the target must succeed on a constitution saving throw, or the poisoned condition doesn’t end on it.

payload.effect: A touched creature takes 11d8 Necrotic damage on a failed Constitution save and can remain Poisoned for 7 days
payload.description:
Your touch inflicts a magical contagion. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take 11d8 Necrotic damage and have the Poisoned condition. Also, choose one ability when you cast the spell. While Poisoned, the target has Disadvantage on saving throws made with the chosen ability.

The target must repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns until it gets three successes or failures. If the target succeeds on three of these saves, the spell ends on the target. If the target fails three of the saves, the spell lasts for 7 days on it.

Whenever the Poisoned target receives an effect that would end the Poisoned condition, the target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw, or the Poisoned condition doesn’t end on it.

entries.name: Contingency
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Bind a prepared self-targeting spell to a trigger so it springs into effect automatically.
entries.searchText:
contingency spell bind a prepared self-targeting spell to a trigger so it springs into effect automatically. level 6 abjuration wizard utility defense trigger 6 abjuration wizard 10 minutes self 10 days store a self-targeting level 5 or lower spell and define the trigger that releases it automatically choose a spell of level 5 or lower that you can cast, that has a casting time of an action, and that can target you. you cast that spell—called the contingent spell—as part of casting contingency, expending spell slots for both, but the contingent spell doesn’t come into effect. instead, it takes effect when a certain trigger occurs. you describe that trigger when you cast the two spells. for example, a contingency cast with water breathing might stipulate that water breathing comes into effect when you are engulfed in water or a similar liquid.

the contingent spell takes effect immediately after the trigger occurs for the first time, whether or not you want it to, and then contingency ends.

the contingent spell takes effect only on you, even if it can normally target others. you can use only one contingency spell at a time. if you cast this spell again, the effect of another contingency spell on you ends. also, contingency ends on you if its material component is ever not on your person.

payload.effect: Store a self-targeting level 5 or lower spell and define the trigger that releases it automatically
payload.description:
Choose a spell of level 5 or lower that you can cast, that has a casting time of an action, and that can target you. You cast that spell—called the contingent spell—as part of casting Contingency, expending spell slots for both, but the contingent spell doesn’t come into effect. Instead, it takes effect when a certain trigger occurs. You describe that trigger when you cast the two spells. For example, a Contingency cast with Water Breathing might stipulate that Water Breathing comes into effect when you are engulfed in water or a similar liquid.

The contingent spell takes effect immediately after the trigger occurs for the first time, whether or not you want it to, and then Contingency ends.

The contingent spell takes effect only on you, even if it can normally target others. You can use only one Contingency spell at a time. If you cast this spell again, the effect of another Contingency spell on you ends. Also, Contingency ends on you if its material component is ever not on your person.

entries.name: Control Water
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A large-scale terrain spell that floods, parts, redirects, or spins a huge volume of water.
entries.searchText:
control water spell a large-scale terrain spell that floods, parts, redirects, or spins a huge volume of water. level 4 transmutation cleric druid wizard concentration utility terrain water control 4 transmutation cleric druid wizard action 300 feet concentration, up to 10 minutes true control water in a 100-foot cube with flood, part water, redirect flow, or whirlpool effects until the spell ends, you control any water inside an area you choose that is a cube up to 100 feet on a side, using one of the following effects. as a magic action on your later turns, you can repeat the same effect or choose a different one.

flood. you cause the water level of all standing water in the area to rise by as much as 20 feet. if you choose an area in a large body of water, you instead create a 20-foot tall wave that travels from one side of the area to the other and then crashes. any huge or smaller vehicles in the wave’s path are carried with it to the other side. any huge or smaller vehicles struck by the wave have a 25 percent chance of capsizing.

the water level remains elevated until the spell ends or you choose a different effect. if this effect produced a wave, the wave repeats on the start of your next turn while the flood effect lasts.

part water. you part water in the area and create a trench. the trench extends across the spell’s area, and the separated water forms a wall to either side. the trench remains until the spell ends or you choose a different effect. the water then slowly fills in the trench over the course of the next round until the normal water level is restored.

redirect flow. you cause flowing water in the area to move in a direction you choose, even if the water has to flow over obstacles, up walls, or in other unlikely directions. the water in the area moves as you direct it, but once it moves beyond the spell’s area, it resumes its flow based on the terrain. the water continues to move in the direction you chose until the spell ends or you choose a different effect.

whirlpool. you cause a whirlpool to form in the center of the area, which must be at least 50 feet square and 25 feet deep. the whirlpool lasts until you choose a different effect or the spell ends. the whirlpool is 5 feet wide at the base, up to 50 feet wide at the top, and 25 feet tall. any creature in the water and within 25 feet of the whirlpool is pulled 10 feet toward it. when a creature enters the whirlpool for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there, it makes a strength saving throw. on a failed save, the creature takes 2d8 bludgeoning damage. on a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage. a creature can swim away from the whirlpool only if it first takes an action to pull away and succeeds on a strength (athletics) check against your spell save dc.

payload.effect: Control water in a 100-foot cube with flood, part water, redirect flow, or whirlpool effects
payload.description:
Until the spell ends, you control any water inside an area you choose that is a Cube up to 100 feet on a side, using one of the following effects. As a Magic action on your later turns, you can repeat the same effect or choose a different one.

Flood. You cause the water level of all standing water in the area to rise by as much as 20 feet. If you choose an area in a large body of water, you instead create a 20-foot tall wave that travels from one side of the area to the other and then crashes. Any Huge or smaller vehicles in the wave’s path are carried with it to the other side. Any Huge or smaller vehicles struck by the wave have a 25 percent chance of capsizing.

The water level remains elevated until the spell ends or you choose a different effect. If this effect produced a wave, the wave repeats on the start of your next turn while the flood effect lasts.

Part Water. You part water in the area and create a trench. The trench extends across the spell’s area, and the separated water forms a wall to either side. The trench remains until the spell ends or you choose a different effect. The water then slowly fills in the trench over the course of the next round until the normal water level is restored.

Redirect Flow. You cause flowing water in the area to move in a direction you choose, even if the water has to flow over obstacles, up walls, or in other unlikely directions. The water in the area moves as you direct it, but once it moves beyond the spell’s area, it resumes its flow based on the terrain. The water continues to move in the direction you chose until the spell ends or you choose a different effect.

Whirlpool. You cause a whirlpool to form in the center of the area, which must be at least 50 feet square and 25 feet deep. The whirlpool lasts until you choose a different effect or the spell ends. The whirlpool is 5 feet wide at the base, up to 50 feet wide at the top, and 25 feet tall. Any creature in the water and within 25 feet of the whirlpool is pulled 10 feet toward it. When a creature enters the whirlpool for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there, it makes a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes 2d8 Bludgeoning damage. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage. A creature can swim away from the whirlpool only if it first takes an action to pull away and succeeds on a Strength (Athletics) check against your spell save DC.

entries.name: Create Undead
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Raise controlled undead servants from humanoid corpses and renew your command over them nightly.
entries.searchText:
create undead spell raise controlled undead servants from humanoid corpses and renew your command over them nightly. level 6 necromancy cleric warlock wizard summon undead control 6 necromancy cleric warlock wizard 1 minute 10 feet instantaneous at night, animate up to three humanoid corpses as ghouls and maintain command over them for 24 hours you can cast this spell only at night. choose up to three corpses of medium or small humanoids within range. each one becomes a ghoul under your control (see “creature stat blocks” for its stat block).

as a bonus action on each of your turns, you can mentally command any creature you animated with this spell if the creature is within 120 feet of you (if you control multiple creatures, you can command any of them at the same time, issuing the same command to them). you decide what action the creature will take and where it will move on its next turn, or you can issue a general command, such as to guard a particular place. if you issue no commands, the creature takes the dodge action and moves only to avoid harm. once given an order, the creature continues to follow the order until its task is complete.

the creature is under your control for 24 hours, after which it stops obeying any command you’ve given it. to maintain control of the creature for another 24 hours, you must cast this spell on the creature before the current 24-hour period ends. this use of the spell reasserts your control over up to three creatures you have animated with this spell rather than animating new ones. using a higher-level spell slot. if you use a level 7 spell slot, you can animate or reassert control over four ghouls. if you use a level 8 spell slot, you can animate or reassert control over five ghouls or two ghasts or wights. if you use a level 9 spell slot, you can animate or reassert control over six ghouls, three ghasts or wights, or two mummies. see “creature stat blocks” for these stat blocks.

payload.effect: At night, animate up to three humanoid corpses as Ghouls and maintain command over them for 24 hours
payload.description:
You can cast this spell only at night. Choose up to three corpses of Medium or Small Humanoids within range. Each one becomes a Ghoul under your control (see “Creature Stat Blocks” for its stat block).

As a Bonus Action on each of your turns, you can mentally command any creature you animated with this spell if the creature is within 120 feet of you (if you control multiple creatures, you can command any of them at the same time, issuing the same command to them). You decide what action the creature will take and where it will move on its next turn, or you can issue a general command, such as to guard a particular place. If you issue no commands, the creature takes the Dodge action and moves only to avoid harm. Once given an order, the creature continues to follow the order until its task is complete.

The creature is under your control for 24 hours, after which it stops obeying any command you’ve given it. To maintain control of the creature for another 24 hours, you must cast this spell on the creature before the current 24-hour period ends. This use of the spell reasserts your control over up to three creatures you have animated with this spell rather than animating new ones.
payload.higherLevel: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. If you use a level 7 spell slot, you can animate or reassert control over four Ghouls. If you use a level 8 spell slot, you can animate or reassert control over five Ghouls or two Ghasts or Wights. If you use a level 9 spell slot, you can animate or reassert control over six Ghouls, three Ghasts or Wights, or two Mummies. See “Creature Stat Blocks” for these stat blocks.

entries.name: Cure Wounds
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A staple restorative spell that heals a creature through divine or primal magic.
entries.searchText: cure wounds spell a staple restorative spell that heals a creature through divine or primal magic. level 1 healing bard cleric druid paladin ranger support abjuration 1 abjuration bard cleric druid paladin ranger action touch instantaneous 1d8 + spellcasting modifier a creature you touch regains a number of hit points equal to 2d8 plus your spellcasting ability modifier. using a higher-level spell slot. the healing increases by 2d8 for each spell slot level above 1.

payload.description: A creature you touch regains a number of Hit Points equal to 2d8 plus your spellcasting ability modifier.
payload.higherLevel: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The healing increases by 2d8 for each spell slot level above 1.

entries.name: Daylight
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A long-duration light spell that floods an area with sunlight and suppresses weaker magical darkness.
entries.searchText:
daylight spell a long-duration light spell that floods an area with sunlight and suppresses weaker magical darkness. level 3 evocation cleric druid paladin ranger sorcerer light utility anti-darkness 3 evocation cleric druid paladin ranger sorcerer action 60 feet 1 hour bright sunlight fills a large area or emanates from an object and can dispel weaker magical darkness for the duration, sunlight spreads from a point within range and fills a 60-foot-radius sphere. the sunlight's area is bright light and sheds dim light for an additional 60 feet.

alternatively, you cast the spell on an object that isn't being worn or carried, causing the sunlight to fill a 60-foot emanation originating from that object. covering that object with something opaque, such as a bowl or helm, blocks the sunlight.

if any of this spell's area overlaps with an area of darkness created by a spell of level 3 or lower, that other spell is dispelled.

payload.effect: Bright sunlight fills a large area or emanates from an object and can dispel weaker magical darkness
payload.description:
For the duration, sunlight spreads from a point within range and fills a 60-foot-radius Sphere. The sunlight's area is Bright Light and sheds Dim Light for an additional 60 feet.

Alternatively, you cast the spell on an object that isn't being worn or carried, causing the sunlight to fill a 60-foot Emanation originating from that object. Covering that object with something opaque, such as a bowl or helm, blocks the sunlight.

If any of this spell's area overlaps with an area of Darkness created by a spell of level 3 or lower, that other spell is dispelled.

entries.name: Death Ward
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A long-duration safeguard that keeps a creature from falling to death the first time disaster strikes.
entries.searchText:
death ward spell a long-duration safeguard that keeps a creature from falling to death the first time disaster strikes. level 4 abjuration cleric paladin support defense death prevention 4 abjuration cleric paladin action touch 8 hours the next time the target would drop to 0 hit points, it drops to 1 instead, or an instant-death effect is negated you touch a creature and grant it a measure of protection from death. the first time the target would drop to 0 hit points before the spell ends, the target instead drops to 1 hit point, and the spell ends.

if the spell is still in effect when the target is subjected to an effect that would kill it instantly without dealing damage, that effect is negated against the target, and the spell ends.

payload.effect: The next time the target would drop to 0 Hit Points, it drops to 1 instead, or an instant-death effect is negated
payload.description:
You touch a creature and grant it a measure of protection from death. The first time the target would drop to 0 Hit Points before the spell ends, the target instead drops to 1 Hit Point, and the spell ends.

If the spell is still in effect when the target is subjected to an effect that would kill it instantly without dealing damage, that effect is negated against the target, and the spell ends.

entries.name: Delayed Blast Fireball
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Condense a growing bead of fire that erupts into a devastating delayed blast.
entries.searchText:
delayed blast fireball spell condense a growing bead of fire that erupts into a devastating delayed blast. level 7 evocation sorcerer wizard concentration damage fire dexterity save area 7 evocation sorcerer wizard action 150 feet concentration, up to 1 minute true save dexterity suspend a glowing bead that explodes in a 20-foot-radius sphere for at least 12d6 fire damage when released 12d6 fire a beam of yellow light flashes from you, then condenses at a chosen point within range as a glowing bead for the duration. when the spell ends, the bead explodes, and each creature in a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on that point makes a dexterity saving throw. a creature takes fire damage equal to the total accumulated damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

the spell’s base damage is 12d6, and the damage increases by 1d6 whenever your turn ends and the spell hasn’t ended.

if a creature touches the glowing bead before the spell ends, that creature makes a dexterity saving throw. on a failed save, the spell ends, causing the bead to explode. on a successful save, the creature can throw the bead up to 40 feet. if the thrown bead enters a creature’s space or collides with a solid object, the spell ends, and the bead explodes.

when the bead explodes, flammable objects in the explosion that aren’t being worn or carried start burning. using a higher-level spell slot. the base damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 7.

payload.effect: Suspend a glowing bead that explodes in a 20-foot-radius Sphere for at least 12d6 Fire damage when released
payload.description:
A beam of yellow light flashes from you, then condenses at a chosen point within range as a glowing bead for the duration. When the spell ends, the bead explodes, and each creature in a 20-foot-radius Sphere centered on that point makes a Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes Fire damage equal to the total accumulated damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

The spell’s base damage is 12d6, and the damage increases by 1d6 whenever your turn ends and the spell hasn’t ended.

If a creature touches the glowing bead before the spell ends, that creature makes a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the spell ends, causing the bead to explode. On a successful save, the creature can throw the bead up to 40 feet. If the thrown bead enters a creature’s space or collides with a solid object, the spell ends, and the bead explodes.

When the bead explodes, flammable objects in the explosion that aren’t being worn or carried start burning.
payload.higherLevel: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The base damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 7.

entries.name: Demiplane
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Open a shadowy doorway to a private extradimensional room you can revisit on later castings.
entries.searchText:
demiplane spell open a shadowy doorway to a private extradimensional room you can revisit on later castings. level 8 conjuration sorcerer warlock wizard utility extradimensional portal storage 8 conjuration sorcerer warlock wizard action 60 feet 1 hour create a shadowy door to an empty demiplane and reconnect to demiplanes from prior castings you create a shadowy medium door on a flat solid surface that you can see within range. this door can be opened and closed, and it leads to a demiplane that is an empty room 30 feet in each dimension, made of wood or stone (your choice).

when the spell ends, the door vanishes, and any objects inside the demiplane remain there. any creatures inside also remain unless they opt to be shunted through the door as it vanishes, landing with the prone condition in the unoccupied spaces closest to the door’s former space.

each time you cast this spell, you can create a new demiplane or connect the shadowy door to a demiplane you created with a previous casting of this spell. additionally, if you know the nature and contents of a demiplane created by a casting of this spell by another creature, you can connect the shadowy door to that demiplane instead.

payload.effect: Create a shadowy door to an empty demiplane and reconnect to demiplanes from prior castings
payload.description:
You create a shadowy Medium door on a flat solid surface that you can see within range. This door can be opened and closed, and it leads to a demiplane that is an empty room 30 feet in each dimension, made of wood or stone (your choice).

When the spell ends, the door vanishes, and any objects inside the demiplane remain there. Any creatures inside also remain unless they opt to be shunted through the door as it vanishes, landing with the Prone condition in the unoccupied spaces closest to the door’s former space.

Each time you cast this spell, you can create a new demiplane or connect the shadowy door to a demiplane you created with a previous casting of this spell. Additionally, if you know the nature and contents of a demiplane created by a casting of this spell by another creature, you can connect the shadowy door to that demiplane instead.

entries.name: Detect Magic
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A ritual-friendly utility spell for sensing the presence of magic nearby.
entries.searchText:
detect magic spell a ritual-friendly utility spell for sensing the presence of magic nearby. level 1 divination bard cleric druid paladin ranger sorcerer warlock wizard ritual utility 1 divination bard cleric druid paladin ranger sorcerer warlock wizard action self concentration, up to 10 minutes true true for the duration, you sense the presence of magical effects within 30 feet of yourself. if you sense such effects, you can take the magic action to see a faint aura around any visible creature or object in the area that bears the magic, and if an effect was created by a spell, you learn the spell's school of magic.

the spell is blocked by 1 foot of stone, dirt, or wood; 1 inch of metal; or a thin sheet of lead.

payload.description:
For the duration, you sense the presence of magical effects within 30 feet of yourself. If you sense such effects, you can take the Magic action to see a faint aura around any visible creature or object in the area that bears the magic, and if an effect was created by a spell, you learn the spell's school of magic.

The spell is blocked by 1 foot of stone, dirt, or wood; 1 inch of metal; or a thin sheet of lead.

entries.name: Dimension Door
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A long-range teleport that instantly carries you and an ally to a distant location.
entries.searchText:
dimension door spell a long-range teleport that instantly carries you and an ally to a distant location. level 4 conjuration bard sorcerer warlock wizard mobility teleport utility 4 conjuration bard sorcerer warlock wizard action 500 feet instantaneous teleport yourself up to 500 feet and optionally bring one willing creature standing within 5 feet of you you teleport to a location within range. you arrive at exactly the spot desired. it can be a place you can see, one you can visualize, or one you can describe by stating distance and direction, such as “200 feet straight downward” or “300 feet upward to the northwest at a 45-degree angle.”

you can also teleport one willing creature. the creature must be within 5 feet of you when you teleport, and it teleports to a space within 5 feet of your destination space.

if you, the other creature, or both would arrive in a space occupied by a creature or completely filled by one or more objects, you and any creature traveling with you each take 4d6 force damage, and the teleportation fails.

payload.effect: Teleport yourself up to 500 feet and optionally bring one willing creature standing within 5 feet of you
payload.description:
You teleport to a location within range. You arrive at exactly the spot desired. It can be a place you can see, one you can visualize, or one you can describe by stating distance and direction, such as “200 feet straight downward” or “300 feet upward to the northwest at a 45-degree angle.”

You can also teleport one willing creature. The creature must be within 5 feet of you when you teleport, and it teleports to a space within 5 feet of your destination space.

If you, the other creature, or both would arrive in a space occupied by a creature or completely filled by one or more objects, you and any creature traveling with you each take 4d6 Force damage, and the teleportation fails.

entries.name: Disguise Self
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: An infiltration spell that changes the caster's apparent appearance for an hour.
entries.searchText:
disguise self spell an infiltration spell that changes the caster's apparent appearance for an hour. level 1 illusion bard sorcerer wizard utility infiltration disguise 1 illusion bard sorcerer wizard action self 1 hour your appearance changes within the limits of your normal body plan until the spell ends you make yourself--including your clothing, armor, weapons, and other belongings on your person--look different until the spell ends. you can seem 1 foot shorter or taller and can appear heavier or lighter. you must adopt a form that has the same basic arrangement of limbs as you have. otherwise, the extent of the illusion is up to you.

the changes wrought by this spell fail to hold up to physical inspection. for example, if you use this spell to add a hat to your outfit, objects pass through the hat, and anyone who touches it would feel nothing.

to discern that you are disguised, a creature must take the study action to inspect your appearance and succeed on an intelligence (investigation) check against your spell save dc.

payload.effect: Your appearance changes within the limits of your normal body plan until the spell ends
payload.description:
You make yourself--including your clothing, armor, weapons, and other belongings on your person--look different until the spell ends. You can seem 1 foot shorter or taller and can appear heavier or lighter. You must adopt a form that has the same basic arrangement of limbs as you have. Otherwise, the extent of the illusion is up to you.

The changes wrought by this spell fail to hold up to physical inspection. For example, if you use this spell to add a hat to your outfit, objects pass through the hat, and anyone who touches it would feel nothing.

To discern that you are disguised, a creature must take the Study action to inspect your appearance and succeed on an Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC.

entries.name: Disintegrate
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Fire a devastating green ray that erases creatures, objects, and magical force.
entries.searchText:
disintegrate spell fire a devastating green ray that erases creatures, objects, and magical force. level 6 transmutation sorcerer wizard damage force dexterity save object 6 transmutation sorcerer wizard action 60 feet instantaneous save dexterity launch a ray that deals 10d6 + 40 force damage and can reduce creatures, objects, or force creations to dust 10d6 + 40 force you launch a green ray at a target you can see within range. the target can be a creature, a nonmagical object, or a creation of magical force, such as the wall created by wall of force.

a creature targeted by this spell makes a dexterity saving throw. on a failed save, the target takes 10d6 + 40 force damage. if this damage reduces it to 0 hit points, it and everything nonmagical it is wearing and carrying are disintegrated into gray dust. the target can be revived only by a true resurrection or a wish spell.

this spell automatically disintegrates a large or smaller nonmagical object or a creation of magical force. if such a target is huge or larger, this spell disintegrates a 10-foot-cube portion of it. using a higher-level spell slot. the damage increases by 3d6 for each spell slot level above 6.

payload.effect: Launch a ray that deals 10d6 + 40 Force damage and can reduce creatures, objects, or force creations to dust
payload.description:
You launch a green ray at a target you can see within range. The target can be a creature, a nonmagical object, or a creation of magical force, such as the wall created by Wall of Force.

A creature targeted by this spell makes a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes 10d6 + 40 Force damage. If this damage reduces it to 0 Hit Points, it and everything nonmagical it is wearing and carrying are disintegrated into gray dust. The target can be revived only by a True Resurrection or a Wish spell.

This spell automatically disintegrates a Large or smaller nonmagical object or a creation of magical force. If such a target is Huge or larger, this spell disintegrates a 10-foot-Cube portion of it.
payload.higherLevel: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 3d6 for each spell slot level above 6.

entries.name: Dispel Evil and Good
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Ward yourself against outsiders, break certain enchantments, or dismiss a nearby extraplanar foe.
entries.searchText:
dispel evil and good spell ward yourself against outsiders, break certain enchantments, or dismiss a nearby extraplanar foe. level 5 abjuration cleric paladin concentration protection banishment charisma save 5 abjuration cleric paladin action self concentration, up to 1 minute true save charisma protect yourself from several creature types, break certain possessions or fear/charm effects, or dismiss a nearby outsider for the duration, celestials, elementals, fey, fiends, and undead have disadvantage on attack rolls against you. you can end the spell early by using either of the following special functions.

break enchantment. as a magic action, you touch a creature that is possessed by or has the charmed or frightened condition from one or more creatures of the types above. the target is no longer possessed, charmed, or frightened by such creatures.

dismissal. as a magic action, you target one creature you can see within 5 feet of you that has one of the creature types above. the target must succeed on a charisma saving throw or be sent back to its home plane if it isn’t there already. if they aren’t on their home plane, undead are sent to the shadowfell, and fey are sent to the feywild.

payload.effect: Protect yourself from several creature types, break certain possessions or fear/charm effects, or dismiss a nearby outsider
payload.description:
For the duration, Celestials, Elementals, Fey, Fiends, and Undead have Disadvantage on attack rolls against you. You can end the spell early by using either of the following special functions.

Break Enchantment. As a Magic action, you touch a creature that is possessed by or has the Charmed or Frightened condition from one or more creatures of the types above. The target is no longer possessed, Charmed, or Frightened by such creatures.

Dismissal. As a Magic action, you target one creature you can see within 5 feet of you that has one of the creature types above. The target must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be sent back to its home plane if it isn’t there already. If they aren’t on their home plane, Undead are sent to the Shadowfell, and Fey are sent to the Feywild.

entries.name: Dispel Magic
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A universal answer spell that ends ongoing magic on a creature, object, or magical effect.
entries.searchText: dispel magic spell a universal answer spell that ends ongoing magic on a creature, object, or magical effect. level 3 abjuration bard cleric druid paladin ranger sorcerer warlock wizard anti-magic utility 3 abjuration bard cleric druid paladin ranger sorcerer warlock wizard action 120 feet instantaneous end one qualifying ongoing spell on a creature, object, or magical effect, with a check for stronger magic choose one creature, object, or magical effect within range. any ongoing spell of level 3 or lower on the target ends. for each ongoing spell of level 4 or higher on the target, make an ability check using your spellcasting ability (dc 10 plus that spell's level). on a successful check, the spell ends. using a higher-level spell slot. you automatically end a spell on the target if the spell's level is equal to or less than the level of the spell slot you use.

payload.effect: End one qualifying ongoing spell on a creature, object, or magical effect, with a check for stronger magic
payload.description: Choose one creature, object, or magical effect within range. Any ongoing spell of level 3 or lower on the target ends. For each ongoing spell of level 4 or higher on the target, make an ability check using your spellcasting ability (DC 10 plus that spell's level). On a successful check, the spell ends.
payload.higherLevel: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You automatically end a spell on the target if the spell's level is equal to or less than the level of the spell slot you use.

entries.name: Divination
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A ritual consultation that asks divine powers one question about events likely to occur soon.
entries.searchText:
divination spell a ritual consultation that asks divine powers one question about events likely to occur soon. level 4 divination cleric druid wizard ritual utility prophecy exploration 4 divination cleric druid wizard action or ritual self instantaneous true ask a deity or its servants one question about a goal, event, or activity within 7 days this spell puts you in contact with a god or a god’s servants. you ask one question about a specific goal, event, or activity to occur within 7 days. the dm offers a truthful reply, which might be a short phrase or cryptic rhyme. the spell doesn’t account for circumstances that might change the answer, such as the casting of other spells.

if you cast the spell more than once before finishing a long rest, there is a cumulative 25 percent chance for each casting after the first that you get no answer.

payload.effect: Ask a deity or its servants one question about a goal, event, or activity within 7 days
payload.description:
This spell puts you in contact with a god or a god’s servants. You ask one question about a specific goal, event, or activity to occur within 7 days. The DM offers a truthful reply, which might be a short phrase or cryptic rhyme. The spell doesn’t account for circumstances that might change the answer, such as the casting of other spells.

If you cast the spell more than once before finishing a Long Rest, there is a cumulative 25 percent chance for each casting after the first that you get no answer.

entries.name: Divine Word
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Speak a word of Upper Planes power that weakens or destroys creatures by their remaining vitality.
entries.searchText:
divine word spell speak a word of upper planes power that weakens or destroys creatures by their remaining vitality. level 7 evocation cleric charisma save control banishment word 7 evocation cleric bonus action 30 feet instantaneous save charisma utter a divine word that inflicts escalating hp-threshold effects and can banish extraplanar creatures you utter a word imbued with power from the upper planes. each creature of your choice in range makes a charisma saving throw. on a failed save, a target that has 50 hit points or fewer suffers an effect based on its current hit points, as shown in the divine word effects table. regardless of its hit points, a celestial, an elemental, a fey, or a fiend target that fails its save is forced back to its plane of origin (if it isn’t there already) and can’t return to the current plane for 24 hours by any means short of a wish spell.

hit points | effect
0–20 | the target dies.
21–30 | the target has the blinded, deafened, and stunned conditions for 1 hour.
31–40 | the target has the blinded and deafened conditions for 10 minutes.
41–50 | the target has the deafened condition for 1 minute.

payload.effect: Utter a divine word that inflicts escalating HP-threshold effects and can banish extraplanar creatures
payload.description:
You utter a word imbued with power from the Upper Planes. Each creature of your choice in range makes a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, a target that has 50 Hit Points or fewer suffers an effect based on its current Hit Points, as shown in the Divine Word Effects table. Regardless of its Hit Points, a Celestial, an Elemental, a Fey, or a Fiend target that fails its save is forced back to its plane of origin (if it isn’t there already) and can’t return to the current plane for 24 hours by any means short of a Wish spell.

Hit Points | Effect
0–20 | The target dies.
21–30 | The target has the Blinded, Deafened, and Stunned conditions for 1 hour.
31–40 | The target has the Blinded and Deafened conditions for 10 minutes.
41–50 | The target has the Deafened condition for 1 minute.

entries.name: Dominate Beast
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A domination spell that charms a Beast and lets you direct it telepathically.
entries.searchText:
dominate beast spell a domination spell that charms a beast and lets you direct it telepathically. level 4 enchantment druid ranger sorcerer concentration control wisdom save charm beast 4 enchantment druid ranger sorcerer action 60 feet concentration, up to 1 minute true save wisdom on a failed wisdom save, a beast is charmed and obeys telepathic commands one beast you can see within range must succeed on a wisdom saving throw or have the charmed condition for the duration. the target has advantage on the save if you or your allies are fighting it. whenever the target takes damage, it repeats the save, ending the spell on itself on a success.

you have a telepathic link with the charmed target while the two of you are on the same plane of existence. on your turn, you can use this link to issue commands to the target (no action required), such as “attack that creature,” “move over there,” or “fetch that object.” the target does its best to obey on its turn. if it completes an order and doesn’t receive further direction from you, it acts and moves as it likes, focusing on protecting itself.

you can command the target to take a reaction but must take your own reaction to do so. using a higher-level spell slot. your concentration can last longer with a spell slot of level 5 (up to 10 minutes), 6 (up to 1 hour), or 7+ (up to 8 hours).

payload.effect: On a failed Wisdom save, a Beast is Charmed and obeys telepathic commands
payload.description:
One Beast you can see within range must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the Charmed condition for the duration. The target has Advantage on the save if you or your allies are fighting it. Whenever the target takes damage, it repeats the save, ending the spell on itself on a success.

You have a telepathic link with the Charmed target while the two of you are on the same plane of existence. On your turn, you can use this link to issue commands to the target (no action required), such as “Attack that creature,” “Move over there,” or “Fetch that object.” The target does its best to obey on its turn. If it completes an order and doesn’t receive further direction from you, it acts and moves as it likes, focusing on protecting itself.

You can command the target to take a Reaction but must take your own Reaction to do so.
payload.higherLevel: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. Your Concentration can last longer with a spell slot of level 5 (up to 10 minutes), 6 (up to 1 hour), or 7+ (up to 8 hours).

entries.name: Dream
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Send a messenger into a target's dreams to converse or deliver a terrifying psychic warning.
entries.searchText:
dream spell send a messenger into a target's dreams to converse or deliver a terrifying psychic warning. level 5 illusion bard warlock wizard utility psychic communication wisdom save 5 illusion bard warlock wizard 1 minute special 8 hours save wisdom enter a known target's dreams to converse or deliver a terrifying ten-word message that can deny rest and deal 3d6 psychic damage 3d6 psychic you target a creature you know on the same plane of existence. you or a willing creature you touch enters a trance state to act as a dream messenger. while in the trance, the messenger is incapacitated and has a speed of 0.

if the target is asleep, the messenger appears in the target’s dreams and can converse with the target as long as it remains asleep, through the spell’s duration. the messenger can also shape the dream’s environment, creating landscapes, objects, and other images. the messenger can emerge from the trance at any time, ending the spell. the target recalls the dream perfectly upon waking.

if the target is awake when you cast the spell, the messenger knows it and can either end the trance (and the spell) or wait for the target to sleep, at which point the messenger enters its dreams.

you can make the messenger terrifying to the target. if you do so, the messenger can deliver a message of no more than ten words, and then the target makes a wisdom saving throw. on a failed save, the target gains no benefit from its rest, and it takes 3d6 psychic damage when it wakes up.

payload.effect: Enter a known target's dreams to converse or deliver a terrifying ten-word message that can deny rest and deal 3d6 Psychic damage
payload.description:
You target a creature you know on the same plane of existence. You or a willing creature you touch enters a trance state to act as a dream messenger. While in the trance, the messenger is Incapacitated and has a Speed of 0.

If the target is asleep, the messenger appears in the target’s dreams and can converse with the target as long as it remains asleep, through the spell’s duration. The messenger can also shape the dream’s environment, creating landscapes, objects, and other images. The messenger can emerge from the trance at any time, ending the spell. The target recalls the dream perfectly upon waking.

If the target is awake when you cast the spell, the messenger knows it and can either end the trance (and the spell) or wait for the target to sleep, at which point the messenger enters its dreams.

You can make the messenger terrifying to the target. If you do so, the messenger can deliver a message of no more than ten words, and then the target makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the target gains no benefit from its rest, and it takes 3d6 Psychic damage when it wakes up.

entries.name: Druidcraft
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A primal utility cantrip for small natural omens and minor sensory effects.
entries.searchText:
druidcraft spell a primal utility cantrip for small natural omens and minor sensory effects. cantrip transmutation druid primal utility 0 transmutation druid action 30 feet instantaneous whispering to the spirits of nature, you create one of the following effects within range.

weather sensor. you create a tiny, harmless sensory effect that predicts what the weather will be at your location for the next 24 hours. the effect might manifest as a golden orb for clear skies, a cloud for rain, falling snowflakes for snow, and so on. this effect persists for 1 round.

bloom. you instantly make a flower blossom, a seed pod open, or a leaf bud bloom.

sensory effect. you create a harmless sensory effect, such as falling leaves, spectral dancing fairies, a gentle breeze, the sound of an animal, or the faint odor of skunk. the effect must fit in a 5-foot cube.

fire play. you light or snuff out a candle, a torch, or a campfire.

payload.description:
Whispering to the spirits of nature, you create one of the following effects within range.

Weather Sensor. You create a Tiny, harmless sensory effect that predicts what the weather will be at your location for the next 24 hours. The effect might manifest as a golden orb for clear skies, a cloud for rain, falling snowflakes for snow, and so on. This effect persists for 1 round.

Bloom. You instantly make a flower blossom, a seed pod open, or a leaf bud bloom.

Sensory Effect. You create a harmless sensory effect, such as falling leaves, spectral dancing fairies, a gentle breeze, the sound of an animal, or the faint odor of skunk. The effect must fit in a 5-foot Cube.

Fire Play. You light or snuff out a candle, a torch, or a campfire.

entries.name: Earthquake
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Rend the ground into dangerous difficult terrain that topples creatures and tears open fissures.
entries.searchText:
earthquake spell rend the ground into dangerous difficult terrain that topples creatures and tears open fissures. level 8 transmutation cleric druid sorcerer concentration dexterity save terrain prone fissures 8 transmutation cleric druid sorcerer action 500 feet concentration, up to 1 minute true save dexterity shake a 100-foot-radius area into difficult terrain, knocking creatures prone and opening fissures choose a point on the ground that you can see within range. for the duration, an intense tremor rips through the ground in a 100-foot-radius circle centered on that point. the ground there is difficult terrain.

when you cast this spell and at the end of each of your turns for the duration, each creature on the ground in the area makes a dexterity saving throw. on a failed save, a creature has the prone condition, and its concentration is broken.

you can also cause the effects below.

fissures. a total of 1d6 fissures open in the spell’s area at the end of the turn you cast it. you choose the fissures’ locations, which can’t be under structures. each fissure is 1d10 × 10 feet deep and 10 feet wide, and it extends from one edge of the spell’s area to another edge. a creature in the same space as a fissure must succeed on a dexterity saving throw or fall in. a creature that successfully saves moves with the fissure’s edge as it opens.

structures. the tremor deals 50 bludgeoning damage to any structure in contact with the ground in the area when you cast the spell and at the end of each of your turns until the spell ends. if a structure drops to 0 hit points, it collapses.

a creature within a distance from a collapsing structure equal to half the structure’s height makes a dexterity saving throw. on a failed save, the creature takes 12d6 bludgeoning damage, has the prone condition, and is buried in the rubble, requiring a dc 20 strength (athletics) check as an action to escape. on a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage only.

payload.effect: Shake a 100-foot-radius area into difficult terrain, knocking creatures prone and opening fissures
payload.description:
Choose a point on the ground that you can see within range. For the duration, an intense tremor rips through the ground in a 100-foot-radius circle centered on that point. The ground there is Difficult Terrain.

When you cast this spell and at the end of each of your turns for the duration, each creature on the ground in the area makes a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature has the Prone condition, and its Concentration is broken.

You can also cause the effects below.

Fissures. A total of 1d6 fissures open in the spell’s area at the end of the turn you cast it. You choose the fissures’ locations, which can’t be under structures. Each fissure is 1d10 × 10 feet deep and 10 feet wide, and it extends from one edge of the spell’s area to another edge. A creature in the same space as a fissure must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or fall in. A creature that successfully saves moves with the fissure’s edge as it opens.

Structures. The tremor deals 50 Bludgeoning damage to any structure in contact with the ground in the area when you cast the spell and at the end of each of your turns until the spell ends. If a structure drops to 0 Hit Points, it collapses.

A creature within a distance from a collapsing structure equal to half the structure’s height makes a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes 12d6 Bludgeoning damage, has the Prone condition, and is buried in the rubble, requiring a DC 20 Strength (Athletics) check as an action to escape. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage only.

entries.name: Eldritch Blast
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A force cantrip that fires a ranged beam and scales into multiple blasts with level.
entries.searchText:
eldritch blast spell a force cantrip that fires a ranged beam and scales into multiple blasts with level. cantrip evocation warlock force spell attack 0 evocation warlock action 120 feet instantaneous spellattack 1d10 force you hurl a beam of crackling energy. make a ranged spell attack against one creature or object in range. on a hit, the target takes 1d10 force damage.

cantrip upgrade. the spell creates two beams at level 5, three beams at level 11, and four beams at level 17. you can direct the beams at the same target or at different ones. make a separate attack roll for each beam.

payload.description:
You hurl a beam of crackling energy. Make a ranged spell attack against one creature or object in range. On a hit, the target takes 1d10 Force damage.

Cantrip Upgrade. The spell creates two beams at level 5, three beams at level 11, and four beams at level 17. You can direct the beams at the same target or at different ones. Make a separate attack roll for each beam.

entries.name: Entangle
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A ground-control spell that restrains creatures in grasping plants until they break free.
entries.searchText:
entangle spell a ground-control spell that restrains creatures in grasping plants until they break free. level 1 conjuration druid ranger control restrained strength save 1 conjuration druid ranger action 90 feet concentration, up to 1 minute true save strength grasping plants sprout from the ground in a 20-foot square within range. for the duration, these plants turn the ground in the area into difficult terrain. they disappear when the spell ends.

each creature (other than you) in the area when you cast the spell must succeed on a strength saving throw or have the restrained condition until the spell ends. a restrained creature can take an action to make a strength (athletics) check against your spell save dc. on a success, it frees itself from the grasping plants and is no longer restrained by them.

payload.description:
Grasping plants sprout from the ground in a 20-foot square within range. For the duration, these plants turn the ground in the area into Difficult Terrain. They disappear when the spell ends.

Each creature (other than you) in the area when you cast the spell must succeed on a Strength saving throw or have the Restrained condition until the spell ends. A Restrained creature can take an action to make a Strength (Athletics) check against your spell save DC. On a success, it frees itself from the grasping plants and is no longer Restrained by them.

entries.name: Etherealness
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Step into the Border Ethereal to drift unseen through the world for hours.
entries.searchText:
etherealness spell step into the border ethereal to drift unseen through the world for hours. level 7 conjuration bard cleric sorcerer warlock wizard utility travel planar 7 conjuration bard cleric sorcerer warlock wizard action self up to 8 hours step into the border ethereal for up to 8 hours and optionally bring extra willing creatures with a higher slot you step into the border regions of the ethereal plane, where it overlaps with your current plane. you remain in the border ethereal for the duration. during this time, you can move in any direction. if you move up or down, every foot of movement costs an extra foot. you can perceive the plane you left, which looks gray, and you can’t see anything there more than 60 feet away.

while on the ethereal plane, you can affect and be affected only by creatures, objects, and effects on that plane. creatures that aren’t on the ethereal plane can’t perceive or interact with you unless a feature gives them the ability to do so.

when the spell ends, you return to the plane you left in the spot that corresponds to your space in the border ethereal. if you appear in an occupied space, you are shunted to the nearest unoccupied space and take force damage equal to twice the number of feet you are moved.

this spell ends instantly if you cast it while you are on the ethereal plane or a plane that doesn’t border it, such as one of the outer planes. using a higher-level spell slot. you can target up to three willing creatures (including yourself) for each spell slot level above 7. the creatures must be within 10 feet of you when you cast the spell.

payload.effect: Step into the Border Ethereal for up to 8 hours and optionally bring extra willing creatures with a higher slot
payload.description:
You step into the border regions of the Ethereal Plane, where it overlaps with your current plane. You remain in the Border Ethereal for the duration. During this time, you can move in any direction. If you move up or down, every foot of movement costs an extra foot. You can perceive the plane you left, which looks gray, and you can’t see anything there more than 60 feet away.

While on the Ethereal Plane, you can affect and be affected only by creatures, objects, and effects on that plane. Creatures that aren’t on the Ethereal Plane can’t perceive or interact with you unless a feature gives them the ability to do so.

When the spell ends, you return to the plane you left in the spot that corresponds to your space in the Border Ethereal. If you appear in an occupied space, you are shunted to the nearest unoccupied space and take Force damage equal to twice the number of feet you are moved.

This spell ends instantly if you cast it while you are on the Ethereal Plane or a plane that doesn’t border it, such as one of the Outer Planes.
payload.higherLevel: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target up to three willing creatures (including yourself) for each spell slot level above 7. The creatures must be within 10 feet of you when you cast the spell.

entries.name: Evard's Black Tentacles
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Writhing tentacles seize an area, restraining creatures and crushing them with force.
entries.searchText:
evard's black tentacles spell writhing tentacles seize an area, restraining creatures and crushing them with force. level 4 conjuration sorcerer wizard concentration control strength save restrained bludgeoning area 4 conjuration sorcerer wizard action 90 feet (20 ft. square) concentration true save strength tentacles fill a 20-foot square with difficult terrain and restrain creatures that fail a strength save 3d6 bludgeoning squirming, ebony tentacles fill a 20-foot square on ground that you can see within range. for the duration, these tentacles turn the ground in that area into difficult terrain.

each creature in that area makes a strength saving throw. on a failed save, it takes 3d6 bludgeoning damage, and it has the restrained condition until the spell ends. a creature also makes that save if it enters the area or ends it turn there. a creature makes that save only once per turn.

a restrained creature can take an action to make a strength (athletics) check against your spell save dc, ending the condition on itself on a success.

payload.effect: Tentacles fill a 20-foot square with Difficult Terrain and restrain creatures that fail a Strength save
payload.description:
Squirming, ebony tentacles fill a 20-foot square on ground that you can see within range. For the duration, these tentacles turn the ground in that area into Difficult Terrain.

Each creature in that area makes a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 3d6 Bludgeoning damage, and it has the Restrained condition until the spell ends. A creature also makes that save if it enters the area or ends it turn there. A creature makes that save only once per turn.

A Restrained creature can take an action to make a Strength (Athletics) check against your spell save DC, ending the condition on itself on a success.

entries.name: Eyebite
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Turn your gaze into a repeating curse that can panic, sicken, or drop targets unconscious.
entries.searchText:
eyebite spell turn your gaze into a repeating curse that can panic, sicken, or drop targets unconscious. level 6 necromancy bard sorcerer warlock wizard concentration control wisdom save fear 6 necromancy bard sorcerer warlock wizard action self concentration, up to 1 minute true save wisdom force a creature within 60 feet to fall asleep, panic, or sicken, and retarget the gaze on later turns for the duration, your eyes become an inky void. one creature of your choice within 60 feet of you that you can see must succeed on a wisdom saving throw or be affected by one of the following effects of your choice for the duration.

on each of your turns until the spell ends, you can take a magic action to target another creature but can’t target a creature again if it has succeeded on a save against this casting of the spell.

asleep. the target has the unconscious condition. it wakes up if it takes any damage or if another creature takes an action to shake it awake.

panicked. the target has the frightened condition. on each of its turns, the frightened target must take the dash action and move away from you by the safest and shortest route available. if the target moves to a space at least 60 feet away from you where it can’t see you, this effect ends.

sickened. the target has the poisoned condition.

payload.effect: Force a creature within 60 feet to fall asleep, panic, or sicken, and retarget the gaze on later turns
payload.description:
For the duration, your eyes become an inky void. One creature of your choice within 60 feet of you that you can see must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be affected by one of the following effects of your choice for the duration.

On each of your turns until the spell ends, you can take a Magic action to target another creature but can’t target a creature again if it has succeeded on a save against this casting of the spell.

Asleep. The target has the Unconscious condition. It wakes up if it takes any damage or if another creature takes an action to shake it awake.

Panicked. The target has the Frightened condition. On each of its turns, the Frightened target must take the Dash action and move away from you by the safest and shortest route available. If the target moves to a space at least 60 feet away from you where it can’t see you, this effect ends.

Sickened. The target has the Poisoned condition.

entries.name: Fabricate
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A long-form transmutation that converts raw materials into finished nonmagical products.
entries.searchText:
fabricate spell a long-form transmutation that converts raw materials into finished nonmagical products. level 4 transmutation wizard utility crafting exploration materials 4 transmutation wizard 10 minutes 120 feet instantaneous convert visible raw materials into finished objects of the same material, within size and skill limits you convert raw materials into products of the same material. for example, you can fabricate a wooden bridge from a clump of trees, a rope from a patch of hemp, or clothes from flax or wool.

choose raw materials that you can see within range. you can fabricate a large or smaller object (contained within a 10-foot cube or eight connected 5-foot cubes) given a sufficient quantity of material. if you're working with metal, stone, or another mineral substance, however, the fabricated object can be no larger than medium (contained within a 5-foot cube). the quality of any fabricated objects is based on the quality of the raw materials.

creatures and magic items can't be created by this spell. you also can't use it to create items that require a high degree of skill?such as weapons and armor?unless you have proficiency with the type of artisan's tools used to craft such objects.

payload.effect: Convert visible raw materials into finished objects of the same material, within size and skill limits
payload.description:
You convert raw materials into products of the same material. For example, you can fabricate a wooden bridge from a clump of trees, a rope from a patch of hemp, or clothes from flax or wool.

Choose raw materials that you can see within range. You can fabricate a Large or smaller object (contained within a 10-foot Cube or eight connected 5-foot Cubes) given a sufficient quantity of material. If you're working with metal, stone, or another mineral substance, however, the fabricated object can be no larger than Medium (contained within a 5-foot Cube). The quality of any fabricated objects is based on the quality of the raw materials.

Creatures and magic items can't be created by this spell. You also can't use it to create items that require a high degree of skill?such as weapons and armor?unless you have proficiency with the type of Artisan's Tools used to craft such objects.

entries.name: Faerie Fire
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A revealing glow that outlines creatures and makes it easier for allies to land attacks.
entries.searchText:
faerie fire spell a revealing glow that outlines creatures and makes it easier for allies to land attacks. level 1 evocation bard druid support advantage dexterity save 1 evocation bard druid action 60 feet concentration, up to 1 minute true save dexterity outlined targets cannot benefit from invisibility, and attacks against them gain advantage objects in a 20-foot cube within range are outlined in blue, green, or violet light (your choice). each creature in the cube is also outlined if it fails a dexterity saving throw. for the duration, objects and affected creatures shed dim light in a 10-foot radius and can't benefit from the invisible condition.

attack rolls against an affected creature or object have advantage if the attacker can see it.

payload.effect: Outlined targets cannot benefit from invisibility, and attacks against them gain advantage
payload.description:
Objects in a 20-foot Cube within range are outlined in blue, green, or violet light (your choice). Each creature in the Cube is also outlined if it fails a Dexterity saving throw. For the duration, objects and affected creatures shed Dim Light in a 10-foot radius and can't benefit from the Invisible condition.

Attack rolls against an affected creature or object have Advantage if the attacker can see it.

entries.name: Find the Path
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Sense the most direct physical route to a known destination for hours or even a full day.
entries.searchText:
find the path spell sense the most direct physical route to a known destination for hours or even a full day. level 6 divination bard cleric druid concentration utility travel navigation 6 divination bard cleric druid 1 minute self concentration, up to 1 day true sense the distance, direction, and most direct physical route to a familiar destination on the same plane you magically sense the most direct physical route to a location you name. you must be familiar with the location, and the spell fails if you name a destination on another plane of existence, a moving destination (such as a mobile fortress), or an unspecific destination (such as “a green dragon’s lair?”).

for the duration, as long as you are on the same plane of existence as the destination, you know how far it is and in what direction it lies. whenever you face a choice of paths along the way there, you know which path is the most direct.

payload.effect: Sense the distance, direction, and most direct physical route to a familiar destination on the same plane
payload.description:
You magically sense the most direct physical route to a location you name. You must be familiar with the location, and the spell fails if you name a destination on another plane of existence, a moving destination (such as a mobile fortress), or an unspecific destination (such as “a green dragon’s lair?”).

For the duration, as long as you are on the same plane of existence as the destination, you know how far it is and in what direction it lies. Whenever you face a choice of paths along the way there, you know which path is the most direct.

entries.name: Finger of Death
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Unleash killing necrotic energy that can raise its humanoid victim as a zombie.
entries.searchText:
finger of death spell unleash killing necrotic energy that can raise its humanoid victim as a zombie. level 7 necromancy sorcerer warlock wizard damage necrotic constitution save undead 7 necromancy sorcerer warlock wizard action 60 feet instantaneous save constitution blast one creature with negative energy for 7d8 + 30 necrotic damage and raise a slain humanoid as a zombie 7d8 + 30 necrotic you unleash negative energy toward a creature you can see within range. the target makes a constitution saving throw, taking 7d8 + 30 necrotic damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

a humanoid killed by this spell rises at the start of your next turn as a zombie (see “creature stat blocks”) that follows your verbal orders.

payload.effect: Blast one creature with negative energy for 7d8 + 30 Necrotic damage and raise a slain Humanoid as a Zombie
payload.description:
You unleash negative energy toward a creature you can see within range. The target makes a Constitution saving throw, taking 7d8 + 30 Necrotic damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

A Humanoid killed by this spell rises at the start of your next turn as a Zombie (see “Creature Stat Blocks”) that follows your verbal orders.

entries.name: Fire Bolt
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A ranged attack cantrip that deals fire damage and scales with character level.
entries.searchText:
fire bolt spell a ranged attack cantrip that deals fire damage and scales with character level. cantrip evocation attack wizard sorcerer arcane fire 0 evocation sorcerer wizard action 120 feet instantaneous spellattack 1d10 fire you hurl a mote of fire at a creature or an object within range. make a ranged spell attack against the target. on a hit, the target takes 1d10 fire damage. a flammable object hit by this spell starts burning if it isn't being worn or carried.

cantrip upgrade. the damage increases by 1d10 when you reach levels 5 (2d10), 11 (3d10), and 17 (4d10).

payload.description:
You hurl a mote of fire at a creature or an object within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes 1d10 Fire damage. A flammable object hit by this spell starts burning if it isn't being worn or carried.

Cantrip Upgrade. The damage increases by 1d10 when you reach levels 5 (2d10), 11 (3d10), and 17 (4d10).

entries.name: Fire Shield
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A protective aura of flame or frost punishes melee attackers and grants elemental resistance.
entries.searchText:
fire shield spell a protective aura of flame or frost punishes melee attackers and grants elemental resistance. level 4 evocation druid sorcerer wizard defense retaliation fire cold resistance 4 evocation druid sorcerer wizard action self 10 minutes choose a warm or chill shield to gain damage resistance and deal 2d8 fire or cold damage to nearby melee attackers 2d8 fire/cold wispy flames wreathe your body for the duration, shedding bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet.

the flames provide you with a warm shield or a chill shield, as you choose. the warm shield grants you resistance to cold damage, and the chill shield grants you resistance to fire damage.

in addition, whenever a creature within 5 feet of you hits you with a melee attack roll, the shield erupts with flame. the attacker takes 2d8 fire damage from a warm shield or 2d8 cold damage from a chill shield.

payload.effect: Choose a warm or chill shield to gain damage resistance and deal 2d8 Fire or Cold damage to nearby melee attackers
payload.description:
Wispy flames wreathe your body for the duration, shedding Bright Light in a 10-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 10 feet.

The flames provide you with a warm shield or a chill shield, as you choose. The warm shield grants you Resistance to Cold damage, and the chill shield grants you Resistance to Fire damage.

In addition, whenever a creature within 5 feet of you hits you with a melee attack roll, the shield erupts with flame. The attacker takes 2d8 Fire damage from a warm shield or 2d8 Cold damage from a chill shield.

entries.name: Fire Storm
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Shape a contiguous storm of fire cubes that engulfs a large battlefield.
entries.searchText:
fire storm spell shape a contiguous storm of fire cubes that engulfs a large battlefield. level 7 evocation cleric druid sorcerer damage fire dexterity save area 7 evocation cleric druid sorcerer action 150 feet instantaneous save dexterity arrange up to ten contiguous 10-foot cubes of fire that deal 7d10 fire damage 7d10 fire a storm of fire appears within range. the area of the storm consists of up to ten 10-foot cubes, which you arrange as you like. each cube must be contiguous with at least one other cube. each creature in the area makes a dexterity saving throw, taking 7d10 fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

flammable objects in the area that aren’t being worn or carried start burning.

payload.effect: Arrange up to ten contiguous 10-foot Cubes of fire that deal 7d10 Fire damage
payload.description:
A storm of fire appears within range. The area of the storm consists of up to ten 10-foot Cubes, which you arrange as you like. Each Cube must be contiguous with at least one other Cube. Each creature in the area makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 7d10 Fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

Flammable objects in the area that aren’t being worn or carried start burning.

entries.name: Fireball

entries.summary: An iconic explosive blast that hits a wide area for heavy fire damage at long range.
entries.searchText:
fireball spell an iconic explosive blast that hits a wide area for heavy fire damage at long range. level 3 evocation sorcerer wizard fire dexterity save area damage 3 evocation sorcerer wizard action 150 feet instantaneous save dexterity 8d6 fire a bright streak flashes from you to a point you choose within range and then blossoms with a low roar into a fiery explosion. each creature in a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on that point makes a dexterity saving throw, taking 8d6 fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

flammable objects in the area that aren't being worn or carried start burning. using a higher-level spell slot. the damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 3.

payload.description:
A bright streak flashes from you to a point you choose within range and then blossoms with a low roar into a fiery explosion. Each creature in a 20-foot-radius Sphere centered on that point makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 8d6 Fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

Flammable objects in the area that aren't being worn or carried start burning.

entries.name: Flame Strike

entries.summary: A pillar of sacred fire crashes down, mixing searing flame with divine radiance.
entries.searchText: flame strike spell a pillar of sacred fire crashes down, mixing searing flame with divine radiance. level 5 evocation cleric damage fire radiant dexterity save area 5 evocation cleric action 60 feet instantaneous save dexterity a 10-foot-radius, 40-foot-high cylinder deals 5d6 fire and 5d6 radiant damage on a dexterity save 5d6 fire + 5d6 radiant a vertical column of brilliant fire roars down from above. each creature in a 10-foot-radius, 40-foot-high cylinder centered on a point within range makes a dexterity saving throw, taking 5d6 fire damage and 5d6 radiant damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. using a higher-level spell slot. the fire damage and the radiant damage increase by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 5.

payload.description: A vertical column of brilliant fire roars down from above. Each creature in a 10-foot-radius, 40-foot-high Cylinder centered on a point within range makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 5d6 Fire damage and 5d6 Radiant damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.
