entries.name: Flesh to Stone
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Lock a creature in place and grind it toward petrification through repeated failed saves.
entries.searchText:
flesh to stone spell lock a creature in place and grind it toward petrification through repeated failed saves. level 6 transmutation druid sorcerer wizard concentration control constitution save petrify 6 transmutation druid sorcerer wizard action 60 feet concentration, up to 1 minute true save constitution restrain a creature and force repeated constitution saves that can turn it to stone you attempt to turn one creature that you can see within range into stone. the target makes a constitution saving throw. on a failed save, it has the restrained condition for the duration. on a successful save, its speed is 0 until the start of your next turn. constructs automatically succeed on the save.

a restrained target makes another constitution saving throw at the end of each of its turns. if it successfully saves against this spell three times, the spell ends. if it fails its saves three times, it is turned to stone and has the petrified condition for the duration. the successes and failures needn’t be consecutive; keep track of both until the target collects three of a kind.

if you maintain your concentration on this spell for the entire possible duration, the target is petrified until the condition is ended by greater restoration or similar magic.

payload.effect: Restrain a creature and force repeated Constitution saves that can turn it to stone
payload.description:
You attempt to turn one creature that you can see within range into stone. The target makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, it has the Restrained condition for the duration. On a successful save, its Speed is 0 until the start of your next turn. Constructs automatically succeed on the save.

A Restrained target makes another Constitution saving throw at the end of each of its turns. If it successfully saves against this spell three times, the spell ends. If it fails its saves three times, it is turned to stone and has the Petrified condition for the duration. The successes and failures needn’t be consecutive; keep track of both until the target collects three of a kind.

If you maintain your Concentration on this spell for the entire possible duration, the target is Petrified until the condition is ended by Greater Restoration or similar magic.

payload.higherLevel: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 3.

entries.name: Fog Cloud
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A wide obscuring cloud that blocks vision and reshapes the battlefield.
entries.searchText: fog cloud spell a wide obscuring cloud that blocks vision and reshapes the battlefield. level 1 conjuration druid ranger sorcerer wizard control obscured 1 conjuration druid ranger sorcerer wizard action 120 feet concentration, up to 1 hour true you create a 20-foot-radius sphere of fog centered on a point within range. the sphere is heavily obscured. it lasts for the duration or until a strong wind (such as one created by gust of wind) disperses it. using a higher-level spell slot. the fog's radius increases by 20 feet for each spell slot level above 1.

payload.description: You create a 20-foot-radius Sphere of fog centered on a point within range. The Sphere is Heavily Obscured. It lasts for the duration or until a strong wind (such as one created by Gust of Wind) disperses it.
payload.higherLevel: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The fog's radius increases by 20 feet for each spell slot level above 1.

entries.name: Forbiddance
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Seal a large sanctified area against teleportation, planar travel, and unwanted creature types.
entries.searchText:
forbiddance spell seal a large sanctified area against teleportation, planar travel, and unwanted creature types. level 6 abjuration cleric ritual ward teleportation planar radiant necrotic 6 abjuration cleric 10 minutes or ritual touch 1 day true ward a huge area against teleportation and planar entry, and damage chosen creature types that cross it 5d10 radiant or necrotic you create a ward against magical travel that protects up to 40,000 square feet of floor space to a height of 30 feet above the floor. for the duration, creatures can’t teleport into the area or use portals, such as those created by the gate spell, to enter the area. the spell proofs the area against planar travel, and therefore prevents creatures from accessing the area by way of the astral plane, the ethereal plane, the feywild, the shadowfell, or the plane shift spell.

in addition, the spell damages types of creatures that you choose when you cast it. choose one or more of the following: aberrations, celestials, elementals, fey, fiends, and undead. when a creature of a chosen type enters the spell’s area for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there, the creature takes 5d10 radiant or necrotic damage (your choice when you cast this spell).

you can designate a password when you cast the spell. a creature that speaks the password as it enters the area takes no damage from the spell.

the spell’s area can’t overlap with the area of another forbiddance spell. if you cast forbiddance every day for 30 days in the same location, the spell lasts until it is dispelled, and the material components are consumed on the last casting.

payload.effect: Ward a huge area against teleportation and planar entry, and damage chosen creature types that cross it
payload.description:
You create a ward against magical travel that protects up to 40,000 square feet of floor space to a height of 30 feet above the floor. For the duration, creatures can’t teleport into the area or use portals, such as those created by the Gate spell, to enter the area. The spell proofs the area against planar travel, and therefore prevents creatures from accessing the area by way of the Astral Plane, the Ethereal Plane, the Feywild, the Shadowfell, or the Plane Shift spell.

In addition, the spell damages types of creatures that you choose when you cast it. Choose one or more of the following: Aberrations, Celestials, Elementals, Fey, Fiends, and Undead. When a creature of a chosen type enters the spell’s area for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there, the creature takes 5d10 Radiant or Necrotic damage (your choice when you cast this spell).

You can designate a password when you cast the spell. A creature that speaks the password as it enters the area takes no damage from the spell.

The spell’s area can’t overlap with the area of another Forbiddance spell. If you cast Forbiddance every day for 30 days in the same location, the spell lasts until it is dispelled, and the Material components are consumed on the last casting.

entries.name: Forcecage
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Seal a target area inside an invisible prison of force that resists escape and dispelling.
entries.searchText:
forcecage spell seal a target area inside an invisible prison of force that resists escape and dispelling. level 7 evocation bard warlock wizard control force prison ward 7 evocation bard warlock wizard action 100 feet concentration, up to 1 hour true create an invisible force cage or box that traps creatures and blocks most escape an immobile, invisible, cube-shaped prison composed of magical force springs into existence around an area you choose within range. the prison can be a cage or a solid box, as you choose.

a prison in the shape of a cage can be up to 20 feet on a side and is made from 1/2-inch diameter bars spaced 1/2 inch apart. a prison in the shape of a box can be up to 10 feet on a side, creating a solid barrier that prevents any matter from passing through it and blocking any spells cast into or out from the area.

when you cast the spell, any creature that is completely inside the cage’s area is trapped. creatures only partially within the area, or those too large to fit inside it, are pushed away from the center of the area until they are completely outside it.

a creature inside the cage can’t leave it by nonmagical means. if the creature tries to use teleportation or interplanar travel to leave, it must first make a charisma saving throw. on a successful save, the creature can use that magic to exit the cage. on a failed save, the creature doesn’t exit the cage and wastes the spell or effect. the cage also extends into the ethereal plane, blocking ethereal travel.

this spell can’t be dispelled by dispel magic.

payload.effect: Create an invisible force cage or box that traps creatures and blocks most escape
payload.description:
An immobile, Invisible, Cube-shaped prison composed of magical force springs into existence around an area you choose within range. The prison can be a cage or a solid box, as you choose.

A prison in the shape of a cage can be up to 20 feet on a side and is made from 1/2-inch diameter bars spaced 1/2 inch apart. A prison in the shape of a box can be up to 10 feet on a side, creating a solid barrier that prevents any matter from passing through it and blocking any spells cast into or out from the area.

When you cast the spell, any creature that is completely inside the cage’s area is trapped. Creatures only partially within the area, or those too large to fit inside it, are pushed away from the center of the area until they are completely outside it.

A creature inside the cage can’t leave it by nonmagical means. If the creature tries to use teleportation or interplanar travel to leave, it must first make a Charisma saving throw. On a successful save, the creature can use that magic to exit the cage. On a failed save, the creature doesn’t exit the cage and wastes the spell or effect. The cage also extends into the Ethereal Plane, blocking ethereal travel.

This spell can’t be dispelled by Dispel Magic.

entries.name: Foresight
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Grant a creature a glimpse of the immediate future so it acts with near-perfect awareness for hours.
entries.searchText: foresight spell grant a creature a glimpse of the immediate future so it acts with near-perfect awareness for hours. level 9 divination bard druid warlock wizard buff advantage defense future 9 divination bard druid warlock wizard 1 minute touch 8 hours grant a willing creature advantage on d20 tests while other creatures have disadvantage on attack rolls against it you touch a willing creature and bestow a limited ability to see into the immediate future. for the duration, the target has advantage on d20 tests, and other creatures have disadvantage on attack rolls against it. the spell ends early if you cast it again.

payload.effect: Grant a willing creature Advantage on D20 Tests while other creatures have Disadvantage on attack rolls against it

entries.name: Freedom of Movement
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A mobility ward that defeats restraints, difficult terrain, and magical movement suppression.
entries.searchText:
freedom of movement spell a mobility ward that defeats restraints, difficult terrain, and magical movement suppression. level 4 abjuration bard cleric druid ranger support mobility defense 4 abjuration bard cleric druid ranger action touch 1 hour the target ignores difficult terrain, resists magical movement reduction, gains a swim speed, and can slip free of nonmagical restraints you touch a willing creature. for the duration, the target’s movement is unaffected by difficult terrain, and spells and other magical effects can neither reduce the target’s speed nor cause the target to have the paralyzed or restrained conditions. the target also has a swim speed equal to its speed.

in addition, the target can spend 5 feet of movement to automatically escape from nonmagical restraints, such as manacles or a creature imposing the grappled condition on it.

level 3 transmutation (sorcerer, warlock, wizard)

casting time: action

range: touch

components: v, s, m (a bit of gauze)

duration: concentration, up to 1 hour

a willing creature you touch shape-shifts, along with everything it’s wearing and carrying, into a misty cloud for the duration. the spell ends on the target if it drops to 0 hit points or if it takes a magic action to end the spell on itself.

while in this form, the target’s only method of movement is a fly speed of 10 feet, and it can hover. the target can enter and occupy the space of another creature. the target has resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage; it has immunity to the prone condition; and it has advantage on strength, dexterity, and constitution saving throws. the target can pass through narrow openings, but it treats liquids as though they were solid surfaces.

the target can’t talk or manipulate objects, and any objects it was carrying or holding can’t be dropped, used, or otherwise interacted with. finally, the target can’t attack or cast spells. using a higher-level spell slot. you can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 3.

payload.effect: The target ignores Difficult Terrain, resists magical movement reduction, gains a Swim Speed, and can slip free of nonmagical restraints
payload.description:
You touch a willing creature. For the duration, the target’s movement is unaffected by Difficult Terrain, and spells and other magical effects can neither reduce the target’s Speed nor cause the target to have the Paralyzed or Restrained conditions. The target also has a Swim Speed equal to its Speed.

In addition, the target can spend 5 feet of movement to automatically escape from nonmagical restraints, such as manacles or a creature imposing the Grappled condition on it.

Level 3 Transmutation (Sorcerer, Warlock, Wizard)

Casting Time: Action

Range: Touch

Components: V, S, M (a bit of gauze)

Duration: Concentration, up to 1 hour

A willing creature you touch shape-shifts, along with everything it’s wearing and carrying, into a misty cloud for the duration. The spell ends on the target if it drops to 0 Hit Points or if it takes a Magic action to end the spell on itself.

While in this form, the target’s only method of movement is a Fly Speed of 10 feet, and it can hover. The target can enter and occupy the space of another creature. The target has Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage; it has Immunity to the Prone condition; and it has Advantage on Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution saving throws. The target can pass through narrow openings, but it treats liquids as though they were solid surfaces.

The target can’t talk or manipulate objects, and any objects it was carrying or holding can’t be dropped, used, or otherwise interacted with. Finally, the target can’t attack or cast spells.

entries.name: Gate
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Tear open a portal to another plane or drag a named extraplanar creature through to your side.
entries.searchText:
gate spell tear open a portal to another plane or drag a named extraplanar creature through to your side. level 9 conjuration cleric sorcerer warlock wizard portal planar travel summoning concentration 9 conjuration cleric sorcerer warlock wizard action 60 feet concentration, up to 1 minute true open a portal to a precise location on another plane or pull a named creature through from another plane you conjure a portal linking an unoccupied space you can see within range to a precise location on a different plane of existence. the portal is a circular opening, which you can make 5 to 20 feet in diameter. you can orient the portal in any direction you choose. the portal lasts for the duration, and the portal’s destination is visible through it.

the portal has a front and a back on each plane where it appears. travel through the portal is possible only by moving through its front. anything that does so is instantly transported to the other plane, appearing in the unoccupied space nearest to the portal.

deities and other planar rulers can prevent portals created by this spell from opening in their presence or anywhere within their domains.

when you cast this spell, you can speak the name of a specific creature (a pseudonym, title, or nickname doesn’t work). if that creature is on a plane other than the one you are on, the portal opens next to the named creature and transports it to the nearest unoccupied space on your side of the portal. you gain no special power over the creature, and it is free to act as the dm deems appropriate. it might leave, attack you, or help you.

payload.effect: Open a portal to a precise location on another plane or pull a named creature through from another plane
payload.description:
You conjure a portal linking an unoccupied space you can see within range to a precise location on a different plane of existence. The portal is a circular opening, which you can make 5 to 20 feet in diameter. You can orient the portal in any direction you choose. The portal lasts for the duration, and the portal’s destination is visible through it.

The portal has a front and a back on each plane where it appears. Travel through the portal is possible only by moving through its front. Anything that does so is instantly transported to the other plane, appearing in the unoccupied space nearest to the portal.

Deities and other planar rulers can prevent portals created by this spell from opening in their presence or anywhere within their domains.

When you cast this spell, you can speak the name of a specific creature (a pseudonym, title, or nickname doesn’t work). If that creature is on a plane other than the one you are on, the portal opens next to the named creature and transports it to the nearest unoccupied space on your side of the portal. You gain no special power over the creature, and it is free to act as the DM deems appropriate. It might leave, attack you, or help you.

entries.name: Geas
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Bind a creature to a long-term command and punish open defiance with psychic agony.
entries.searchText:
geas spell bind a creature to a long-term command and punish open defiance with psychic agony. level 5 enchantment bard cleric druid paladin wizard control wisdom save psychic 5 enchantment bard cleric druid paladin wizard 1 minute 60 feet 30 days save wisdom on a failed wisdom save, charm a creature with a long-term command that deals 5d10 psychic damage when defied 5d10 psychic you give a verbal command to a creature that you can see within range, ordering it to carry out some service or refrain from an action or a course of activity as you decide. the target must succeed on a wisdom saving throw or have the charmed condition for the duration. the target automatically succeeds if it can’t understand your command.

while charmed, the creature takes 5d10 psychic damage if it acts in a manner directly counter to your command. it takes this damage no more than once each day.

you can issue any command you choose, short of an activity that would result in certain death. should you issue a suicidal command, the spell ends.

a remove curse, greater restoration, or wish spell ends this spell. using a higher-level spell slot. if you use a level 7 or 8 spell slot, the duration is 365 days. if you use a level 9 spell slot, the spell lasts until it is ended by one of the spells mentioned above.

payload.effect: On a failed Wisdom save, charm a creature with a long-term command that deals 5d10 Psychic damage when defied
payload.description:
You give a verbal command to a creature that you can see within range, ordering it to carry out some service or refrain from an action or a course of activity as you decide. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the Charmed condition for the duration. The target automatically succeeds if it can’t understand your command.

While Charmed, the creature takes 5d10 Psychic damage if it acts in a manner directly counter to your command. It takes this damage no more than once each day.

You can issue any command you choose, short of an activity that would result in certain death. Should you issue a suicidal command, the spell ends.

A Remove Curse, Greater Restoration, or Wish spell ends this spell.
payload.higherLevel: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. If you use a level 7 or 8 spell slot, the duration is 365 days. If you use a level 9 spell slot, the spell lasts until it is ended by one of the spells mentioned above.

entries.name: Giant Insect
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A summoned giant insect ally fights beside you with a form-specific stat block.
entries.searchText:
giant insect spell a summoned giant insect ally fights beside you with a form-specific stat block. level 4 conjuration druid concentration summon ally beast control 4 conjuration druid action 60 feet concentration, up to 10 minutes true summon a giant centipede, spider, or wasp ally that obeys your commands and scales with slot level you summon a giant centipede, spider, or wasp (chosen when you cast the spell). it manifests in an unoccupied space you can see within range and uses the giant insect stat block. the form you choose determines certain details in its stat block. the creature disappears when it drops to 0 hit points or when the spell ends.

payload.effect: Summon a giant centipede, spider, or wasp ally that obeys your commands and scales with slot level
payload.description:
You summon a giant centipede, spider, or wasp (chosen when you cast the spell). It manifests in an unoccupied space you can see within range and uses the Giant Insect stat block. Multiattack. The insect makes a number of attacks equal to half this spell's level (round down).

Web Bolt (Spider Only). Ranged Attack Roll: Bonus equals your spell attack modifier, range 60 ft. Hit: 1d10 + 3 plus the spell's level Bludgeoning damage, and the target's Speed is reduced to 0 until the start of the insect's next turn.

entries.name: Guards and Wards
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Layer a stronghold with fog, locks, webs, illusions, and one extra magical trap effect.
entries.searchText:
guards and wards spell layer a stronghold with fog, locks, webs, illusions, and one extra magical trap effect. level 6 abjuration bard wizard utility ward trap illusion 6 abjuration bard wizard 1 hour touch 24 hours ward a large area with fogged corridors, locked doors, webbed stairs, and a chosen extra spell effect you create a ward that protects up to 2,500 square feet of floor space. the warded area can be up to 20 feet tall, and you shape it as one 50-foot square, one hundred 5-foot squares that are contiguous, or twenty-five 10-foot squares that are contiguous.

when you cast this spell, you can specify individuals that are unaffected by the spell’s effects. you can also specify a password that, when spoken aloud within 5 feet of the warded area, makes the speaker immune to its effects.

the spell creates the effects below within the warded area. dispel magic has no effect on guards and wards itself, but each of the following effects can be dispelled. if all four are dispelled, guards and wards ends. if you cast the spell every day for 365 days on the same area, the spell thereafter lasts until all its effects are dispelled.

corridors. fog fills all the warded corridors, making them heavily obscured. in addition, at each intersection or branching passage offering a choice of direction, there is a 50 percent chance that a creature other than you believes it is going in the opposite direction from the one it chooses.

doors. all doors in the warded area are magically locked, as if sealed by the arcane lock spell. in addition, you can cover up to ten doors with an illusion to make them appear as plain sections of wall.

stairs. webs fill all stairs in the warded area from top to bottom, as in the web spell. these strands regrow in 10 minutes if they are destroyed while guards and wards lasts.

other spell effect. place one of the following magical effects within the warded area:

dancing lights in four corridors, with a simple program that the lights repeat as long as guards and wards lasts

magic mouth in two locations

stinking cloud in two locations (the vapors return within 10 minutes if dispersed while guards and wards lasts)

gust of wind in one corridor or room (the wind blows continuously while the spell lasts)

suggestion in one 5-foot square; any creature that enters that square receives the suggestion mentally

payload.effect: Ward a large area with fogged corridors, locked doors, webbed stairs, and a chosen extra spell effect
payload.description:
You create a ward that protects up to 2,500 square feet of floor space. The warded area can be up to 20 feet tall, and you shape it as one 50-foot square, one hundred 5-foot squares that are contiguous, or twenty-five 10-foot squares that are contiguous.

When you cast this spell, you can specify individuals that are unaffected by the spell’s effects. You can also specify a password that, when spoken aloud within 5 feet of the warded area, makes the speaker immune to its effects.

The spell creates the effects below within the warded area. Dispel Magic has no effect on Guards and Wards itself, but each of the following effects can be dispelled. If all four are dispelled, Guards and Wards ends. If you cast the spell every day for 365 days on the same area, the spell thereafter lasts until all its effects are dispelled.

Corridors. Fog fills all the warded corridors, making them Heavily Obscured. In addition, at each intersection or branching passage offering a choice of direction, there is a 50 percent chance that a creature other than you believes it is going in the opposite direction from the one it chooses.

Doors. All doors in the warded area are magically locked, as if sealed by the Arcane Lock spell. In addition, you can cover up to ten doors with an illusion to make them appear as plain sections of wall.

Stairs. Webs fill all stairs in the warded area from top to bottom, as in the Web spell. These strands regrow in 10 minutes if they are destroyed while Guards and Wards lasts.

Other Spell Effect. Place one of the following magical effects within the warded area:

Dancing Lights in four corridors, with a simple program that the lights repeat as long as Guards and Wards lasts

Magic Mouth in two locations

Stinking Cloud in two locations (the vapors return within 10 minutes if dispersed while Guards and Wards lasts)

Gust of Wind in one corridor or room (the wind blows continuously while the spell lasts)

Suggestion in one 5-foot square; any creature that enters that square receives the suggestion mentally

entries.name: Hallow
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Consecrate a large area with a lasting ward and bind a powerful supernatural effect to it.
entries.searchText:
hallow spell consecrate a large area with a lasting ward and bind a powerful supernatural effect to it. level 5 abjuration cleric utility ward holy protection teleportation 5 abjuration cleric 24 hours touch until dispelled consecrate an area with a lasting ward against chosen creature types and one bound extra effect you touch a point and infuse an area around it with holy or unholy power. the area can have a radius up to 60 feet, and the spell fails if the radius includes an area already under the effect of hallow. the affected area has the following effects.

hallowed ward. choose any of these creature types: aberration, celestial, elemental, fey, fiend, or undead. creatures of the chosen types can’t willingly enter the area, and any creature that is possessed by or that has the charmed or frightened condition from such creatures isn’t possessed, charmed, or frightened by them while in the area.

extra effect. you bind an extra effect to the area from the list below:

courage. creatures of any types you choose can’t gain the frightened condition while in the area.

darkness. darkness fills the area. normal light, as well as magical light created by spells of a level lower than this spell, can’t illuminate the area.

daylight. bright light fills the area. magical darkness created by spells of a level lower than this spell can’t extinguish the light.

peaceful rest. dead bodies interred in the area can’t be turned into undead.

extradimensional interference. creatures of any types you choose can’t enter or exit the area using teleportation or interplanar travel.

fear. creatures of any types you choose have the frightened condition while in the area.

resistance. creatures of any types you choose have resistance to one damage type of your choice while in the area.

silence. no sound can emanate from within the area, and no sound can reach into it.

tongues. creatures of any types you choose can communicate with any other creature in the area even if they don’t share a common language.

vulnerability. creatures of any types you choose have vulnerability to one damage type of your choice while in the area.

payload.effect: Consecrate an area with a lasting ward against chosen creature types and one bound extra effect
payload.description:
You touch a point and infuse an area around it with holy or unholy power. The area can have a radius up to 60 feet, and the spell fails if the radius includes an area already under the effect of Hallow. The affected area has the following effects.

Hallowed Ward. Choose any of these creature types: Aberration, Celestial, Elemental, Fey, Fiend, or Undead. Creatures of the chosen types can’t willingly enter the area, and any creature that is possessed by or that has the Charmed or Frightened condition from such creatures isn’t possessed, Charmed, or Frightened by them while in the area.

Extra Effect. You bind an extra effect to the area from the list below:

Courage. Creatures of any types you choose can’t gain the Frightened condition while in the area.

Darkness. Darkness fills the area. Normal light, as well as magical light created by spells of a level lower than this spell, can’t illuminate the area.

Daylight. Bright light fills the area. Magical Darkness created by spells of a level lower than this spell can’t extinguish the light.

Peaceful Rest. Dead bodies interred in the area can’t be turned into Undead.

Extradimensional Interference. Creatures of any types you choose can’t enter or exit the area using teleportation or interplanar travel.

Fear. Creatures of any types you choose have the Frightened condition while in the area.

Resistance. Creatures of any types you choose have Resistance to one damage type of your choice while in the area.

Silence. No sound can emanate from within the area, and no sound can reach into it.

Tongues. Creatures of any types you choose can communicate with any other creature in the area even if they don’t share a common language.

Vulnerability. Creatures of any types you choose have Vulnerability to one damage type of your choice while in the area.

entries.name: Hallucinatory Terrain
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A wide-area illusion that disguises natural terrain as something far more misleading or dangerous.
entries.searchText:
hallucinatory terrain spell a wide-area illusion that disguises natural terrain as something far more misleading or dangerous. level 4 illusion bard druid warlock wizard control utility terrain exploration illusion 4 illusion bard druid warlock wizard 10 minutes 300 feet 24 hours make a 150-foot cube of natural terrain look, sound, and smell like different terrain for 24 hours you make natural terrain in a 150-foot cube in range look, sound, and smell like another sort of natural terrain. thus, open fields or a road can be made to resemble a swamp, hill, crevasse, or some other difficult or impassable terrain. a pond can be made to seem like a grassy meadow, a precipice like a gentle slope, or a rock-strewn gully like a wide and smooth road. manufactured structures, equipment, and creatures within the area aren’t changed.

the tactile characteristics of the terrain are unchanged, so creatures entering the area are likely to notice the illusion. if the difference isn’t obvious by touch, a creature examining the illusion can take the study action to make an intelligence (investigation) check against your spell save dc to disbelieve it. if a creature discerns that the terrain is illusory, the creature sees a vague image superimposed on the real terrain.

payload.effect: Make a 150-foot cube of natural terrain look, sound, and smell like different terrain for 24 hours
payload.description:
You make natural terrain in a 150-foot Cube in range look, sound, and smell like another sort of natural terrain. Thus, open fields or a road can be made to resemble a swamp, hill, crevasse, or some other difficult or impassable terrain. A pond can be made to seem like a grassy meadow, a precipice like a gentle slope, or a rock-strewn gully like a wide and smooth road. Manufactured structures, equipment, and creatures within the area aren’t changed.

The tactile characteristics of the terrain are unchanged, so creatures entering the area are likely to notice the illusion. If the difference isn’t obvious by touch, a creature examining the illusion can take the Study action to make an Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC to disbelieve it. If a creature discerns that the terrain is illusory, the creature sees a vague image superimposed on the real terrain.

entries.name: Hunter's Mark

hunter's mark spell a tracking-focused mark that boosts weapon pressure against one chosen target. level 1 divination ranger weapon damage concentration 1 divination ranger bonus action 90 feet concentration, up to 1 hour true you magically mark one creature you can see within range as your quarry. until the spell ends, you deal an extra 1d6 force damage to the target whenever you hit it with an attack roll. you also have advantage on any wisdom (perception or survival) check you make to find it.

hypnotic pattern spell a powerful control spell that can freeze a crowd in place if they fail a wisdom save. level 3 illusion bard sorcerer warlock wizard control wisdom save charmed incapacitated 3 illusion bard sorcerer warlock wizard action 120 feet concentration, up to 1 minute true save wisdom creatures in the area that fail the save are charmed, incapacitated, and reduced to speed 0 you create a twisting pattern of colors in a 30-foot cube within range. the pattern appears for a moment and vanishes. each creature in the area who can see the pattern must succeed on a wisdom saving throw or have the charmed condition for the duration. while charmed, the creature has the incapacitated condition and a speed of 0.

entries.name: Imprisonment
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Seal a creature into a chosen magical prison that can last until a specific trigger ends it.
entries.searchText:
imprisonment spell seal a creature into a chosen magical prison that can last until a specific trigger ends it. level 9 abjuration warlock wizard wisdom save prison restraint stasis ward 9 abjuration warlock wizard 1 minute 30 feet until dispelled save wisdom trap a creature in one of several magical prisons until a specified trigger or a level 9 dispel magic ends the spell you create a magical restraint to hold a creature that you can see within range. the target must make a wisdom saving throw. on a successful save, the target is unaffected, and it is immune to this spell for the next 24 hours. on a failed save, the target is imprisoned. while imprisoned, the target doesn’t need to breathe, eat, or drink, and it doesn’t age. divination spells can’t locate or perceive the imprisoned target, and the target can’t teleport.

until the spell ends, the target is also affected by one of the following effects of your choice:

burial. the target is entombed beneath the earth in a hollow globe of magical force that is just large enough to contain the target. nothing can pass into or out of the globe.

chaining. chains firmly rooted in the ground hold the target in place. the target has the restrained condition and can’t be moved by any means.

hedged prison. the target is trapped in a demiplane that is warded against teleportation and planar travel. the demiplane is your choice of a labyrinth, a cage, a tower, or the like.

minimus containment. the target becomes 1 inch tall and is trapped inside an indestructible gemstone or a similar object. light can pass through the gemstone (allowing the target to see out and other creatures to see in), but nothing else can pass through by any means.

slumber. the target has the unconscious condition and can’t be awoken.

ending the spell. when you cast the spell, specify a trigger that will end it. the trigger can be as simple or as elaborate as you choose, but the dm must agree that it has a high likelihood of happening within the next decade. the trigger must be an observable action, such as someone making a particular offering at the temple of your god, saving your true love, or defeating a specific monster.

a dispel magic spell can end the spell only if it is cast with a level 9 spell slot, targeting either the prison or the component used to create it.

payload.effect: Trap a creature in one of several magical prisons until a specified trigger or a level 9 Dispel Magic ends the spell
payload.description:
You create a magical restraint to hold a creature that you can see within range. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a successful save, the target is unaffected, and it is immune to this spell for the next 24 hours. On a failed save, the target is imprisoned. While imprisoned, the target doesn’t need to breathe, eat, or drink, and it doesn’t age. Divination spells can’t locate or perceive the imprisoned target, and the target can’t teleport.

Until the spell ends, the target is also affected by one of the following effects of your choice:

Burial. The target is entombed beneath the earth in a hollow globe of magical force that is just large enough to contain the target. Nothing can pass into or out of the globe.

Chaining. Chains firmly rooted in the ground hold the target in place. The target has the Restrained condition and can’t be moved by any means.

Hedged Prison. The target is trapped in a demiplane that is warded against teleportation and planar travel. The demiplane is your choice of a labyrinth, a cage, a tower, or the like.

Minimus Containment. The target becomes 1 inch tall and is trapped inside an indestructible gemstone or a similar object. Light can pass through the gemstone (allowing the target to see out and other creatures to see in), but nothing else can pass through by any means.

Slumber. The target has the Unconscious condition and can’t be awoken.

Ending the Spell. When you cast the spell, specify a trigger that will end it. The trigger can be as simple or as elaborate as you choose, but the DM must agree that it has a high likelihood of happening within the next decade. The trigger must be an observable action, such as someone making a particular offering at the temple of your god, saving your true love, or defeating a specific monster.

A Dispel Magic spell can end the spell only if it is cast with a level 9 spell slot, targeting either the prison or the component used to create it.

entries.name: Incendiary Cloud
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Unleash a rolling cloud of smoke and embers that obscures the field and scorches anyone caught inside.
entries.searchText:
incendiary cloud spell unleash a rolling cloud of smoke and embers that obscures the field and scorches anyone caught inside. level 8 conjuration druid sorcerer wizard concentration fire dexterity save sphere obscured 8 conjuration druid sorcerer wizard action 150 feet concentration, up to 1 minute true save dexterity create a mobile 20-foot-radius cloud of smoke and embers that heavily obscures and deals 10d8 fire damage 10d8 fire a swirling cloud of embers and smoke fills a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on a point within range. the cloud’s area is heavily obscured. it lasts for the duration or until a strong wind (like that created by gust of wind) disperses it.

when the cloud appears, each creature in it makes a dexterity saving throw, taking 10d8 fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. a creature must also make this save when the sphere moves into its space and when it enters the sphere or ends its turn there. a creature makes this save only once per turn.

the cloud moves 10 feet away from you in a direction you choose at the start of each of your turns.

payload.effect: Create a mobile 20-foot-radius cloud of smoke and embers that heavily obscures and deals 10d8 Fire damage
payload.description:
A swirling cloud of embers and smoke fills a 20-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point within range. The cloud’s area is Heavily Obscured. It lasts for the duration or until a strong wind (like that created by Gust of Wind) disperses it.

When the cloud appears, each creature in it makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 10d8 Fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature must also make this save when the Sphere moves into its space and when it enters the Sphere or ends its turn there. A creature makes this save only once per turn.

The cloud moves 10 feet away from you in a direction you choose at the start of each of your turns.

entries.name: Legend Lore
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Learn significant lore about a famous person, place, or object from the world itself.
entries.searchText:
legend lore spell learn significant lore about a famous person, place, or object from the world itself. level 5 divination bard cleric wizard utility knowledge lore 5 divination bard cleric wizard 10 minutes self instantaneous learn significant lore about a famous person, place, or object name or describe a famous person, place, or object. the spell brings to your mind a brief summary of the significant lore about that famous thing, as described by the dm.

the lore might consist of important details, amusing revelations, or even secret lore that has never been widely known. the more information you already know about the thing, the more precise and detailed the information you receive is. that information is accurate but might be couched in figurative language or poetry, as determined by the dm.

if the famous thing you chose isn’t actually famous, you hear sad musical notes played on a trombone, and the spell fails.

payload.effect: Learn significant lore about a famous person, place, or object
payload.description:
Name or describe a famous person, place, or object. The spell brings to your mind a brief summary of the significant lore about that famous thing, as described by the DM.

The lore might consist of important details, amusing revelations, or even secret lore that has never been widely known. The more information you already know about the thing, the more precise and detailed the information you receive is. That information is accurate but might be couched in figurative language or poetry, as determined by the DM.

If the famous thing you chose isn’t actually famous, you hear sad musical notes played on a trombone, and the spell fails.

entries.name: Leomund's Secret Chest
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A hidden extraplanar storage spell that hides a chest on the Ethereal Plane until recalled.
entries.searchText:
leomund's secret chest spell a hidden extraplanar storage spell that hides a chest on the ethereal plane until recalled. level 4 conjuration cleric wizard utility storage ethereal security 4 conjuration cleric wizard action touch until dispelled hide a chest and its contents on the ethereal plane and recall it later by touching its replica you hide a chest and all its contents on the ethereal plane. you must touch the chest and the miniature replica that serve as material components for the spell. the chest can contain up to 12 cubic feet of nonliving material (3 feet by 2 feet by 2 feet).

while the chest remains on the ethereal plane, you can take a magic action and touch the replica to recall the chest. it appears in an unoccupied space on the ground within 5 feet of you. you can send the chest back to the ethereal plane by taking a magic action to touch the chest and the replica.

after 60 days, there is a cumulative 5 percent chance at the end of each day that the spell ends. the spell also ends if you cast this spell again or if the tiny replica chest is destroyed. if the spell ends and the larger chest is on the ethereal plane, the chest remains there for you or someone else to find.

payload.effect: Hide a chest and its contents on the Ethereal Plane and recall it later by touching its replica
payload.description:
You hide a chest and all its contents on the Ethereal Plane. You must touch the chest and the miniature replica that serve as Material components for the spell. The chest can contain up to 12 cubic feet of nonliving material (3 feet by 2 feet by 2 feet).

While the chest remains on the Ethereal Plane, you can take a Magic action and touch the replica to recall the chest. It appears in an unoccupied space on the ground within 5 feet of you. You can send the chest back to the Ethereal Plane by taking a Magic action to touch the chest and the replica.

After 60 days, there is a cumulative 5 percent chance at the end of each day that the spell ends. The spell also ends if you cast this spell again or if the Tiny replica chest is destroyed. If the spell ends and the larger chest is on the Ethereal Plane, the chest remains there for you or someone else to find.

entries.name: Locate Creature
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A tracking divination that points you toward a known creature or the nearest creature of a chosen kind.
entries.searchText:
locate creature spell a tracking divination that points you toward a known creature or the nearest creature of a chosen kind. level 4 divination bard cleric druid paladin ranger wizard tracking utility exploration 4 divination bard cleric druid paladin ranger wizard action self concentration, up to 1 hour true sense the direction and movement of a familiar creature or the nearest creature of a known kind within 1,000 feet describe or name a creature that is familiar to you. you sense the direction to the creature’s location if that creature is within 1,000 feet of you. if the creature is moving, you know the direction of its movement.

the spell can locate a specific creature known to you or the nearest creature of a specific kind (such as a human or a unicorn) if you have seen such a creature up close—within 30 feet—at least once. if the creature you described or named is in a different form, such as under the effects of a flesh to stone or polymorph spell, this spell doesn’t locate the creature.

this spell can’t locate a creature if any thickness of lead blocks a direct path between you and the creature.

payload.effect: Sense the direction and movement of a familiar creature or the nearest creature of a known kind within 1,000 feet
payload.description:
Describe or name a creature that is familiar to you. You sense the direction to the creature’s location if that creature is within 1,000 feet of you. If the creature is moving, you know the direction of its movement.

The spell can locate a specific creature known to you or the nearest creature of a specific kind (such as a human or a unicorn) if you have seen such a creature up close—within 30 feet—at least once. If the creature you described or named is in a different form, such as under the effects of a Flesh to Stone or Polymorph spell, this spell doesn’t locate the creature.

This spell can’t locate a creature if any thickness of lead blocks a direct path between you and the creature.

entries.name: Mage Hand
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A utility cantrip that creates a spectral hand for simple remote manipulation.
entries.searchText:
mage hand spell a utility cantrip that creates a spectral hand for simple remote manipulation. cantrip utility bard warlock wizard arcane spectral hand 0 conjuration bard warlock wizard action 30 feet 1 minute a spectral, floating hand appears at a point you choose within range. the hand lasts for the duration. the hand vanishes if it is ever more than 30 feet away from you or if you cast this spell again.

when you cast the spell, you can use the hand to manipulate an object, open an unlocked door or container, stow or retrieve an item from an open container, or pour the contents out of a vial.

the hand can't attack, activate magic items, or carry more than 10 pounds.

When you cast the spell, you can use the hand to manipulate an object, open an unlocked door or container, stow or retrieve an item from an open container, or pour the contents out of a vial.

magic circle spell a warding spell that locks out selected supernatural creature types and weakens them around the circle. level 3 abjuration cleric paladin warlock wizard ward fiends undead utility 3 abjuration cleric paladin warlock wizard 1 minute 10 feet 1 hour a cylinder ward interferes with celestials, elementals, fey, fiends, or undead and makes them less dangerous near the circle you create a 10-foot-radius, 20-foot-tall cylinder of magical energy centered on a point on the ground that you can see within range. glowing runes appear wherever the cylinder intersects with the floor or other surface.

Choose one or more of the following types of creatures: Celestials, Elementals, Fey, Fiends, or Undead. The circle affects a creature of the chosen type in the following ways:

entries.name: Magic Jar
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Store your soul in a vessel, then attempt to possess another humanoid body.
entries.searchText:
magic jar spell store your soul in a vessel, then attempt to possess another humanoid body. level 6 necromancy wizard control charisma save possession soul 6 necromancy wizard 1 minute self until dispelled save charisma project your soul into a container and attempt to possess a humanoid body within 100 feet your body falls into a catatonic state as your soul leaves it and enters the container you used for the spell’s material component. while your soul inhabits the container, you are aware of your surroundings as if you were in the container’s space. you can’t move or take reactions. the only action you can take is to project your soul up to 100 feet out of the container, either returning to your living body (and ending the spell) or attempting to possess a humanoid’s body.

you can attempt to possess any humanoid within 100 feet of you that you can see (creatures warded by a protection from evil and good or magic circle spell can’t be possessed). the target makes a charisma saving throw. on a failed save, your soul enters the target’s body, and the target’s soul becomes trapped in the container. on a successful save, the target resists your efforts to possess it, and you can’t attempt to possess it again for 24 hours.

once you possess a creature’s body, you control it. your hit points, hit point dice, strength, dexterity, constitution, speed, and senses are replaced by the creature’s. you otherwise keep your game statistics.

meanwhile, the possessed creature’s soul can perceive from the container using its own senses, but it can’t move and it is incapacitated.

while possessing a body, you can take a magic action to return from the host body to the container if it is within 100 feet of you, returning the host creature’s soul to its body. if the host body dies while you’re in it, the creature dies, and you make a charisma saving throw against your own spellcasting dc. on a success, you return to the container if it is within 100 feet of you. otherwise, you die.

if the container is destroyed or the spell ends, your soul returns to your body. if your body is more than 100 feet away from you or if your body is dead, you die. if another creature’s soul is in the container when it is destroyed, the creature’s soul returns to its body if the body is alive and within 100 feet. otherwise, that creature dies.

when the spell ends, the container is destroyed.

payload.effect: Project your soul into a container and attempt to possess a humanoid body within 100 feet
payload.description:
Your body falls into a catatonic state as your soul leaves it and enters the container you used for the spell’s Material component. While your soul inhabits the container, you are aware of your surroundings as if you were in the container’s space. You can’t move or take Reactions. The only action you can take is to project your soul up to 100 feet out of the container, either returning to your living body (and ending the spell) or attempting to possess a Humanoid’s body.

You can attempt to possess any Humanoid within 100 feet of you that you can see (creatures warded by a Protection from Evil and Good or Magic Circle spell can’t be possessed). The target makes a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, your soul enters the target’s body, and the target’s soul becomes trapped in the container. On a successful save, the target resists your efforts to possess it, and you can’t attempt to possess it again for 24 hours.

Once you possess a creature’s body, you control it. Your Hit Points, Hit Point Dice, Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, Speed, and senses are replaced by the creature’s. You otherwise keep your game statistics.

Meanwhile, the possessed creature’s soul can perceive from the container using its own senses, but it can’t move and it is Incapacitated.

While possessing a body, you can take a Magic action to return from the host body to the container if it is within 100 feet of you, returning the host creature’s soul to its body. If the host body dies while you’re in it, the creature dies, and you make a Charisma saving throw against your own spellcasting DC. On a success, you return to the container if it is within 100 feet of you. Otherwise, you die.

If the container is destroyed or the spell ends, your soul returns to your body. If your body is more than 100 feet away from you or if your body is dead, you die. If another creature’s soul is in the container when it is destroyed, the creature’s soul returns to its body if the body is alive and within 100 feet. Otherwise, that creature dies.

When the spell ends, the container is destroyed.

entries.name: Meld into Stone

meld into stone spell a ritual-friendly hiding spell that lets the caster merge into stone for hours of concealment. level 3 transmutation cleric druid ranger ritual stealth utility 3 transmutation cleric druid ranger action or ritual touch 8 hours true you merge into a stone surface with your gear until you leave it or the stone is badly damaged you step into a stone object or surface large enough to fully contain your body, merging yourself and your equipment with the stone for the duration. you must touch the stone to do so. nothing of your presence remains visible or otherwise detectable by nonmagical senses.

while merged with the stone, you can't see what occurs outside it, and any wisdom (perception) checks you make to hear sounds outside it are made with disadvantage. you remain aware of the passage of time and can cast spells on yourself while merged in the stone. you can use 5 feet of movement to leave the stone where you entered it, which ends the spell. you otherwise can't move.

minor physical damage to the stone doesn't harm you, but its partial destruction or a change in its shape (to the extent that you no longer fit within it) expels you and deals 6d6 force damage to you. the stone's complete destruction (or transmutation into a different substance) expels you and deals 50 force damage to you. if expelled, you move into an unoccupied space closest to where you first entered and have the prone condition.

payload.effect: You merge into a stone surface with your gear until you leave it or the stone is badly damaged

You step into a stone object or surface large enough to fully contain your body, merging yourself and your equipment with the stone for the duration. You must touch the stone to do so. Nothing of your presence remains visible or otherwise detectable by nonmagical senses.

While merged with the stone, you can't see what occurs outside it, and any Wisdom (Perception) checks you make to hear sounds outside it are made with Disadvantage. You remain aware of the passage of time and can cast spells on yourself while merged in the stone. You can use 5 feet of movement to leave the stone where you entered it, which ends the spell. You otherwise can't move.

The stone's complete destruction (or transmutation into a different substance) expels you and deals 50 Force damage to you. If expelled, you move into an unoccupied space closest to where you first entered and have the Prone condition.

entries.name: Minor Illusion

minor illusion spell a flexible illusion cantrip that creates a short-lived sound or simple image for trickery and misdirection. cantrip illusion bard sorcerer warlock wizard utility misdirection 0 illusion bard sorcerer warlock wizard action 30 feet 1 minute create a sound or a small image that lasts for the duration unless inspected or recast you create a sound or an image of an object within range that lasts for the duration. see the descriptions below for the effects of each. the illusion ends if you cast this spell again.

if a creature takes a study action to examine the sound or image, the creature can determine that it is an illusion with a successful intelligence (investigation) check against your spell save dc. if a creature discerns the illusion for what it is, the illusion becomes faint to the creature.

sound. if you create a sound, its volume can range from a whisper to a scream. it can be your voice, someone else's voice, a lion's roar, a beating of drums, or any other sound you choose. the sound continues unabated throughout the duration, or you can make discrete sounds at different times before the spell ends.

payload.effect: Create a sound or a small image that lasts for the duration unless inspected or recast
payload.description:
You create a sound or an image of an object within range that lasts for the duration. See the descriptions below for the effects of each. The illusion ends if you cast this spell again.

If a creature takes a Study action to examine the sound or image, the creature can determine that it is an illusion with a successful Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC. If a creature discerns the illusion for what it is, the illusion becomes faint to the creature.

Sound. If you create a sound, its volume can range from a whisper to a scream. It can be your voice, someone else's voice, a lion's roar, a beating of drums, or any other sound you choose. The sound continues unabated throughout the duration, or you can make discrete sounds at different times before the spell ends.

Image. If you create an image of an object--such as a chair, muddy footprints, or a small chest--it must be no larger than a 5-foot Cube. The image can't create sound, light, smell, or any other sensory effect. Physical interaction with the image reveals it to be an illusion, since things can pass through it.

entries.name: Mirage Arcane
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Remake a vast landscape into an illusory terrain that looks, feels, and functions like something else.
entries.searchText:
mirage arcane spell remake a vast landscape into an illusory terrain that looks, feels, and functions like something else. level 7 illusion bard druid wizard utility terrain large area 7 illusion bard druid wizard 10 minutes sight 10 days transform up to 1 mile square of terrain into a convincing illusory landscape that can impede movement you make terrain in an area up to 1 mile square look, sound, smell, and even feel like some other sort of terrain. open fields or a road could be made to resemble a swamp, hill, crevasse, or some other rough or impassable terrain. a pond can be made to seem like a grassy meadow, a precipice like a gentle slope, or a rock-strewn gully like a wide and smooth road.

similarly, you can alter the appearance of structures or add them where none are present. the spell doesn’t disguise, conceal, or add creatures.

the illusion includes audible, visual, tactile, and olfactory elements, so it can turn clear ground into difficult terrain (or vice versa) or otherwise impede movement through the area. any piece of the illusory terrain (such as a rock or stick) that is removed from the spell’s area disappears immediately.

creatures with truesight can see through the illusion to the terrain’s true form; however, all other elements of the illusion remain, so while the creature is aware of the illusion’s presence, the creature can still physically interact with the illusion.

payload.effect: Transform up to 1 mile square of terrain into a convincing illusory landscape that can impede movement
payload.description:
You make terrain in an area up to 1 mile square look, sound, smell, and even feel like some other sort of terrain. Open fields or a road could be made to resemble a swamp, hill, crevasse, or some other rough or impassable terrain. A pond can be made to seem like a grassy meadow, a precipice like a gentle slope, or a rock-strewn gully like a wide and smooth road.

Similarly, you can alter the appearance of structures or add them where none are present. The spell doesn’t disguise, conceal, or add creatures.

The illusion includes audible, visual, tactile, and olfactory elements, so it can turn clear ground into Difficult Terrain (or vice versa) or otherwise impede movement through the area. Any piece of the illusory terrain (such as a rock or stick) that is removed from the spell’s area disappears immediately.

Creatures with Truesight can see through the illusion to the terrain’s true form; however, all other elements of the illusion remain, so while the creature is aware of the illusion’s presence, the creature can still physically interact with the illusion.

payload.effect: Become Invisible while an illusory double appears, moves, speaks, and can serve as your remote senses

entries.name: Modify Memory
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Charm a creature, incapacitate it, and rewrite or erase part of its recent memories.
entries.searchText:
modify memory spell charm a creature, incapacitate it, and rewrite or erase part of its recent memories. level 5 enchantment bard wizard concentration control wisdom save memory charm 5 enchantment bard wizard action 30 feet concentration, up to 1 minute true save wisdom on a failed wisdom save, charm and incapacitate a creature while you alter or erase a memory you attempt to reshape another creature’s memories. one creature that you can see within range makes a wisdom saving throw. if you are fighting the creature, it has advantage on the save. on a failed save, the target has the charmed condition for the duration. while charmed in this way, the target also has the incapacitated condition and is unaware of its surroundings, though it can hear you. if it takes any damage or is targeted by another spell, this spell ends, and no memories are modified.

while this charm lasts, you can affect the target’s memory of an event that it experienced within the last 24 hours and that lasted no more than 10 minutes. you can permanently eliminate all memory of the event, allow the target to recall the event with perfect clarity, change its memory of the event’s details, or create a memory of some other event.

you must speak to the target to describe how its memories are affected, and it must be able to understand your language for the modified memories to take root. its mind fills in any gaps in the details of your description. if the spell ends before you finish describing the modified memories, the creature’s memory isn’t altered. otherwise, the modified memories take hold when the spell ends.

a modified memory doesn’t necessarily affect how a creature behaves, particularly if the memory contradicts the creature’s natural inclinations, alignment, or beliefs. an illogical modified memory, such as a false memory of how much the creature enjoyed swimming in acid, is dismissed as a bad dream. the dm might deem a modified memory too nonsensical to affect a creature.

a remove curse or greater restoration spell cast on the target restores the creature’s true memory. using a higher-level spell slot. you can alter the target’s memories of an event that took place up to 7 days ago (level 6 spell slot), 30 days ago (level 7 spell slot), 365 days ago (level 8 spell slot), or any time in the creature’s past (level 9 spell slot).

While Charmed in this way, the target also has the Incapacitated condition and is unaware of its surroundings, though it can hear you. If it takes any damage or is targeted by another spell, this spell ends, and no memories are modified.

While this charm lasts, you can affect the target’s memory of an event that it experienced within the last 24 hours and that lasted no more than 10 minutes. You can permanently eliminate all memory of the event, allow the target to recall the event with perfect clarity, change its memory of the event’s details, or create a memory of some other event.

You must speak to the target to describe how its memories are affected, and it must be able to understand your language for the modified memories to take root. Its mind fills in any gaps in the details of your description. If the spell ends before you finish describing the modified memories, the creature’s memory isn’t altered. Otherwise, the modified memories take hold when the spell ends.

A modified memory doesn’t necessarily affect how a creature behaves, particularly if the memory contradicts the creature’s natural inclinations, alignment, or beliefs. An illogical modified memory, such as a false memory of how much the creature enjoyed swimming in acid, is dismissed as a bad dream. The DM might deem a modified memory too nonsensical to affect a creature.

entries.name: Mordenkainen?s Magnificent Mansion
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Conjure an extradimensional mansion with a banquet, many rooms, and a silent staff of servants.
entries.searchText:
mordenkainen?s magnificent mansion spell conjure an extradimensional mansion with a banquet, many rooms, and a silent staff of servants. level 7 conjuration bard wizard utility shelter extradimensional 7 conjuration bard wizard 1 minute 300 feet 24 hours open a shimmering door to an extradimensional mansion with furnished rooms, food, and 100 servants you conjure a shimmering door in range that lasts for the duration. the door leads to an extradimensional dwelling and is 5 feet wide and 10 feet tall. you and any creature you designate when you cast the spell can enter the extradimensional dwelling as long as the door remains open. you can open or close it (no action required) if you are within 30 feet of it. while closed, the door is imperceptible.

beyond the door is a magnificent foyer with numerous chambers beyond. the dwelling’s atmosphere is clean, fresh, and warm.

you can create any floor plan you like for the dwelling, but it can’t exceed 50 contiguous 10-foot cubes. the place is furnished and decorated as you choose. it contains sufficient food to serve a nine-course banquet for up to 100 people. furnishings and other objects created by this spell dissipate into smoke if removed from it.

a staff of 100 near-transparent servants attends all who enter. you determine the appearance of these servants and their attire. they are invulnerable and obey your commands. each servant can perform tasks that a human could perform, but they can’t attack or take any action that would directly harm another creature. thus the servants can fetch things, clean, mend, fold clothes, light fires, serve food, pour wine, and so on. the servants can’t leave the dwelling.

when the spell ends, any creatures or objects left inside the extradimensional space are expelled into the unoccupied spaces nearest to the entrance.

payload.effect: Open a shimmering door to an extradimensional mansion with furnished rooms, food, and 100 servants

You conjure a shimmering door in range that lasts for the duration. The door leads to an extradimensional dwelling and is 5 feet wide and 10 feet tall. You and any creature you designate when you cast the spell can enter the extradimensional dwelling as long as the door remains open. You can open or close it (no action required) if you are within 30 feet of it. While closed, the door is imperceptible.

Beyond the door is a magnificent foyer with numerous chambers beyond. The dwelling’s atmosphere is clean, fresh, and warm.

You can create any floor plan you like for the dwelling, but it can’t exceed 50 contiguous 10-foot Cubes. The place is furnished and decorated as you choose. It contains sufficient food to serve a nine-course banquet for up to 100 people. Furnishings and other objects created by this spell dissipate into smoke if removed from it.

A staff of 100 near-transparent servants attends all who enter. You determine the appearance of these servants and their attire. They are invulnerable and obey your commands. Each servant can perform tasks that a human could perform, but they can’t attack or take any action that would directly harm another creature. Thus the servants can fetch things, clean, mend, fold clothes, light fires, serve food, pour wine, and so on. The servants can’t leave the dwelling.

When the spell ends, any creatures or objects left inside the extradimensional space are expelled into the unoccupied spaces nearest to the entrance.

entries.name: Mordenkainen's Faithful Hound

mordenkainen's faithful hound spell a hidden phantom watchdog guards an area, barks at intruders, and bites enemies with force. level 4 conjuration wizard defense guardian force dexterity save alarm 4 conjuration wizard action 30 feet 8 hours save dexterity a hidden hound watches an area for hours, alarms on intruders, and bites nearby enemies for force damage 4d8 force you conjure a phantom watchdog in an unoccupied space that you can see within range. the hound remains for the duration or until the two of you are more than 300 feet apart from each other.

no one but you can see the hound, and it is intangible and invulnerable. when a small or larger creature comes within 30 feet of it without first speaking the password that you specify when you cast this spell, the hound starts barking loudly. the hound has truesight with a range of 30 feet.

payload.effect: A hidden hound watches an area for hours, alarms on intruders, and bites nearby enemies for force damage
payload.description:
You conjure a phantom watchdog in an unoccupied space that you can see within range. The hound remains for the duration or until the two of you are more than 300 feet apart from each other.

No one but you can see the hound, and it is intangible and invulnerable. When a Small or larger creature comes within 30 feet of it without first speaking the password that you specify when you cast this spell, the hound starts barking loudly. The hound has Truesight with a range of 30 feet.

At the start of each of your turns, the hound attempts to bite one enemy within 5 feet of it. That enemy must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take 4d8 Force damage.

entries.name: Mordenkainen's Private Sanctum
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A warded secure zone that blocks sight, sound, divination, teleportation, and planar travel.
entries.searchText:
mordenkainen's private sanctum spell a warded secure zone that blocks sight, sound, divination, teleportation, and planar travel. level 4 abjuration wizard utility warding security divination defense teleportation 4 abjuration wizard 10 minutes 120 feet (square) 24 hours ward a cube against sound, vision, divination, teleportation, and planar travel, with permanent upkeep potential you make an area within range magically secure. the area is a cube that can be as small as 5 feet to as large as 100 feet on each side. the spell lasts for the duration.

when you cast the spell, you decide what sort of security the spell provides, choosing any of the following properties:

- sound can't pass through the barrier at the edge of the warded area.
- the barrier of the warded area appears dark and foggy, preventing vision (including darkvision) through it.
- sensors created by divination spells can't appear inside the protected area or pass through the barrier at its perimeter.
- creatures in the area can't be targeted by divination spells.
- nothing can teleport into or out of the warded area.
- planar travel is blocked within the warded area.

casting this spell on the same spot every day for 365 days makes the spell last until dispelled. using a higher-level spell slot. you can increase the size of the cube by 100 feet for each spell slot level above 4.

payload.effect: Ward a Cube against sound, vision, divination, teleportation, and planar travel, with permanent upkeep potential
payload.description:
You make an area within range magically secure. The area is a Cube that can be as small as 5 feet to as large as 100 feet on each side. The spell lasts for the duration.

When you cast the spell, you decide what sort of security the spell provides, choosing any of the following properties:

- Sound can't pass through the barrier at the edge of the warded area.
- The barrier of the warded area appears dark and foggy, preventing vision (including Darkvision) through it.
- Sensors created by Divination spells can't appear inside the protected area or pass through the barrier at its perimeter.
- Creatures in the area can't be targeted by Divination spells.
- Nothing can teleport into or out of the warded area.
- Planar travel is blocked within the warded area.

Casting this spell on the same spot every day for 365 days makes the spell last until dispelled.

entries.name: Move Earth
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Slowly reshape a wide stretch of terrain into trenches, berms, walls, or pillars.
entries.searchText:
move earth spell slowly reshape a wide stretch of terrain into trenches, berms, walls, or pillars. level 6 transmutation druid sorcerer wizard concentration utility terrain earth 6 transmutation druid sorcerer wizard action 120 feet concentration, up to 2 hours true reshape a large area of dirt, sand, or clay over time into trenches, walls, pillars, or raised ground choose an area of terrain no larger than 40 feet on a side within range. you can reshape dirt, sand, or clay in the area in any manner you choose for the duration. you can raise or lower the area’s elevation, create or fill in a trench, erect or flatten a wall, or form a pillar. the extent of any such changes can’t exceed half the area’s largest dimension. for example, if you affect a 40-foot square, you can create a pillar up to 20 feet high, raise or lower the square’s elevation by up to 20 feet, dig a trench up to 20 feet deep, and so on. it takes 10 minutes for these changes to complete. because the terrain’s transformation occurs slowly, creatures in the area can’t usually be trapped or injured by the ground’s movement.

at the end of every 10 minutes you spend concentrating on the spell, you can choose a new area of terrain to affect within range.

this spell can’t manipulate natural stone or stone construction. rocks and structures shift to accommodate the new terrain. if the way you shape the terrain would make a structure unstable, it might collapse.

similarly, this spell doesn’t directly affect plant growth. the moved earth carries any plants along with it.

payload.effect: Reshape a large area of dirt, sand, or clay over time into trenches, walls, pillars, or raised ground
payload.description:
Choose an area of terrain no larger than 40 feet on a side within range. You can reshape dirt, sand, or clay in the area in any manner you choose for the duration. You can raise or lower the area’s elevation, create or fill in a trench, erect or flatten a wall, or form a pillar. The extent of any such changes can’t exceed half the area’s largest dimension. For example, if you affect a 40-foot square, you can create a pillar up to 20 feet high, raise or lower the square’s elevation by up to 20 feet, dig a trench up to 20 feet deep, and so on. It takes 10 minutes for these changes to complete. Because the terrain’s transformation occurs slowly, creatures in the area can’t usually be trapped or injured by the ground’s movement.

At the end of every 10 minutes you spend concentrating on the spell, you can choose a new area of terrain to affect within range.

This spell can’t manipulate natural stone or stone construction. Rocks and structures shift to accommodate the new terrain. If the way you shape the terrain would make a structure unstable, it might collapse.

Similarly, this spell doesn’t directly affect plant growth. The moved earth carries any plants along with it.
