entries.name: Time Stop
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Freeze the world around you long enough to take several turns before anyone else can react.
entries.searchText:
time stop spell freeze the world around you long enough to take several turns before anyone else can react. level 9 transmutation sorcerer wizard time self-buff turns action economy 9 transmutation sorcerer wizard action self instantaneous take 1d4 + 1 turns in a row while time is stopped for everyone else unless you affect another creature you briefly stop the flow of time for everyone but yourself. no time passes for other creatures, while you take 1d4 + 1 turns in a row, during which you can use actions and move as normal.

this spell ends if one of the actions you use during this period, or any effects that you create during it, affects a creature other than you or an object being worn or carried by someone other than you. in addition, the spell ends if you move to a place more than 1,000 feet from the location where you cast it.

payload.effect: Take 1d4 + 1 turns in a row while time is stopped for everyone else unless you affect another creature
payload.description:
You briefly stop the flow of time for everyone but yourself. No time passes for other creatures, while you take 1d4 + 1 turns in a row, during which you can use actions and move as normal.

This spell ends if one of the actions you use during this period, or any effects that you create during it, affects a creature other than you or an object being worn or carried by someone other than you. In addition, the spell ends if you move to a place more than 1,000 feet from the location where you cast it.

entries.name: Transport via Plants
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Open a living passage between two large plants anywhere on the same plane.
entries.searchText: transport via plants spell open a living passage between two large plants anywhere on the same plane. level 6 conjuration druid utility travel teleportation plant 6 conjuration druid action 10 feet 1 minute create a magical link between two large inanimate plants so creatures can step through one and emerge from the other this spell creates a magical link between a large or larger inanimate plant within range and another plant, at any distance, on the same plane of existence. you must have seen or touched the destination plant at least once before. for the duration, any creature can step into the target plant and exit from the destination plant by using 5 feet of movement.

payload.effect: Create a magical link between two large inanimate plants so creatures can step through one and emerge from the other
payload.description: This spell creates a magical link between a Large or larger inanimate plant within range and another plant, at any distance, on the same plane of existence. You must have seen or touched the destination plant at least once before. For the duration, any creature can step into the target plant and exit from the destination plant by using 5 feet of movement.

entries.name: Tree Stride
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Step through living trees to cross a wooded battlefield or forest in an instant.
entries.searchText:
tree stride spell step through living trees to cross a wooded battlefield or forest in an instant. level 5 conjuration druid ranger concentration travel movement nature 5 conjuration druid ranger action self concentration, up to 1 minute true move from one living tree to another of the same kind within 500 feet once on each of your turns you gain the ability to enter a tree and move from inside it to inside another tree of the same kind within 500 feet. both trees must be living and at least the same size as you. you must use 5 feet of movement to enter a tree. you instantly know the location of all other trees of the same kind within 500 feet and, as part of the move used to enter the tree, can either pass into one of those trees or step out of the tree you’re in. you appear in a spot of your choice within 5 feet of the destination tree, using another 5 feet of movement. if you have no movement left, you appear within 5 feet of the tree you entered.

you can use this transportation ability only once on each of your turns. you must end each turn outside a tree.

payload.effect: Move from one living tree to another of the same kind within 500 feet once on each of your turns
payload.description:
You gain the ability to enter a tree and move from inside it to inside another tree of the same kind within 500 feet. Both trees must be living and at least the same size as you. You must use 5 feet of movement to enter a tree. You instantly know the location of all other trees of the same kind within 500 feet and, as part of the move used to enter the tree, can either pass into one of those trees or step out of the tree you’re in. You appear in a spot of your choice within 5 feet of the destination tree, using another 5 feet of movement. If you have no movement left, you appear within 5 feet of the tree you entered.

You can use this transportation ability only once on each of your turns. You must end each turn outside a tree.

entries.name: True Polymorph
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Transform creatures or objects into entirely new forms, potentially making the change permanent.
entries.searchText:
true polymorph spell transform creatures or objects into entirely new forms, potentially making the change permanent. level 9 transmutation bard warlock wizard wisdom save transformation concentration shapeshift 9 transmutation bard warlock wizard action 30 feet concentration, up to 1 hour true save wisdom transform a creature or object into another form, potentially permanently if you maintain concentration for the full duration choose one creature or nonmagical object that you can see within range. the creature shape-shifts into a different creature or a nonmagical object, or the object shape-shifts into a creature (the object must be neither worn nor carried). the transformation lasts for the duration or until the target dies or is destroyed, but if you maintain concentration on this spell for the full duration, the spell lasts until dispelled.

an unwilling creature can make a wisdom saving throw, and if it succeeds, it isn’t affected by this spell.

creature into creature. if you turn a creature into another kind of creature, the new form can be any kind you choose that has a challenge rating equal to or less than the target’s challenge rating or level. the target’s game statistics are replaced by the stat block of the new form, but it retains its hit points, hit point dice, alignment, and personality.

the target gains a number of temporary hit points equal to the hit points of the new form. the spell ends early on the target if it has no temporary hit points left.

the target is limited in the actions it can perform by the anatomy of its new form, and it can’t speak or cast spells.

the target’s gear melds into the new form. the creature can’t use or otherwise benefit from any of that equipment.

object into creature. you can turn an object into any kind of creature, as long as the creature’s size is no larger than the object’s size and the creature has a challenge rating of 9 or lower. the creature is friendly to you and your allies. in combat, it takes its turns immediately after yours, and it obeys your commands.

if the spell lasts more than an hour, you no longer control the creature. it might remain friendly to you, depending on how you have treated it.

creature into object. if you turn a creature into an object, it transforms along with whatever it is wearing and carrying into that form, as long as the object’s size is no larger than the creature’s size. the creature’s statistics become those of the object, and the creature has no memory of time spent in this form after the spell ends and it returns to normal.

payload.effect: Transform a creature or object into another form, potentially permanently if you maintain Concentration for the full duration
payload.description:
Choose one creature or nonmagical object that you can see within range. The creature shape-shifts into a different creature or a nonmagical object, or the object shape-shifts into a creature (the object must be neither worn nor carried). The transformation lasts for the duration or until the target dies or is destroyed, but if you maintain Concentration on this spell for the full duration, the spell lasts until dispelled.

An unwilling creature can make a Wisdom saving throw, and if it succeeds, it isn’t affected by this spell.

Creature into Creature. If you turn a creature into another kind of creature, the new form can be any kind you choose that has a Challenge Rating equal to or less than the target’s Challenge Rating or level. The target’s game statistics are replaced by the stat block of the new form, but it retains its Hit Points, Hit Point Dice, alignment, and personality.

The target gains a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to the Hit Points of the new form. The spell ends early on the target if it has no Temporary Hit Points left.

The target is limited in the actions it can perform by the anatomy of its new form, and it can’t speak or cast spells.

The target’s gear melds into the new form. The creature can’t use or otherwise benefit from any of that equipment.

Object into Creature. You can turn an object into any kind of creature, as long as the creature’s size is no larger than the object’s size and the creature has a Challenge Rating of 9 or lower. The creature is Friendly to you and your allies. In combat, it takes its turns immediately after yours, and it obeys your commands.

If the spell lasts more than an hour, you no longer control the creature. It might remain Friendly to you, depending on how you have treated it.

Creature into Object. If you turn a creature into an object, it transforms along with whatever it is wearing and carrying into that form, as long as the object’s size is no larger than the creature’s size. The creature’s statistics become those of the object, and the creature has no memory of time spent in this form after the spell ends and it returns to normal.

entries.name: True Resurrection
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Restore a long-dead creature completely, even providing a new body if the original is gone.
entries.searchText:
true resurrection spell restore a long-dead creature completely, even providing a new body if the original is gone. level 9 necromancy cleric druid resurrection healing support restoration 9 necromancy cleric druid 1 hour touch instantaneous return a creature dead up to 200 years to life with all hit points restored and a new body if needed you touch a creature that has been dead for no longer than 200 years and that died for any reason except old age. the creature is revived with all its hit points.

this spell closes all wounds, neutralizes any poison, cures all magical contagions, and lifts any curses affecting the creature when it died. the spell replaces damaged or missing organs and limbs. if the creature was undead, it is restored to its non-undead form.

the spell can provide a new body if the original no longer exists, in which case you must speak the creature’s name. the creature then appears in an unoccupied space you choose within 10 feet of you.

payload.effect: Return a creature dead up to 200 years to life with all Hit Points restored and a new body if needed
payload.description:
You touch a creature that has been dead for no longer than 200 years and that died for any reason except old age. The creature is revived with all its Hit Points.

This spell closes all wounds, neutralizes any poison, cures all magical contagions, and lifts any curses affecting the creature when it died. The spell replaces damaged or missing organs and limbs. If the creature was Undead, it is restored to its non-Undead form.

The spell can provide a new body if the original no longer exists, in which case you must speak the creature’s name. The creature then appears in an unoccupied space you choose within 10 feet of you.

entries.name: True Seeing
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Grant a creature supernatural sight that pierces illusions and hidden truths.
entries.searchText: true seeing spell grant a creature supernatural sight that pierces illusions and hidden truths. level 6 divination bard cleric sorcerer warlock wizard utility vision 6 divination bard cleric sorcerer warlock wizard action touch 1 hour give a willing creature truesight out to 120 feet for 1 hour for the duration, the willing creature you touch has truesight with a range of 120 feet.

payload.effect: Give a willing creature Truesight out to 120 feet for 1 hour
payload.description: For the duration, the willing creature you touch has Truesight with a range of 120 feet.

entries.name: Tsunami
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Raise a colossal moving wall of water that batters creatures and sweeps them along as it collapses.
entries.searchText:
tsunami spell raise a colossal moving wall of water that batters creatures and sweeps them along as it collapses. level 8 conjuration druid strength save bludgeoning wave large area battlefield 8 conjuration druid 1 minute 1 mile 6 rounds save strength create a massive advancing wall of water that deals bludgeoning damage and drags creatures through its path 6d10 bludgeoning a wall of water springs into existence at a point you choose within range. you can make the wall up to 300 feet long, 300 feet high, and 50 feet thick. the wall lasts for the duration.

when the wall appears, each creature in its area makes a strength saving throw, taking 6d10 bludgeoning damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

at the start of each of your turns after the wall appears, the wall, along with any creatures in it, moves 50 feet away from you. any huge or smaller creature inside the wall or whose space the wall enters when it moves must succeed on a strength saving throw or take 5d10 bludgeoning damage. a creature can take this damage only once per round. at the end of the turn, the wall’s height is reduced by 50 feet, and the damage the wall deals on later rounds is reduced by 1d10. when the wall reaches 0 feet in height, the spell ends.

a creature caught in the wall can move by swimming. because of the wave’s force, though, the creature must succeed on a strength (athletics) check against your spell save dc to move at all. if it fails the check, it can’t move. a creature that moves out of the wall falls to the ground.

payload.effect: Create a massive advancing wall of water that deals Bludgeoning damage and drags creatures through its path
payload.description:
A wall of water springs into existence at a point you choose within range. You can make the wall up to 300 feet long, 300 feet high, and 50 feet thick. The wall lasts for the duration.

When the wall appears, each creature in its area makes a Strength saving throw, taking 6d10 Bludgeoning damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

At the start of each of your turns after the wall appears, the wall, along with any creatures in it, moves 50 feet away from you. Any Huge or smaller creature inside the wall or whose space the wall enters when it moves must succeed on a Strength saving throw or take 5d10 Bludgeoning damage. A creature can take this damage only once per round. At the end of the turn, the wall’s height is reduced by 50 feet, and the damage the wall deals on later rounds is reduced by 1d10. When the wall reaches 0 feet in height, the spell ends.

A creature caught in the wall can move by swimming. Because of the wave’s force, though, the creature must succeed on a Strength (Athletics) check against your spell save DC to move at all. If it fails the check, it can’t move. A creature that moves out of the wall falls to the ground.

entries.name: Vicious Mockery
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A cutting insult that deals psychic damage and hinders the target's next attack.
entries.searchText:
vicious mockery spell a cutting insult that deals psychic damage and hinders the target's next attack. cantrip enchantment bard psychic save 0 enchantment bard action 60 feet instantaneous save wisdom 1d6 psychic you unleash a string of insults laced with subtle enchantments at one creature you can see or hear within range. the target must succeed on a wisdom saving throw or take 1d6 psychic damage and have disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn.

cantrip upgrade. the damage increases by 1d6 when you reach levels 5 (2d6), 11 (3d6), and 17 (4d6).

payload.description:
You unleash a string of insults laced with subtle enchantments at one creature you can see or hear within range. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or take 1d6 Psychic damage and have Disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn.

Cantrip Upgrade. The damage increases by 1d6 when you reach levels 5 (2d6), 11 (3d6), and 17 (4d6).

entries.name: Vitriolic Sphere
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A globe of acid erupts across a wide area, dealing an immediate blast and a lingering burn.
entries.searchText: vitriolic sphere spell a globe of acid erupts across a wide area, dealing an immediate blast and a lingering burn. level 4 evocation sorcerer wizard damage dexterity save acid area 4 evocation sorcerer wizard action 150 feet (20 ft. sphere) instantaneous save dexterity a 20-foot-radius sphere deals 10d4 acid damage on a failed dexterity save plus 5d4 more at the end of the target's next turn 10d4 acid + 5d4 acid you point at a location within range, and a glowing, 1-foot-diameter ball of acid streaks there and explodes in a 20-foot-radius sphere. each creature in that area makes a dexterity saving throw. on a failed save, a creature takes 10d4 acid damage and another 5d4 acid damage at the end of its next turn. on a successful save, a creature takes half the initial damage only. using a higher-level spell slot. the initial damage increases by 2d4 for each spell slot level above 4.

payload.effect: A 20-foot-radius Sphere deals 10d4 Acid damage on a failed Dexterity save plus 5d4 more at the end of the target's next turn
payload.description: You point at a location within range, and a glowing, 1-foot-diameter ball of acid streaks there and explodes in a 20-foot-radius Sphere. Each creature in that area makes a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 10d4 Acid damage and another 5d4 Acid damage at the end of its next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half the initial damage only.
payload.higherLevel: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The initial damage increases by 2d4 for each spell slot level above 4.

entries.name: Wall of Fire
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A blazing barrier that scorches creatures where it appears and threatens anything lingering nearby.
entries.searchText:
wall of fire spell a blazing barrier that scorches creatures where it appears and threatens anything lingering nearby. level 4 evocation druid sorcerer wizard concentration control damage wall fire dexterity save 4 evocation druid sorcerer wizard action 120 feet concentration, up to 1 minute true save dexterity create a long wall or ring of fire that deals 5d8 fire damage when it appears and punishes creatures near one side 5d8 fire you create a wall of fire on a solid surface within range. you can make the wall up to 60 feet long, 20 feet high, and 1 foot thick, or a ringed wall up to 20 feet in diameter, 20 feet high, and 1 foot thick. the wall is opaque and lasts for the duration.

when the wall appears, each creature in its area makes a dexterity saving throw, taking 5d8 fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

one side of the wall, selected by you when you cast this spell, deals 5d8 fire damage to each creature that ends its turn within 10 feet of that side or inside the wall. a creature takes the same damage when it enters the wall for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there. the other side of the wall deals no damage. using a higher-level spell slot. the damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 4.

payload.effect: Create a long wall or ring of fire that deals 5d8 Fire damage when it appears and punishes creatures near one side
payload.description:
You create a wall of fire on a solid surface within range. You can make the wall up to 60 feet long, 20 feet high, and 1 foot thick, or a ringed wall up to 20 feet in diameter, 20 feet high, and 1 foot thick. The wall is opaque and lasts for the duration.

When the wall appears, each creature in its area makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 5d8 Fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

One side of the wall, selected by you when you cast this spell, deals 5d8 Fire damage to each creature that ends its turn within 10 feet of that side or inside the wall. A creature takes the same damage when it enters the wall for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there. The other side of the wall deals no damage.
payload.higherLevel: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 4.

entries.name: Wall of Force
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: An invisible force barrier blocks movement, damage, and even ethereal travel.
entries.searchText:
wall of force spell an invisible force barrier blocks movement, damage, and even ethereal travel. level 5 evocation wizard concentration control defense wall force 5 evocation wizard action 120 feet concentration, up to 10 minutes true create an invisible wall, dome, or globe of force that nothing can physically pass through an invisible wall of force springs into existence at a point you choose within range. the wall appears in any orientation you choose, as a horizontal or vertical barrier or at an angle. it can be free floating or resting on a solid surface. you can form it into a hemispherical dome or a globe with a radius of up to 10 feet, or you can shape a flat surface made up of ten 10-foot-by-10-foot panels. each panel must be contiguous with another panel. in any form, the wall is 1/4 inch thick and lasts for the duration. if the wall cuts through a creature's space when it appears, the creature is pushed to one side of the wall (you choose which side).

nothing can physically pass through the wall. it is immune to all damage and can't be dispelled by dispel magic. a disintegrate spell destroys the wall instantly, however. the wall also extends into the ethereal plane and blocks ethereal travel through the wall.

payload.effect: Create an invisible wall, dome, or globe of force that nothing can physically pass through
payload.description:
An Invisible wall of force springs into existence at a point you choose within range. The wall appears in any orientation you choose, as a horizontal or vertical barrier or at an angle. It can be free floating or resting on a solid surface. You can form it into a hemispherical dome or a globe with a radius of up to 10 feet, or you can shape a flat surface made up of ten 10-foot-by-10-foot panels. Each panel must be contiguous with another panel. In any form, the wall is 1/4 inch thick and lasts for the duration. If the wall cuts through a creature's space when it appears, the creature is pushed to one side of the wall (you choose which side).

Nothing can physically pass through the wall. It is immune to all damage and can't be dispelled by Dispel Magic. A Disintegrate spell destroys the wall instantly, however. The wall also extends into the Ethereal Plane and blocks ethereal travel through the wall.

entries.name: Wall of Ice
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Shape a massive icy barrier that freezes on impact and punishes anything that breaks through it.
entries.searchText:
wall of ice spell shape a massive icy barrier that freezes on impact and punishes anything that breaks through it. level 6 evocation wizard concentration control wall cold dexterity save 6 evocation wizard action 120 feet concentration, up to 10 minutes true save dexterity create a dome, globe, or paneled wall of ice that deals cold damage when formed and leaves a frigid damaging breach when broken 10d6 cold you create a wall of ice on a solid surface within range. you can form it into a hemispherical dome or a globe with a radius of up to 10 feet, or you can shape a flat surface made up of ten 10-foot-square panels. each panel must be contiguous with another panel. in any form, the wall is 1 foot thick and lasts for the duration.

if the wall cuts through a creature’s space when it appears, the creature is pushed to one side of the wall (you choose which side) and makes a dexterity saving throw, taking 10d6 cold damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

the wall is an object that can be damaged and thus breached. it has ac 12 and 30 hit points per 10-foot section, and it has immunity to cold, poison, and psychic damage and vulnerability to fire damage. reducing a 10-foot section of wall to 0 hit points destroys it and leaves behind a sheet of frigid air in the space the wall occupied.

a creature moving through the sheet of frigid air for the first time on a turn makes a constitution saving throw, taking 5d6 cold damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. using a higher-level spell slot. the damage the wall deals when it appears increases by 2d6 and the damage from passing through the sheet of frigid air increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 6.

payload.effect: Create a dome, globe, or paneled wall of ice that deals Cold damage when formed and leaves a frigid damaging breach when broken
payload.description:
You create a wall of ice on a solid surface within range. You can form it into a hemispherical dome or a globe with a radius of up to 10 feet, or you can shape a flat surface made up of ten 10-foot-square panels. Each panel must be contiguous with another panel. In any form, the wall is 1 foot thick and lasts for the duration.

If the wall cuts through a creature’s space when it appears, the creature is pushed to one side of the wall (you choose which side) and makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 10d6 Cold damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

The wall is an object that can be damaged and thus breached. It has AC 12 and 30 Hit Points per 10-foot section, and it has Immunity to Cold, Poison, and Psychic damage and Vulnerability to Fire damage. Reducing a 10-foot section of wall to 0 Hit Points destroys it and leaves behind a sheet of frigid air in the space the wall occupied.

A creature moving through the sheet of frigid air for the first time on a turn makes a Constitution saving throw, taking 5d6 Cold damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.
payload.higherLevel: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage the wall deals when it appears increases by 2d6 and the damage from passing through the sheet of frigid air increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 6.

entries.name: Wall of Stone
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A solid stone barrier reshapes the battlefield and can become permanent if maintained.
entries.searchText:
wall of stone spell a solid stone barrier reshapes the battlefield and can become permanent if maintained. level 5 evocation druid sorcerer wizard concentration control wall stone terrain 5 evocation druid sorcerer wizard action 120 feet concentration, up to 10 minutes true create ten panels of solid stone that can enclose areas, bridge gaps, and become permanent if maintained a nonmagical wall of solid stone springs into existence at a point you choose within range. the wall is 6 inches thick and is composed of ten 10-foot-by-10-foot panels. each panel must be contiguous with another panel. alternatively, you can create 10-foot-by-20-foot panels that are only 3 inches thick.

if the wall cuts through a creature's space when it appears, the creature is pushed to one side of the wall (you choose which side). if a creature would be surrounded on all sides by the wall (or the wall and another solid surface), that creature can make a dexterity saving throw. on a success, it can use its reaction to move up to its speed so that it is no longer enclosed by the wall.

the wall can have any shape you desire, though it can't occupy the same space as a creature or object. the wall doesn't need to be vertical or rest on a firm foundation. it must, however, merge with and be solidly supported by existing stone. thus, you can use this spell to bridge a chasm or create a ramp.

if you create a span greater than 20 feet in length, you must halve the size of each panel to create supports. you can crudely shape the wall to create battlements and the like.

the wall is an object made of stone that can be damaged and thus breached. each panel has ac 15 and 30 hit points per inch of thickness, and it has immunity to poison and psychic damage. reducing a panel to 0 hit points destroys it and might cause connected panels to collapse at the dm's discretion.

if you maintain your concentration on this spell for its full duration, the wall becomes permanent and can't be dispelled. otherwise, the wall disappears when the spell ends.

payload.effect: Create ten panels of solid stone that can enclose areas, bridge gaps, and become permanent if maintained
payload.description:
A nonmagical wall of solid stone springs into existence at a point you choose within range. The wall is 6 inches thick and is composed of ten 10-foot-by-10-foot panels. Each panel must be contiguous with another panel. Alternatively, you can create 10-foot-by-20-foot panels that are only 3 inches thick.

If the wall cuts through a creature's space when it appears, the creature is pushed to one side of the wall (you choose which side). If a creature would be surrounded on all sides by the wall (or the wall and another solid surface), that creature can make a Dexterity saving throw. On a success, it can use its Reaction to move up to its Speed so that it is no longer enclosed by the wall.

The wall can have any shape you desire, though it can't occupy the same space as a creature or object. The wall doesn't need to be vertical or rest on a firm foundation. It must, however, merge with and be solidly supported by existing stone. Thus, you can use this spell to bridge a chasm or create a ramp.

If you create a span greater than 20 feet in length, you must halve the size of each panel to create supports. You can crudely shape the wall to create battlements and the like.

The wall is an object made of stone that can be damaged and thus breached. Each panel has AC 15 and 30 Hit Points per inch of thickness, and it has Immunity to Poison and Psychic damage. Reducing a panel to 0 Hit Points destroys it and might cause connected panels to collapse at the DM's discretion.

If you maintain your Concentration on this spell for its full duration, the wall becomes permanent and can't be dispelled. Otherwise, the wall disappears when the spell ends.

entries.name: Wall of Thorns
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Raise a dense bramble barrier that tears at creatures when it appears and when they force through it.
entries.searchText:
wall of thorns spell raise a dense bramble barrier that tears at creatures when it appears and when they force through it. level 6 conjuration druid concentration control wall piercing slashing dexterity save 6 conjuration druid action 120 feet concentration, up to 10 minutes true save dexterity create a thorn wall that blocks line of sight, slows movement, and deals 7d8 piercing or slashing damage 7d8 piercing or slashing you create a wall of tangled brush bristling with needle-sharp thorns. the wall appears within range on a solid surface and lasts for the duration. you choose to make the wall up to 60 feet long, 10 feet high, and 5 feet thick or a circle that has a 20-foot diameter and is up to 20 feet high and 5 feet thick. the wall blocks line of sight.

when the wall appears, each creature in its area makes a dexterity saving throw, taking 7d8 piercing damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

a creature can move through the wall, albeit slowly and painfully. for every 1 foot a creature moves through the wall, it must spend 4 feet of movement. furthermore, the first time a creature enters a space in the wall on a turn or ends its turn there, the creature makes a dexterity saving throw, taking 7d8 slashing damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. a creature makes this save only once per turn. using a higher-level spell slot. both types of damage increase by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 6.

payload.effect: Create a thorn wall that blocks line of sight, slows movement, and deals 7d8 Piercing or Slashing damage
payload.description:
You create a wall of tangled brush bristling with needle-sharp thorns. The wall appears within range on a solid surface and lasts for the duration. You choose to make the wall up to 60 feet long, 10 feet high, and 5 feet thick or a circle that has a 20-foot diameter and is up to 20 feet high and 5 feet thick. The wall blocks line of sight.

When the wall appears, each creature in its area makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 7d8 Piercing damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

A creature can move through the wall, albeit slowly and painfully. For every 1 foot a creature moves through the wall, it must spend 4 feet of movement. Furthermore, the first time a creature enters a space in the wall on a turn or ends its turn there, the creature makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 7d8 Slashing damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature makes this save only once per turn.
payload.higherLevel: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. Both types of damage increase by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 6.

entries.name: Water Walk
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A ritual-friendly travel spell that lets a group cross dangerous liquid surfaces as if they were solid ground.
entries.searchText:
water walk spell a ritual-friendly travel spell that lets a group cross dangerous liquid surfaces as if they were solid ground. level 3 transmutation cleric druid ranger sorcerer ritual travel utility 3 transmutation cleric druid ranger sorcerer action or ritual 30 feet 1 hour true up to ten willing creatures can move across liquids such as water, mud, snow, or lava as if on solid ground this spell grants the ability to move across any liquid surface--such as water, acid, mud, snow, quicksand, or lava--as if it were harmless solid ground (creatures crossing molten lava can still take damage from the heat). up to ten willing creatures of your choice within range gain this ability for the duration.

an affected target must take a bonus action to pass from the liquid's surface into the liquid itself and vice versa, but if the target falls into the liquid, the target passes through the surface into the liquid below.

payload.effect: Up to ten willing creatures can move across liquids such as water, mud, snow, or lava as if on solid ground
payload.description:
This spell grants the ability to move across any liquid surface--such as water, acid, mud, snow, quicksand, or lava--as if it were harmless solid ground (creatures crossing molten lava can still take damage from the heat). Up to ten willing creatures of your choice within range gain this ability for the duration.

An affected target must take a Bonus Action to pass from the liquid's surface into the liquid itself and vice versa, but if the target falls into the liquid, the target passes through the surface into the liquid below.

entries.name: Weird
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Overwhelm multiple creatures with psychic nightmares that terrify them and grind them down.
entries.searchText:
weird spell overwhelm multiple creatures with psychic nightmares that terrify them and grind them down. level 9 illusion warlock wizard wisdom save psychic frightened large area concentration 9 illusion warlock wizard action 120 feet concentration, up to 1 minute true save wisdom frighten creatures in a large sphere with psychic terrors that keep dealing psychic damage on failed saves 10d10 psychic you try to create illusory terrors in others’ minds. each creature of your choice in a 30-foot-radius sphere centered on a point within range makes a wisdom saving throw. on a failed save, a target takes 10d10 psychic damage and has the frightened condition for the duration. on a successful save, a target takes half as much damage only.

a frightened target makes a wisdom saving throw at the end of each of its turns. on a failed save, it takes 5d10 psychic damage. on a successful save, the spell ends on that target.

payload.effect: Frighten creatures in a large sphere with psychic terrors that keep dealing Psychic damage on failed saves
payload.description:
You try to create illusory terrors in others’ minds. Each creature of your choice in a 30-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point within range makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 10d10 Psychic damage and has the Frightened condition for the duration. On a successful save, a target takes half as much damage only.

A Frightened target makes a Wisdom saving throw at the end of each of its turns. On a failed save, it takes 5d10 Psychic damage. On a successful save, the spell ends on that target.

entries.name: Wind Walk
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Turn a whole group into swift cloud forms for long-distance travel and safe descent.
entries.searchText:
wind walk spell turn a whole group into swift cloud forms for long-distance travel and safe descent. level 6 transmutation druid utility travel flight group 6 transmutation druid 1 minute 30 feet 8 hours transform yourself and up to ten willing creatures into fast-moving cloud forms with flight and damage resistance you and up to ten willing creatures of your choice within range assume gaseous forms for the duration, appearing as wisps of cloud. while in this cloud form, a target has a fly speed of 300 feet and can hover; it has immunity to the prone condition; and it has resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage. the only actions a target can take in this form are the dash action or a magic action to begin reverting to its normal form. reverting takes 1 minute, during which the target has the stunned condition. until the spell ends, the target can revert to cloud form, which also requires a magic action followed by a 1-minute transformation.

if a target is in cloud form and flying when the effect ends, the target descends 60 feet per round for 1 minute until it lands, which it does safely. if it can’t land after 1 minute, it falls the remaining distance.

payload.effect: Transform yourself and up to ten willing creatures into fast-moving cloud forms with flight and damage resistance
payload.description:
You and up to ten willing creatures of your choice within range assume gaseous forms for the duration, appearing as wisps of cloud. While in this cloud form, a target has a Fly Speed of 300 feet and can hover; it has Immunity to the Prone condition; and it has Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage. The only actions a target can take in this form are the Dash action or a Magic action to begin reverting to its normal form. Reverting takes 1 minute, during which the target has the Stunned condition. Until the spell ends, the target can revert to cloud form, which also requires a Magic action followed by a 1-minute transformation.

If a target is in cloud form and flying when the effect ends, the target descends 60 feet per round for 1 minute until it lands, which it does safely. If it can’t land after 1 minute, it falls the remaining distance.

entries.name: Wind Wall
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A battlefield-control wall that blocks projectiles, disperses gases, and batters creatures on contact.
entries.searchText:
wind wall spell a battlefield-control wall that blocks projectiles, disperses gases, and batters creatures on contact. level 3 evocation druid ranger control strength save bludgeoning wall 3 evocation druid ranger action 120 feet concentration, up to 1 minute true save strength 4d8 bludgeoning a wall of strong wind rises from the ground at a point you choose within range. you can make the wall up to 50 feet long, 15 feet high, and 1 foot thick. you can shape the wall in any way you choose so long as it makes one continuous path along the ground. the wall lasts for the duration.

when the wall appears, each creature in its area makes a strength saving throw, taking 4d8 bludgeoning damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

the strong wind keeps fog, smoke, and other gases at bay. small or smaller flying creatures or objects can't pass through the wall. loose, lightweight materials brought into the wall fly upward. arrows, bolts, and other ordinary projectiles launched at targets behind the wall are deflected upward and miss automatically. boulders hurled by giants or siege engines, and similar projectiles, are unaffected. creatures in gaseous form can't pass through it.

payload.description:
A wall of strong wind rises from the ground at a point you choose within range. You can make the wall up to 50 feet long, 15 feet high, and 1 foot thick. You can shape the wall in any way you choose so long as it makes one continuous path along the ground. The wall lasts for the duration.

When the wall appears, each creature in its area makes a Strength saving throw, taking 4d8 Bludgeoning damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

The strong wind keeps fog, smoke, and other gases at bay. Small or smaller flying creatures or objects can't pass through the wall. Loose, lightweight materials brought into the wall fly upward. Arrows, bolts, and other ordinary projectiles launched at targets behind the wall are deflected upward and miss automatically. Boulders hurled by Giants or siege engines, and similar projectiles, are unaffected. Creatures in gaseous form can't pass through it.

entries.name: Wish
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Bend reality to duplicate lower-level magic or attempt nearly any effect at enormous personal risk.
entries.searchText:
wish spell bend reality to duplicate lower-level magic or attempt nearly any effect at enormous personal risk. level 9 conjuration sorcerer wizard reality utility duplication apex magic 9 conjuration sorcerer wizard action self instantaneous duplicate lower-level spells or attempt to reshape reality itself at severe personal cost wish is the mightiest spell a mortal can cast. by simply speaking aloud, you can alter reality itself.

the basic use of this spell is to duplicate any other spell of level 8 or lower. if you use it this way, you don’t need to meet any requirements to cast that spell, including costly components. the spell simply takes effect.

alternatively, you can create one of the following effects of your choice:

object creation. you create one object of up to 25,000 gp in value that isn’t a magic item. the object can be no more than 300 feet in any dimension, and it appears in an unoccupied space that you can see on the ground.

instant health. you allow yourself and up to twenty creatures that you can see to regain all hit points, and you end all effects on them listed in the greater restoration spell.

resistance. you grant up to ten creatures that you can see resistance to one damage type that you choose. this resistance is permanent.

spell immunity. you grant up to ten creatures you can see immunity to a single spell or other magical effect for 8 hours.

sudden learning. you replace one of your feats with another feat for which you are eligible. you lose all the benefits of the old feat and gain the benefits of the new one. you can’t replace a feat that is a prerequisite for any of your other feats or features.

roll redo. you undo a single recent event by forcing a reroll of any die roll made within the last round (including your last turn). reality reshapes itself to accommodate the new result. for example, a wish spell could undo an ally’s failed saving throw or a foe’s critical hit. you can force the reroll to be made with advantage or disadvantage, and you choose whether to use the reroll or the original roll.

reshape reality. you may wish for something not included in any of the other effects. to do so, state your wish to the dm as precisely as possible. the dm has great latitude in ruling what occurs in such an instance; the greater the wish, the greater the likelihood that something goes wrong. this spell might simply fail, the effect you desire might be achieved only in part, or you might suffer an unforeseen consequence as a result of how you worded the wish. for example, wishing that a villain were dead might propel you forward in time to a period when that villain is no longer alive, effectively removing you from the game. similarly, wishing for a legendary magic item or an artifact might instantly transport you to the presence of the item’s current owner. if your wish is granted and its effects have consequences for a whole community, region, or world, you are likely to attract powerful foes. if your wish would affect a god, the god’s divine servants might instantly intervene to prevent it or to encourage you to craft the wish in a particular way. if your wish would undo the multiverse itself, threaten the city of sigil, or affect the lady of pain in any way, you see an image of her in your mind for a moment; she shakes her head, and your wish fails.

the stress of casting wish to produce any effect other than duplicating another spell weakens you. after enduring that stress, each time you cast a spell until you finish a long rest, you take 1d10 necrotic damage per level of that spell. this damage can’t be reduced or prevented in any way. in addition, your strength score becomes 3 for 2d4 days. for each of those days that you spend resting and doing nothing more than light activity, your remaining recovery time decreases by 2 days. finally, there is a 33 percent chance that you are unable to cast wish ever again if you suffer this stress.

payload.effect: Duplicate lower-level spells or attempt to reshape reality itself at severe personal cost
payload.description:
Wish is the mightiest spell a mortal can cast. By simply speaking aloud, you can alter reality itself.

The basic use of this spell is to duplicate any other spell of level 8 or lower. If you use it this way, you don’t need to meet any requirements to cast that spell, including costly components. The spell simply takes effect.

Alternatively, you can create one of the following effects of your choice:

Object Creation. You create one object of up to 25,000 GP in value that isn’t a magic item. The object can be no more than 300 feet in any dimension, and it appears in an unoccupied space that you can see on the ground.

Instant Health. You allow yourself and up to twenty creatures that you can see to regain all Hit Points, and you end all effects on them listed in the Greater Restoration spell.

Resistance. You grant up to ten creatures that you can see Resistance to one damage type that you choose. This Resistance is permanent.

Spell Immunity. You grant up to ten creatures you can see immunity to a single spell or other magical effect for 8 hours.

Sudden Learning. You replace one of your feats with another feat for which you are eligible. You lose all the benefits of the old feat and gain the benefits of the new one. You can’t replace a feat that is a prerequisite for any of your other feats or features.

Roll Redo. You undo a single recent event by forcing a reroll of any die roll made within the last round (including your last turn). Reality reshapes itself to accommodate the new result. For example, a Wish spell could undo an ally’s failed saving throw or a foe’s Critical Hit. You can force the reroll to be made with Advantage or Disadvantage, and you choose whether to use the reroll or the original roll.

Reshape Reality. You may wish for something not included in any of the other effects. To do so, state your wish to the DM as precisely as possible. The DM has great latitude in ruling what occurs in such an instance; the greater the wish, the greater the likelihood that something goes wrong. This spell might simply fail, the effect you desire might be achieved only in part, or you might suffer an unforeseen consequence as a result of how you worded the wish. For example, wishing that a villain were dead might propel you forward in time to a period when that villain is no longer alive, effectively removing you from the game. Similarly, wishing for a Legendary magic item or an Artifact might instantly transport you to the presence of the item’s current owner. If your wish is granted and its effects have consequences for a whole community, region, or world, you are likely to attract powerful foes. If your wish would affect a god, the god’s divine servants might instantly intervene to prevent it or to encourage you to craft the wish in a particular way. If your wish would undo the multiverse itself, threaten the City of Sigil, or affect the Lady of Pain in any way, you see an image of her in your mind for a moment; she shakes her head, and your wish fails.

The stress of casting Wish to produce any effect other than duplicating another spell weakens you. After enduring that stress, each time you cast a spell until you finish a Long Rest, you take 1d10 Necrotic damage per level of that spell. This damage can’t be reduced or prevented in any way. In addition, your Strength score becomes 3 for 2d4 days. For each of those days that you spend resting and doing nothing more than light activity, your remaining recovery time decreases by 2 days. Finally, there is a 33 percent chance that you are unable to cast Wish ever again if you suffer this stress.

entries.name: Wolf
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: A swift hunting beast with pack tactics and a punishing bite.
entries.searchText:
wolf creature a swift hunting beast with pack tactics and a punishing bite. beast cr 1/4 pack tactics keen hearing keen smell druid form medium beast 1/4 13 11 (2d8 + 2) 40 ft. 12 15 12 3 12 6 perception +3 stealth +4 passive perception 13 keen hearing and smell pack tactics bite: a melee strike that deals piercing damage and can drag a smaller target to the ground. forest grassland tundra true traits

pack tactics. the wolf has advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of the wolf's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the incapacitated condition.

actions

bite. melee attack roll: +4, reach 5 ft. hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) piercing damage. if the target is a medium or smaller creature, it has the prone condition.

actions: Bite: a melee strike that deals piercing damage and can drag a smaller target to the ground.
payload.description:
Traits

Pack Tactics. The wolf has Advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of the wolf's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.

Actions

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Piercing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the Prone condition.

entries.name: Word of Recall
entries.attribution: Wizards of the Coast open rules content
entries.summary: Teleport yourself and nearby allies back to a prepared sanctuary in an instant.
entries.searchText:
word of recall spell teleport yourself and nearby allies back to a prepared sanctuary in an instant. level 6 conjuration cleric utility travel teleportation sanctuary 6 conjuration cleric action 5 feet instantaneous teleport yourself and up to five willing creatures to a previously prepared sanctuary you and up to five willing creatures within 5 feet of you instantly teleport to a previously designated sanctuary. you and any creatures that teleport with you appear in the nearest unoccupied space to the spot you designated when you prepared your sanctuary (see below). if you cast this spell without first preparing a sanctuary, the spell has no effect.

you must designate a location, such as a temple, as a sanctuary by casting this spell there.

payload.effect: Teleport yourself and up to five willing creatures to a previously prepared sanctuary
payload.description:
You and up to five willing creatures within 5 feet of you instantly teleport to a previously designated sanctuary. You and any creatures that teleport with you appear in the nearest unoccupied space to the spot you designated when you prepared your sanctuary (see below). If you cast this spell without first preparing a sanctuary, the spell has no effect.

You must designate a location, such as a temple, as a sanctuary by casting this spell there.
